### 3. 解决 issue #0080

`src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts` 需加 `createOperationColumnKey` 函数:

```ts
export const createOperationColumnKey = (displayColumns: DisplayColumnRow[]): string => {
  // 简单实现:基于 Date.now + 已有 columns key 集合避免冲突
  const existingKeys = new Set(displayColumns.map((c) => c.key));
  let key = `column_actions_${Date.now()}`;
  let suffix = 0;
  while (existingKeys.has(key)) {
    suffix += 1;
    key = `column_actions_${Date.now()}_${suffix}`;
  }
  return key;
};
```

### 4. 解封 6 `-shared` 治理违规(15 文件)

按 projectmem "保持 app-local" 决策,**正确解法是删 app 端 import**,不是加 barrel 把 utility 升级为 public:

- cmdb-shared: 删 `app/cmdb/...` 的 3 处 import,改用 `app/cmdb/utils/` 复制实现(或提取到 `app/cmdb/utils/`)
- mlops-shared: 1 处
- monitor-shared: 3 处
- opspilot-cards: 5 处
- opspilot-selector-shared: 2 处
- user-preferences: 1 处

- 创建 `src/components/layout/index.ts` barrel (re-export sub-layout + side-menu)

## Scope

This worktree is the active governance branch for `web/`. `Storybook` is the source of truth for shared components. App-local components stay app-local unless they show real cross-app reuse.

## Current Allowlist

The current target is to drive `src/components` toward zero accidental `src/app` ownership leaks. At the moment, the remaining direct `src/components -> src/app` imports are intentionally allowlisted and should not be treated as pending extractions without a new decision:

- `src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts` -> `@/app/ops-analysis/utils/dateTime`
- `src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/table.tsx` -> `@/app/ops-analysis/utils/dateTime`

Why they stay:

- `formatOpsDisplayTime` and `formatOpsRequestTime` are now shared across widget runtime, app hooks, request transforms, and settings flows.
- That makes `dateTime.ts` a broader ops-analysis domain utility, not a widget-family-owned helper.
- Any future extraction should move it to a truly shared domain utility layer, not back into one widget family.

  - that means the remaining duplication is no longer framework-only, it is a stable business workspace shape:

    - right-side action cluster
    - paginated selectable instance table
    - page-local config/edit modals

  - avoid creating separate monitor/log wrappers for what is now a proven cross-app business pattern
- Migration:
  - migrated log integration receive to `IntegrationInstanceManagementShell`
  - migrated monitor integration asset to the same shell
  - preserved page-local:

    - action content
    - row selection
    - modal and drawer wiring
    - pagination and refresh flows
- Storybook:

- Reason:
  - these pages all repeated the same monitor-domain workspace contract:
    - `ResizableSidebar`
    - monitor object `TreeSelectorPanel`
    - full-height right-side task surface
  - the sidebar tree is not generic navigation, it is a stable monitor object selector pattern that carries domain semantics across multiple monitor workflows
- Cross-app comparison:
  - this pattern is broader than one page but still monitor-specific
  - event strategy uses the shell for table management
  - event template uses the same shell for grouped card selection
  - monitor view uses the same shell for list/topology switching
  - because the repeated contract depends on monitor object navigation semantics, promoting it to a framework-wide generic layout would be premature and would create a low-value wrapper
- Decision:
  - extract a monitor business component:
    - [src/components/monitor-object-workspace-shell/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-object-workspace-shell/index.tsx)

  - extend the shell only with a real, currently-proven `sidebarHeader` slot for pages whose object tree includes domain actions
- Migration:
  - migrated monitor event strategy to the new shell while preserving search, table pagination, and asset selection flow
  - migrated monitor event template to the same shell while preserving bulk selection, grouped cards, and sticky bottom action bar
  - migrated monitor view to the same shell while preserving segmented list/topology switching
  - expanded the same shell into monitor integration group management while preserving all-menu filtering, search state, and rule list pagination
  - expanded the same shell into monitor integration asset management while preserving time refresh, row selection, dropdown actions, and template/config drawers
  - expanded the same shell into monitor object-type management using the new sidebar header slot while preserving type add/edit/delete controls, drag sorting, and object table drag behavior
  - expanded the same shell into monitor event alert while preserving tabs, filter controls, chart panel, and alert table composition
- Storybook:

    - [src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx)
  - Storybook now covers:
    - default object-tree workspace composition
    - toolbar-driven content composition on the same shell
    - management-table content composition used by integration list surfaces
    - sidebar-header composition used by monitor object-type management

- Migration:
  - no code migration in this slice
  - governance action here is to record the hold clearly so future extractions stay evidence-based

  - no new story required

  - that left two sibling business components with overlapping shell behavior inside one family
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `CredentialsPanel` is not a framework component and should stay business-specific
  - but its shell semantics are now a strict specialization of `SectionPanel`:
    - same guide panel framing
    - same alarm integration header semantics
    - only a compact heading variant and different default spacing
- Decision:
  - keep `AlarmIntegrationGuideCredentialsPanel` as a named business component because it still carries distinct intent in alarm integration flows
  - reimplement it on top of `AlarmIntegrationGuideSectionPanel` so the family has one underlying section-shell contract
- Migration:
  - replaced direct `PanelShell + SectionHeader` assembly inside `credentials-panel.tsx`
  - now composes `AlarmIntegrationGuideSectionPanel` with `headerVariant="compact"` and the same business defaults
- Storybook:
  - no new story needed
  - the existing business stories remain the source of truth:
    - [src/stories/alarm-integration-guide-credentials-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-integration-guide-credentials-panel.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/alarm-integration-guide-section-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-integration-guide-section-panel.stories.tsx)

### Alarm integration guide section panel extraction

- Reason:

    - governed section header
    - standard bordered guide-shell body
  - the repetition was no longer just framework reuse, it had become a stable business-family pattern specific to alarm integration guidance
  - leaving the shell duplicated in each guide would keep business components stylistically aligned only by convention rather than by a shared contract
- Cross-app comparison:
  - Zabbix uses this shell for connection values, parameter mapping, field mapping, script template, deployment steps, verification, and troubleshooting
  - SNMP trap uses the same shell for its access guide step sequence
  - K8s notes use the same shell with a slight header/body spacing and description treatment variation

  - keep it inside the alarm integration guide family because the abstraction is domain-shaped and would become garbage if promoted to the framework layer
- Migration:
  - migrated repeated guide section shells in Zabbix to `AlarmIntegrationGuideSectionPanel`
  - migrated SNMP trap access guide shell to the same business component
  - migrated K8s notes shell to the same business component while preserving its custom description styling and filled background
- Storybook:

    - [src/stories/alarm-integration-guide-section-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-integration-guide-section-panel.stories.tsx)

### SectionHeader variant convergence for guide and panel headings
- Candidate:
  - the repeated `SectionHeader` style recipes that were still passed as raw class strings across multiple apps and shared business families:
    - [src/components/alarm-zabbix-guide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-zabbix-guide/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/alarm-k8s-guide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-k8s-guide/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/snmp-trap-guide-panel/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/snmp-trap-guide-panel/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/alarm-integration-guides/credentials-panel.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-integration-guides/credentials-panel.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/detail/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/detail/page.tsx)
- Reason:
  - the shared component already existed, but its real style variants were still encoded ad hoc in consumer class strings
  - that left the design system incomplete: consumers were technically reusing the component, but not yet reusing a governed API for the repeated visual contracts
  - this is a classic governance smell, the shell is shared but the variant semantics are still fragmented
- Cross-app comparison:
  - alarm guide panels and SNMP trap guidance repeatedly used the same panel-heading treatment:
    - `text-[16px] leading-6`
    - standard guide description copy sizing
  - alarm detail guide subsections and credentials panels repeatedly used the same compact-heading treatment:
    - `text-[16px] font-medium leading-tight`
  - these are stable design-system variants, not one-off business exceptions
- Decision:
  - extend `SectionHeader` with explicit shared variants instead of creating another wrapper component or continuing to repeat style strings
  - added:
    - `variant="panel"`
    - `variant="compact"`
  - this keeps the abstraction in the framework layer where it belongs
- Migration:
  - migrated repeated guide-panel headings in Zabbix, K8s notes, and SNMP trap guidance to `variant="panel"`
  - migrated alarm integration compact subsection headings and credentials panel headings to `variant="compact"`
  - left genuinely custom description styling as an override on top of the variant, for example the K8s notes eyebrow-like description
- Storybook:
  - updated the shared contract story at:
    - [src/stories/section-header.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/section-header.stories.tsx)
  - Storybook now documents the panel and compact variants as first-class shared API, rather than relying on consumer-level className conventions

### Alarm integration subsection heading convergence onto SectionHeader

    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetData/list/fieldModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetData/list/fieldModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/detail/associations/associationsModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/detail/associations/associationsModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/detail/attributes/attributesModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/detail/attributes/attributesModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/detail/attributes/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/detail/attributes/page.tsx)
- Reason:
  - each of these surfaces still owned real CMDB-specific bodies, validation, or visual workflows
  - but they no longer needed app-local modal footer assembly once `OperateFormModal` and `ModalActionFooter` were already the governed framework contract
  - leaving them on raw `OperateModal` would keep recreating slightly different confirm/cancel layouts inside one app family
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `fieldModal`, attribute group editing, attribute editing, and association editing match the same standard action-modal pattern already converged in `system-manager` and earlier CMDB management flows
  - `selectInstance` and `exportModal` keep business-specific body layouts, but their action area still uses the same framework shell contract
  - by contrast, this pass deliberately does not claim that the table/filter/export body, association visualization body, or attribute-builder body are framework components
- Decision:
  - converge these surfaces on `OperateFormModal`
  - keep the bodies app-local because the repeated pattern is the modal shell contract, not the entire business interaction
  - treat this as framework-shell reuse plus business-component retention, which avoids both duplicate shells and fake abstraction
- Migration:
  - replaced raw `OperateModal` footer assembly with `OperateFormModal` props
  - mapped app-specific extra actions into shared footer slots:
    - `fieldModal` uses `secondaryActions` for "confirm and associate"
    - attribute group modal uses `secondaryActions` for "continue add"
  - left custom business content intact, including drag sorting, relation selection, search/filter panels, and attribute-builder forms
- Storybook:
  - no new Storybook leaf was added in this slice because the governing framework component already exists at:
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormModal`
  - this migration increases real app coverage behind that existing Storybook contract instead of creating another near-duplicate story or component family

### MLOps modal contract convergence

  - they differed in field content and submission targets, but not in modal-shell semantics

  - their action pattern matches the same modal contract already adopted in `system-manager`, `cmdb`, and alarm settings drawers:

  - unlike heavier editors such as algorithm configuration, these modals do not need custom footer structure or special multi-step shell behavior

  - this change increases cross-app adoption of that contract and reduces duplicate modal shell implementations inside MLOps

- Reason:
  - these four surfaces are still standard action modals:
    - title
    - form body
    - confirm / cancel
    - loading state
  - their business-specific complexity is in the form fields, not in the shell contract
- Cross-app comparison:
  - their action pattern matches the same contract already converged across `system-manager`, `cmdb`, `mlops`, `monitor`, `node-manager`, and `job`
  - they differ from heavier OpsPilot surfaces such as tool selectors, knowledge extraction steps, and rule builders, which carry richer business workflows and should not be flattened into a fake shared abstraction
- Decision:

  - keep the knowledge/provider/settings form bodies app-local

  - replaced raw `OperateModal` usage with `OperateFormModal`
  - preserved existing validation, loading state, and field initialization behavior

  - this change increases real cross-domain adoption of the existing shared modal contract:

### OpsPilot edge modal contract convergence

  - `configModal` is a longer provider-edit form but still stays inside one standard action-modal contract

### Charts type taxonomy convergence
- Candidate:

    - [src/stories/time-series-bar-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/time-series-bar-chart.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/time-series-composed-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/time-series-composed-chart.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/mini-trend-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/mini-trend-chart.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/monitor-echarts-line-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-echarts-line-chart.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/stacked-bar-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/stacked-bar-chart.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/horizontal-category-bar-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/horizontal-category-bar-chart.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/chart-drag-selection.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/chart-drag-selection.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - after organizing state, dimension, legend, and formatting branches, the remaining flat chart-type titles still mixed runtime shape and implementation naming
  - these stories now show a second stable grouping layer:
    - time-series renderers
    - category/distribution charts
    - interaction helpers
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `TimeSeriesAreaChartCanvas`, `TimeSeriesBarChart`, `TimeSeriesComposedChart`, `MiniTrendChart`, and `EChartsLineChart` all define reusable time-series display grammar used across monitor, log, mlops, and dashboard surfaces
  - `StackedBarChart` and `HorizontalCategoryBarChart` define category/distribution chart variants with different orientation and stacking semantics
  - `useChartDragSelection` is a reusable interaction primitive rather than a chart renderer, so it should not sit beside chart-type leaves without its own branch
- Decision:
  - extend the Charts taxonomy with three additional stable subtrees:
    - `Framework/Charts/TimeSeries/*`
    - `Framework/Charts/Category/*`
    - `Framework/Charts/Interaction/*`
  - keep the previously introduced `State/*`, `Dimension/*`, `Legend/*`, and `Formatting/*` branches unchanged
- Migration:
  - retitled the remaining time-series, category, and interaction stories into the new subtree structure
  - updated the family overview text so the full current chart taxonomy is explicit inside Storybook
- Storybook:
  - no new chart leaf stories added in this slice
  - no shared runtime API changed
  - the Charts family now reads as a real framework taxonomy instead of a mix of grouped and flat titles

  - `ChartLegend` and `ChartWithSidebarLegend` define the shared legend/layout branch
  - `ChartAxisTickLabel`, `ChartSeriesTooltip`, and `EChartsTooltipCard` define the shared formatting branch
  - those branches are reused across multiple chart types and product areas, so Storybook should expose them as governed subtrees
- Decision:
  - keep one `Framework/Charts/FamilyOverview`

    - `Framework/Charts/Dimension/*`

### Forms upload taxonomy convergence

    - `UploadDropPanel` is the shared primitive
    - `SingleFileUploadPanel` and `MultiFileUploadPanel` are compatibility wrappers for explicit consumer contracts
  - keeping all three as siblings under `Framework/Forms/*` hid that ownership structure
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `UploadDropPanel` is the framework primitive behind upload flows used by OpsAnalysis, Job, OpsPilot, CMDB, and integrations
  - the single-file and multi-file wrappers differ only in consumer-facing semantics and defaults, not in core panel structure
  - that makes them one component family, not three unrelated forms leaves

    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/SingleFilePanel`
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/MultiFilePanel`
- Migration:
  - retitled the three existing upload stories into the new subtree
  - added a family overview that explains primitive-versus-wrapper ownership
- Storybook:
  - one new family story added
  - no shared runtime API changed in this slice
  - the upload subtree now expresses the actual framework contract hierarchy instead of a flat list

### Security taxonomy convergence
- Candidate:
  - the shared Security stories that still lived as a flat list:
    - [src/stories/password-policy-notice.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/password-policy-notice.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/password-fields.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/password-fields.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/password-form-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/password-form-modal.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/editable-password-field.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/editable-password-field.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/auth-secret-field.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/auth-secret-field.stories.tsx)
  - the existing family overview:

- Reason:
  - Security now governs two distinct shared subdomains:
    - password policy and password-entry workflows
    - secret-entry leaves and auth-secret switching
  - leaving every member directly under `Framework/Security/*` made the family flatter than the actual design-system ownership model
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `PasswordPolicyNotice`, `PasswordFields`, and `PasswordFormModal` form one stable password workflow contract reused by user-profile, system-manager, and auth reset flows
  - `EditablePasswordField` and `AuthSecretField` form the secret-input branch reused by OpsPilot, NodeManager, integration editing, and dynamic-form consumers
  - the two branches are related, but they are not the same abstraction level
- Decision:
  - keep one `Framework/Security/FamilyOverview`
  - reorganize members into two stable subtrees:
    - `Framework/Security/Password/*`
    - `Framework/Security/Secrets/*`
- Migration:
  - retitled:
    - `Framework/Security/Password/PolicyNotice`
    - `Framework/Security/Password/Fields`
    - `Framework/Security/Password/FormModal`
    - `Framework/Security/Secrets/EditablePasswordField`
    - `Framework/Security/Secrets/AuthSecretField`
  - updated the family overview to explicitly showcase both password workflow and secret-input workflow
- Storybook:
  - no story removed
  - no shared API changed in this slice
  - the Security subtree now exposes clearer ownership boundaries instead of a flat list

### Secret input convergence
- Candidate:
  - the editable password input under
    [src/components/dynamic-form/editPasswordField.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/dynamic-form/editPasswordField.tsx)
  - the password branch of
    [src/components/auth-secret-field/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/auth-secret-field/index.tsx)
- Reason:
  - both components were rendering the same core secret-input behavior:
    - masked input
    - visibility toggle
    - editable current value
    - plain `string -> onChange(string)` contract
  - `AuthSecretField` adds a legitimate `private_key` variant, but its password branch should not keep a second standalone password-input implementation
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `EditablePasswordField` is already used by `DynamicForm`, `OpsPilot` provider settings, skill configuration, and SystemManager auth-source forms
  - `AuthSecretField` is used across NodeManager controller installation flows and integration batch editing
  - difference analysis:
    - `AuthSecretField` owns the auth-type switch between password and private-key upload
    - `EditablePasswordField` is the stable leaf-level password/secret input contract
- Decision:
  - keep `AuthSecretField` as the higher-level auth secret switcher
  - reuse `EditablePasswordField` for its password branch instead of preserving two parallel password-input implementations
- Migration:
  - extended `EditablePasswordField` with `disabled` and `className` support
  - rewired `AuthSecretField` password mode to delegate to it
  - preserved the private-key upload branch and all current consumer APIs
- Storybook:

- Candidate:
  - the temporary-password reset form in
    [src/app/(core)/auth/signin/PasswordResetForm.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/(core)/auth/signin/PasswordResetForm.tsx)
  - the existing shared security contract:
    - [src/components/security/password-fields/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/security/password-fields/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/security/password-policy-notice/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/security/password-policy-notice/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/security/password-form-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/security/password-form-modal/index.tsx)
- Reason:
  - the security layer already had one governed password contract for:
    - password fields
    - password-policy notice
    - validation rules
  - but the auth temporary-password reset flow was still hand-rolling its own `Input.Password` pair and local length check
  - that created an unnecessary second implementation for the same password-entry semantics
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `top-menu/user-info/passwordModal.tsx` and `system-manager-user-password-modal/index.tsx` already consume the governed security password components
  - the auth reset flow differs in page layout and submission endpoint, but not in password-entry semantics
  - difference analysis:
    - auth reset is page-scoped, not modal-scoped
    - password policy fetch and submit endpoints differ from authenticated in-app flows
    - the field contract itself is the same
- Decision:
  - do not introduce a new auth-specific shared shell
  - reuse the existing security field and policy components directly inside the auth page
- Migration:
  - replaced the hand-written password inputs in `PasswordResetForm.tsx`
  - wired the page to `PasswordFields`
  - added `PasswordPolicyNotice`
  - reused `usePasswordPolicy` so the page follows the same password policy source as other governed security flows
- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - no shared component API changed
  - existing `Framework/Security/*` stories remain the source of truth for the reused shared contract

### Upload drop-panel convergence
- Candidate:
  - the two framework-level upload panels:
    - [src/components/single-file-upload-panel/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/single-file-upload-panel/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/multi-file-upload-panel/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/multi-file-upload-panel/index.tsx)
  - their current consumers across business domains:
    - `OpsAnalysis` import/export flows
    - `Job` file distribution flows
    - `OpsPilot` local knowledge upload flows
    - `CMDB` and `Integrations` import shells layered on the single-file variant
- Reason:
  - both components rendered the same drag-and-drop upload surface with the same shell structure
  - the real differences were only default semantics:
    - single-file mode prefers `maxCount`
    - multi-file mode prefers `multiple`
    - icon defaults differ
  - maintaining two parallel implementations at the framework layer would keep accidental duplication inside the design system itself
- Cross-app comparison:
  - business flows differ in validation, help copy, allowed file types, and follow-up actions
  - the actual upload surface contract is the same across those flows:
    - drag area
    - icon
    - primary upload text
    - hint block
    - optional extra actions below
- Decision:
  - extract one lower-level shared primitive:
    - [src/components/upload-drop-panel/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/upload-drop-panel/index.tsx)
  - keep `SingleFileUploadPanel` and `MultiFileUploadPanel` as compatibility wrappers with their existing public APIs
  - make Storybook treat `UploadDropPanel` as the primary framework contract
- Migration:
  - added the shared `UploadDropPanel`
  - rewired `single-file-upload-panel` and `multi-file-upload-panel` to delegate to it
  - preserved wrapper behavior and existing business imports
- Storybook:
  - added [src/stories/upload-drop-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/upload-drop-panel.stories.tsx)
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/DropPanel`
  - existing single/multi wrapper stories remain for compatibility coverage
  - the new Storybook entry is now the canonical framework upload-surface contract

  - the new family-level contract:

  - dataset upload and dataset release had already matured into a coherent business workflow family, but Storybook still represented them as flat `Business/MLOps/*` entries
  - that made it harder to scan MLOps contracts by workflow boundary, especially once both upload and release had their own dedicated stories and were already shown together inside the top-level family overview

  - updated:

### UserProfile family contract convergence
- Candidate:
  - the shared top-menu user profile surfaces:
    - [src/components/top-menu/user-info/userInformation.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/top-menu/user-info/userInformation.tsx)
    - [src/components/top-menu/user-info/passwordModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/top-menu/user-info/passwordModal.tsx)
    - [src/components/top-menu/user-info/versionModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/top-menu/user-info/versionModal.tsx)
  - the new Storybook contract entries:
    - [src/stories/system-manager-user-profile-password-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-user-profile-password-modal.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/system-manager-user-profile-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-user-profile-family.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - the user-profile flow had stopped being a single-component case: account information, password change, and version history are now three distinct shared business surfaces
  - `UserInformation` and `VersionModal` already had Storybook entries, but the password modal still lacked a top-menu user-profile contract, and the subtree as a whole lacked one family overview
  - Storybook-first governance works better when related shared business surfaces are visible both individually and as one discoverable family
- Cross-app comparison:
  - these components are shared, but they are not framework primitives:
    - `UserInformation` owns editable account profile state
    - `PasswordModal` owns self-service password reset for the signed-in user
    - `VersionModal` owns release-log browsing for the current product surface
  - what they share is a stable business boundary: the top-menu user profile workflow
  - that means the right contract shape is a business family, not a framework category
- Decision:
  - keep each member as its own shared business component
  - add the minimum story seams needed to render the password surface without backend coupling
  - add one family overview under `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/*` so the subtree becomes navigable as a coherent governed family
- Migration:
  - added optional injection props to `PasswordModal` for:
    - password policy loading
    - password reset submission
  - kept runtime behavior unchanged when those props are not supplied
  - added a standalone user-profile password story and a user-profile family overview story
- Storybook:
  - added:
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/PasswordModal`
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/FamilyOverview`
  - existing member stories continue to represent:
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/InformationDrawer`
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/VersionModal`
  - this turns user profile into a first-class governed business subtree rather than a loose pair of isolated stories
  - follow-up:
    - [src/stories/system-manager-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-family.stories.tsx) now includes the new user-profile family as a first-level SystemManager overview section, so the subtree is discoverable from both the dedicated family path and the domain-level overview

### User profile version modal contract completion
- Candidate:
  - the shared user-profile support surface:
    - [src/components/top-menu/user-info/versionModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/top-menu/user-info/versionModal.tsx)
  - the new Storybook contract:
    - [src/stories/system-manager-version-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-version-modal.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - `VersionModal` is a shared runtime component in the top-menu user profile flow, but it had no Storybook contract
  - unlike the recent `OperateFormModal` migrations, this component does not primarily suffer from shell duplication; its real governance gap was that the behavior lived only in runtime and depended directly on live fetches plus markdown loading
  - Storybook-first governance requires the component contract to be inspectable without backend coupling
- Cross-app comparison:
  - this surface belongs with the same user-profile family as:
    - [src/components/top-menu/user-info/userInformation.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/top-menu/user-info/userInformation.tsx)
  - the stable business behavior is:
    - version log modal
    - tabbed release file selection
    - markdown content rendering
    - empty-state handling when no version logs exist
  - there is no evidence yet that this should become a broader framework modal abstraction; the missing contract is business-level, not shell-level
- Decision:
  - keep the component on its current modal shell
  - add the minimum injection points needed for Storybook verification:
    - version-file loading override
    - version-content rendering override
  - use those seams only to decouple Storybook from live `/api/versions` and markdown fetch behavior
- Migration:
  - added optional injection props to `VersionModal` for:
    - fetching version files
    - rendering version content
  - kept runtime behavior unchanged when those props are not supplied
- Storybook:
  - added:
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/VersionModal`
  - story variants cover:
    - populated version log content
    - empty-state behavior
  - this closes a Storybook contract gap for a shared user-profile component without introducing a new abstraction

  - because this component sits in the global user profile flow, letting it keep a local footer composition would preserve style drift on one of the most visible shared account surfaces
  - it was also missing a Storybook contract entirely, which made it a shared runtime component outside the governed component center

  - add just enough action injection to let Storybook render this shared business component without live backend coupling

    - base info update
  - left nested password and email verification subflows unchanged

### Integration editing modal-shell convergence follow-up
- Candidate:
  - the remaining shared integration editing surfaces that were still hand-composing:
    - [src/components/operate-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/operate-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx)
  - the already-governed framework shell they should reuse:
    - [src/components/operate-form-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/operate-form-modal/index.tsx)
  - the migrated shared business components:
    - [src/components/integration-instance-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-instance-edit-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-config-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-config-edit-modal/index.tsx)
  - the Storybook contract alignment:
    - [src/stories/integration-instance-edit-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-instance-edit-modal.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-batch-edit-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-batch-edit-modal.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-config-edit-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-config-edit-modal.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these three integration components were already shared business surfaces, but they still rewired the same governed modal footer contract locally
  - after `OperateFormModal` existed, leaving them on manual `OperateModal + ModalActionFooter` composition would preserve duplicate framework ownership inside one business family
  - this is exactly the kind of reuse debt the component-governance branch is supposed to pay down: same framework shell, different integration-specific body and data flow
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `IntegrationInstanceEditModal` covers single-instance rename/group editing and batch group assignment
  - `IntegrationBatchEditModal` covers bulk multi-field editing with optional field toggles and auth-secret handling
  - `IntegrationConfigEditModal` covers async-loaded config editing with empty-state handling
  - their bodies differ, but the shell contract stays stable:
    - same operation modal frame
    - same confirm/cancel action semantics
    - same loading/disabled footer responsibilities
  - that means the reuse boundary is the modal shell only; the integration body logic remains business-owned
- Decision:
  - do not create another integration-specific modal shell
  - migrate these components onto the existing framework `OperateFormModal`
  - also correct the config editor story ownership from `Framework/*` back to `Business/Integrations/Editing/*`, because async configuration editing is integration-domain behavior, not a generic framework overlay
- Migration:
  - migrated:
    - `IntegrationInstanceEditModal`
    - `IntegrationBatchEditModal`
    - `IntegrationConfigEditModal`
  - no business behavior changed; only shell ownership moved to the governed framework composite
- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - updated story ownership:
    - `Framework/Overlays/AsyncConfigModal`
  - existing integration editing stories now all point at components that reuse the same governed modal-shell contract, keeping Storybook truthful about both framework and business ownership

- Candidate:

  - the new shared shell:

  - first-wave shared component migrations:
    - [src/components/knowledge-qa-edit-drawer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/knowledge-qa-edit-drawer/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/knowledge-qa-detail-drawer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/knowledge-qa-detail-drawer/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/alarm-detail-drawer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-detail-drawer/index.tsx)
  - the Storybook contract:
    - [src/stories/content-form-drawer.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/content-form-drawer.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/feedback-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/feedback-family.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - several shared read/write drawers were hand-composing the same `ContentDrawer` shell with the same governed footer semantics
  - the stable shared pattern is not the body content itself, but a content-oriented drawer that still wants standardized confirm/cancel and optional secondary actions
  - leaving each drawer to wire that footer locally would keep action ordering, spacing, and read-only vs edit action treatment drifting across apps
- Cross-app comparison:
  - direct shared-component matches already existed in:
    - knowledge QA editing
    - knowledge QA detail review
    - alarm detail inspection
  - wider app-level evidence also exists in CMDB and alarm flows where `ContentDrawer` repeatedly receives `ModalActionFooter`
  - the shared behavior stays stable:
    - right-side content/detail drawer
    - governed footer actions
    - page- or business-owned body, tabs, and domain actions
  - the variable part remains body structure and business controls, so the right abstraction boundary is still the shell only
- Decision:
  - add `ContentFormDrawer` as the content-side peer to `OperateFormDrawer` and `OperateFormModal`
  - keep it thin and API-compatible with the current `ContentDrawer` plus `ModalActionFooter` composition
  - migrate only the shared components that already match cleanly in this slice; defer mixed patterns such as nested modal flows
- Migration:
  - added `ContentFormDrawer`
  - migrated:
    - knowledge QA edit drawer
    - knowledge QA detail drawer
    - alarm detail drawer
  - intentionally left `cmdb-subscription-drawer` unchanged because its repeated governed action contract lives in an inner modal rather than the outer content drawer shell
- Storybook:
  - added:
    - `Framework/Feedback/ContentFormDrawer`
  - updated:
    - `Framework/Feedback/FamilyOverview`
  - this keeps the new shell visible in Storybook as part of the governed feedback/drawer contract center instead of leaving it implicit inside reused business components

- Candidate:

  - first-wave runtime migrations in alarm settings:
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertAssign/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertAssign/components/operateModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertEnrichment/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertEnrichment/components/operateModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/correlationRules/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/correlationRules/components/operateModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/components/operateModal.tsx)
  - the Storybook contract:
    - [src/stories/operate-form-drawer.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/operate-form-drawer.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - the repo has many drawers and modals that manually restitch the same footer contract around `OperateDrawer`
  - this repetition is not business-specific in itself; the stable shared pattern is a right-side operation drawer with a governed confirm/cancel footer
  - leaving every page to wire this shell by hand keeps spacing, action ordering, disabled/loading semantics, and secondary-action placement vulnerable to drift
- Cross-app comparison:
  - repeated pattern examples exist across alarm, cmdb, job, log, mlops, monitor, node-manager, ops-analysis, opspilot, and system-manager
  - the stable shared behavior is:
    - same drawer shell
    - same footer action component
    - page-owned submit handler and copy
  - the unstable part is form body and domain semantics, so the right abstraction boundary is the shell only, not a business form scaffold
- Decision:
  - introduce one thin framework component that composes `OperateDrawer` and `ModalActionFooter`
  - keep the body and submit logic inside each page or business component
  - migrate the alarm settings operate modals first because they already shared nearly identical shell wiring and benefit immediately from style convergence
- Migration:
  - added `OperateFormDrawer` as a framework shell
  - migrated four alarm settings operate modals to the new shell without changing their form bodies or submit logic
  - deferred wider repo migration to later slices so the abstraction can prove itself on one governed family first
- Storybook:
  - added one shared framework story:
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormDrawer`
  - this story is now the contract source for the composed shell instead of requiring reviewers to infer usage from repeated page-level wiring

    - [src/components/operate-form-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/operate-form-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/operate-drawer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/operate-drawer/index.tsx)

- Reason:

  - after the modal/drawer shell convergence work, these members now form a stable framework family rather than a loose set of unrelated stories
  - adding a family overview makes Storybook better at showing when to use:
    - raw shell primitives
    - governed form-shell composites
    - lightweight empty feedback
- Cross-app comparison:
  - framework feedback members travel across multiple business domains and are intentionally style-governing, not business-owning
  - the modal and drawer pairs now show a clear boundary:

    - composed form shell for the default confirm/cancel operation pattern
  - `CompactEmptyState` complements that family by covering the low-data/precondition branch of shared feedback surfaces
- Decision:
  - add one `Framework/Feedback/FamilyOverview`
  - keep the existing single-component stories in place
  - use the family overview as the high-level contract map for future feedback-surface migrations

  - this now gives the framework layer a navigable contract center for feedback surfaces instead of relying on users to infer relationships from scattered single stories

### Alarm integration guides Storybook convergence
- Candidate:
  - the alarm integration detail guide surfaces that already form one coherent business family, but were not fully represented in Storybook:
    - [src/app/alarm/components/k8sGuide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/components/k8sGuide/index.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/components/zabbixGuide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/components/zabbixGuide/index.tsx)
    - the already-governed SNMP peer:
      - [src/components/snmp-trap-guide-panel/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/snmp-trap-guide-panel/index.tsx)
  - the new Storybook contract entries:
    - [src/stories/alarm-integration-k8s-guide.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-integration-k8s-guide.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/alarm-integration-zabbix-guide.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-integration-zabbix-guide.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/alarm-integration-guides-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-integration-guides-family.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - `alarm/(pages)/integration/detail/page.tsx` already depends on three distinct source-specific guide surfaces
  - only the SNMP guide had been surfaced in Storybook before this slice
  - that left Kubernetes and Zabbix onboarding contracts outside the component-governance center even though they are stable business UI surfaces with non-trivial layout, copy, and action semantics
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared low-level primitives inside these guides are already governed elsewhere:
    - `CompactEmptyState`
    - `CodeSnippet`
    - `CopyableDetailList`
    - copy helpers and endpoint/secret displays
  - the guide containers themselves are not yet strong cross-app shared candidates:
    - `K8sGuide` is alarm-specific and tightly coupled to alarm source metadata download flow
    - `ZabbixGuide` is alarm-specific and tightly coupled to alarm integration webhook mapping semantics
    - `SnmpTrapGuidePanel` is simpler and already lives as a shared business component because its structure is reusable enough on its own
  - this means the current family boundary is business-domain level, not framework level
- Decision:
  - do not force `K8sGuide` and `ZabbixGuide` into a generic shared integration-guide abstraction yet
  - instead, make the family explicit in Storybook under:
    - `Business/Alarm/Integrations/Guides/*`
  - keep `SnmpTrapGuidePanel` as an existing governed shared business peer and reference it from the family overview
- Migration:
  - no runtime replacement was performed in this slice
  - governance work focused on Storybook-first visibility:
    - add standalone stories for `K8sGuide` and `ZabbixGuide`
    - add one family overview that shows the three guide types as one alarm integration guide family
- Storybook:
  - added:
    - `Business/Alarm/Integrations/Guides/K8sGuide`
    - `Business/Alarm/Integrations/Guides/ZabbixGuide`
    - `Business/Alarm/Integrations/Guides/FamilyOverview`
  - existing `Business/Integrations/SNMP/GuidePanel` story remains as the reusable SNMP member contract
  - this keeps Storybook truthful: the family is visible as alarm business UI, while the one reusable SNMP panel remains discoverable in the broader integrations space

### Integrations onboarding family contract convergence
- Candidate:
  - the shared onboarding business components that already serve multiple integration flows, but were only represented as isolated single-component stories:
    - [src/components/integration-step-callout/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-step-callout/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-setting-row/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-setting-row/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-access-complete/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-access-complete/index.tsx)
  - the new family-level contract entry:
    - [src/stories/integration-onboarding-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-onboarding-family.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - Storybook already exposed these onboarding surfaces individually, but did not show them as one governed business family
  - that weakened Storybook as the contract center because reviewers could inspect props one by one, but not the shared flow semantics they create together
  - all three components belong to the same business boundary: guided integration onboarding before or after app-specific configuration shells
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `IntegrationStepCallout` is reused by monitor and log K8s onboarding through app-specific presets
  - `IntegrationSettingRow` is reused by monitor flow onboarding, monitor K8s onboarding, log K8s onboarding, and shared K8s asset field composition
  - `IntegrationAccessComplete` is reused by monitor flow, monitor K8s, log K8s, and CMDB K8s completion flows
  - the shared semantics are stable across those apps:
    - prerequisite guidance
    - labeled onboarding field rows
    - completion state with next-step actions
- Decision:
  - keep the composed onboarding overview under `Business/Integrations/Onboarding/*`
  - keep the member component stories in framework taxonomy where their structural contracts now live:
    - `Framework/Content/StepCallout`
    - `Framework/Layout/SettingRow`
    - `Framework/Feedback/CompletionResult`
  - this preserves the business boundary at the family-composition level while keeping individual shared primitives governed in one place
- Migration:
  - no runtime component migration was needed in this slice because the shared components were already in use across apps
  - the governance change is Storybook-first:
    - add one composed family overview that demonstrates how the family travels together through onboarding flows
- Storybook:
  - added one new business story:
    - `Business/Integrations/Onboarding/FamilyOverview`
  - existing member stories stay in place:
    - `Framework/Content/StepCallout`
    - `Framework/Layout/SettingRow`
    - `Framework/Feedback/CompletionResult`
  - this makes onboarding a first-class governed subtree rather than a loose set of isolated stories

### OpsAnalysis dashboard canvas empty-state convergence
- Candidate:
  - the final runtime raw `Empty` branch left in app code:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardCanvas.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardCanvas.tsx)
  - the new governed business component and contract entry:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-dashboard-empty-state/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-dashboard-empty-state/index.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-dashboard-empty-state.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-dashboard-empty-state.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - this empty state is not a generic "no data" surface
  - it expresses a specific `ops-analysis` dashboard workflow state: the canvas has no view yet, and the next correct action is to add one
  - the domain design guidance in [src/app/ops-analysis/design.md](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/design.md) explicitly requires empty states to provide a next-step action, including "no widget: prompt to add a component"
- Cross-app comparison:
  - true framework-level shared empty patterns already governed elsewhere:
    - `CompactEmptyState` for selection prerequisites and simple list/table empties
    - `ChartEmptyState` for chart/data absences
  - this dashboard canvas case differs in three important ways:
    - it is canvas-level, not chart-level
    - it requires a business action, not just passive feedback
    - it is specific to dashboard editing semantics in `ops-analysis`
  - similar create-entry affordances in other apps are either toolbar buttons or list onboarding states, but they do not share the same canvas contract strongly enough to justify a new framework abstraction
- Decision:
  - do not create a new framework "action empty state"
  - introduce one `ops-analysis` business component instead
  - keep permission gating outside the component so the component owns the visual contract while the page keeps authority over edit permissions
- Migration:
  - replaced the inline `antd` `Empty` block in:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardCanvas.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardCanvas.tsx)
  - moved the business styling and layout contract into:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-dashboard-empty-state/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-dashboard-empty-state/index.tsx)
- Storybook:
  - added one new business story:
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/Dashboard/EmptyState`
  - story variants document:
    - standard add-view prompt
    - locked action state
    - read-only state without an action
  - this keeps the contract in Storybook and avoids duplicate app-local empty-state markup

### Refresh action convergence follow-up
- Candidate:
  - the remaining icon-only refresh affordances that still had app-local button markup even after the first `RefreshIconButton` migration wave:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/incidents/detail/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/incidents/detail/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/knowledgeGraphPage.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/knowledgeGraphPage.tsx)
  - the shared contract center:
    - [src/components/refresh-icon-button/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/refresh-icon-button/index.tsx)
    - [src/stories/refresh-icon-button.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/refresh-icon-button.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these four surfaces were all the same business-light interaction pattern: an icon-only control whose sole job is to refresh the current view
  - they differed only in chrome:
    - bordered pill button
    - text-style toolbar icon
    - absolute-positioned page-corner icon
    - loading-capable floating action
  - leaving them app-local would keep rebuilding the same framework behavior in multiple apps with slightly different markup
- Cross-app comparison:
  - true matches:
    - dashboard toolbar refresh in ops-analysis
    - incident detail refresh in alarm
    - node list refresh in node-manager
    - graph refresh in opspilot
  - intentionally excluded from this abstraction:
    - `job-record` re-execute action, because it is not a refresh semantic
    - `unifiedFilterBar` reset action, because it resets filters rather than reloads data
    - spinner/status actions such as environment sync and credential fetch, because those are business actions with different feedback semantics
- Decision:
  - keep one shared framework component for icon-only refresh affordances
  - extend the component with a small `variant` surface instead of spawning app-specific refresh wrappers
  - add only the variant required by current real usage:
    - `default`
    - `text`
- Migration:
  - extended the shared contract:
    - [src/components/refresh-icon-button/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/refresh-icon-button/index.tsx)
  - documented the new display variant in Storybook:
    - [src/stories/refresh-icon-button.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/refresh-icon-button.stories.tsx)
  - migrated app surfaces:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/incidents/detail/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/incidents/detail/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/knowledgeGraphPage.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/knowledgeGraphPage.tsx)
- Storybook:
  - no story removed
  - one shared story variant added: `Text`
  - this keeps refresh affordances in `Framework/Actions/RefreshIconButton`, so app pages no longer define their own refresh-button contract
  - verification:
    - repo search shows these four migrated surfaces now consume `RefreshIconButton`
    - the non-migrated refresh-like actions are all semantically different and intentionally remain outside this contract

    - [src/stories/system-manager-transfer-left-tree.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-transfer-left-tree.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/system-manager-transfer-right-tree.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-transfer-right-tree.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/system-manager-transfer-tree-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-transfer-tree-panel.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/system-manager-user-permission-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-user-permission-modal.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - Storybook was splitting one shared business family across two domains:
    - `Business/SystemManager/RoleTransfer/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserOrg/*`
  - that weakens Storybook as the contract center because the family overview and the family members no longer live under the same discoverable path
  - the components themselves are already family-owned and reused as role-assignment building blocks inside user/group flows, so the mismatch was taxonomy-only
- Cross-app comparison:
  - these members all belong to the same role-assignment surface:
    - transfer shell
    - left tree
    - right tree
    - transfer panel
    - permission modal
  - `UserOrg` remains a higher-level business workflow that composes this family together with:
    - group navigation
    - group edit modal
    - password modal
  - that means the right Storybook relationship is:
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserOrg/FamilyOverview` for the composed admin flow
    - `Business/SystemManager/RoleTransfer/*` for the dedicated shared role-transfer family
- Decision:
  - keep the `UserOrg` family overview as the orchestration-level contract
  - move all role-transfer member stories under the dedicated `RoleTransfer` subtree
  - do not create duplicate stories under both trees

- Storybook:

    - `Business/SystemManager/RoleTransfer/*`

### Storybook taxonomy and contract-center convergence for Integrations family
- Candidate:
  - the shared integration business surfaces already present in `src/components`, but still represented in Storybook as a flat list plus a separate `K8s` subtree:
    - catalog entry card
    - detail layout shell
    - instance/config editing modals
    - batch and Excel import modals
    - K8s-specific onboarding surfaces
- Reason:
  - this family had enough shared business components to justify a governed Storybook family, but it still lacked a single overview-level contract entry
  - the generic business stories were also still flat under `Business/Integrations/*`, which makes family discovery weaker than the other converged domains
  - `K8s` already behaved like a true sub-family instead of a one-off variation, so the taxonomy needed to reflect both the generic integration core and the K8s specialization

### Storybook ownership correction for integration onboarding surfaces
- Candidate:
  - four shared integration surfaces that were still classified as `Framework/*` stories even though their props and reuse boundaries are already integration-domain specific:
    - [src/stories/integration-automatic-configuration-shell.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-automatic-configuration-shell.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-setting-row.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-setting-row.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-step-callout.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-step-callout.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-access-complete.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-access-complete.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these components are shared, but they are not generic framework primitives in the same sense as buttons, tables, or layout shells
  - each one encodes integration-onboarding semantics directly:
    - automatic configuration workspace has import, batch operation, monitored-object, and permission-aware confirm semantics
    - setting row is only used by integration access flows
    - step callout ships with integration onboarding presets
    - access-complete ships with integration completion presets across monitor/log/cmdb onboarding flows
  - leaving them under `Framework/*` weakens the design-system boundary by making domain-specific shared components look universally reusable when they are not
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `IntegrationAutomaticConfigurationShell` is reused in:
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx)
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx)
  - `IntegrationSettingRow` is reused in monitor/log K8s access configuration and monitor flow access configuration
  - `IntegrationStepCallout` is reused in monitor/log K8s access configuration via integration-owned presets
  - `IntegrationAccessComplete` is reused in monitor/log/cmdb completion flows via integration-owned presets
- Decision:
  - keep the integrations family overview in `Business/Integrations/*`
  - keep the individual shared component stories in framework taxonomy:
    - `Framework/Layout/ConfigurationWorkspace`
    - `Framework/Layout/SettingRow`
    - `Framework/Content/StepCallout`
    - `Framework/Feedback/CompletionResult`
  - use the business family overview to show how those framework contracts compose into one onboarding workflow, rather than duplicating leaf ownership under business paths
- Migration:
  - retitled:
    - [src/stories/integration-automatic-configuration-shell.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-automatic-configuration-shell.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-setting-row.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-setting-row.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-step-callout.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-step-callout.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-access-complete.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-access-complete.stories.tsx)
- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - no story removed
  - this slice clarifies ownership, so the integrations family can compose onboarding and configuration shells without re-owning their focused contract stories
  - verification:
    - repo search now shows `Framework/Layout/ConfigurationWorkspace`
    - repo search now shows `Framework/Layout/SettingRow` and `Framework/Content/StepCallout`
    - repo search now shows `Framework/Feedback/CompletionResult`
    - scoped `eslint` passed for the retitled story files

### Ops Analysis config family contract and runtime convergence
- Candidate:
  - the governed config family under [src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections)
  - the existing Storybook contracts:
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-config-sections.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-config-sections.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-value-format-config.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-value-format-config.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-threshold-color-config.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-threshold-color-config.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-value-mappings-config.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-value-mappings-config.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-single-value-settings-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-single-value-settings-panel.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-gauge-settings-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-gauge-settings-panel.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - this family was already the public business surface for gauge and single-value settings, but it still depended on `app/ops-analysis` for unit catalogs, threshold contracts, value-mapping contracts, and display-format runtime
  - the same runtime semantics were also consumed by shared widgets such as `single`, `gauge`, `bar-gauge`, `table`, `state-timeline`, and `top-n`, so leaving them under `app/ops-analysis` created a second contract center outside Storybook
  - there was also duplicate path/value access logic split between `thresholdUtils` and `objectPath`
- Cross-app comparison:
  - repeated semantics are real and stable across multiple shared business surfaces:
    - config sections use unit catalogs, threshold editing, and value-mapping editing
    - widgets use the same threshold/value-mapping/unit rules to render values consistently
    - Storybook already exercises both the panel-level family and widget-facing configurations
  - page orchestration, query composition, and chart-theme selection remain app-local and were intentionally left in `app/ops-analysis`
- Decision:
  - create family-owned contract/runtime modules:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/types.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/types.ts)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/runtime.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/runtime.ts)
  - make the old app entrypoints re-export these modules instead of keeping parallel implementations
  - migrate shared widgets to the same family-owned runtime so config editing and widget rendering now share one governed contract
- Migration:
  - added:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/types.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/types.ts)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/runtime.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/runtime.ts)

### Storybook taxonomy convergence for governed widget families
- Candidate:
  - the Storybook titles for the already-governed widget families:
    - Monitor widget stories under [src/stories/monitor-*.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-dashboard-widgets.stories.tsx)
    - Ops Analysis widget stories under [src/stories/ops-analysis-*.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-widgets.stories.tsx)
    - Log analysis widget stories under [src/stories/log-analysis-*.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/log-analysis-widgets.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - Storybook already separates `Framework/*` and `Business/*`, but these three governed business families still used inconsistent leaf naming like `OpsAnalysisBar`, `LogAnalysisLine`, and `WidgetsOverview`
  - that inconsistency makes component discovery harder and weakens Storybook as the single contract center because a family is not visually grouped the same way across domains
  - the underlying components were already converged; the taxonomy was the lagging part
- Cross-app comparison:
  - Monitor, Ops Analysis, and Log all now have real shared business families with overview stories plus multiple member stories
  - each family benefits from the same classification shape:
    - `Business/<Domain>/<Family>/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/<Domain>/<Family>/<Component>`
  - by contrast, app-local chart wrappers such as:
    - `src/app/monitor/components/charts/lineChart.tsx`
    - `src/app/mlops/components/charts/lineChart.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/components/charts/barChart.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/components/charts/barChart.tsx`
    are not good shared-component candidates right now because they are already business wrappers on top of framework chart primitives with different data contracts, overlays, and interaction semantics
  - a couple of adjacent business-family stories were also still on the old flat naming style and benefited from the same cleanup:
    - `Business/Monitor/Controls/InstanceSelector`
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/ViewToolbar/*`
- Decision:
  - keep the shared widget implementations as-is
  - normalize Storybook titles for the governed business families into a single taxonomy:
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/Widgets/*`
    - `Business/Log/Analysis/Widgets/*`
    - `Business/Monitor/Widgets/*`
    - plus adjacent already-governed business slices:
      - `Business/Monitor/Controls/*`
      - `Business/OpsAnalysis/ViewToolbar/*`
  - do not force a fake cross-app abstraction for app-local chart wrappers when the true reuse point is already the framework chart layer in `src/components`
- Migration:
  - retitled Ops Analysis widget stories, including:
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-widgets.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-widgets.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/ops-analysis-bar.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/ops-analysis-bar.stories.tsx)
