### Ops Analysis widget runtime convergence
- Candidate:
  - the shared widget family under [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets)
  - especially the members still borrowing app-owned runtime helpers:
    - [bar.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/bar.tsx)
    - [line.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/line.tsx)
    - [pie.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/pie.tsx)
    - [single.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/single.tsx)
    - [gauge.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/gauge.tsx)
    - [bar-gauge.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/bar-gauge.tsx)
    - [top-n.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/top-n.tsx)
- Reason:
  - even after contract convergence, the shared widget family still depended on `app/ops-analysis` for chart data transformation, chart theming, legend color generation, and comparable-value extraction
  - those semantics are intrinsic to the shared widget family itself and are already exercised through Storybook, so keeping them in the app tree left a second hidden contract center outside Storybook
  - the page-level request-building side of `compareQuery` is not shared widget runtime and should remain app-local
- Cross-app comparison:
  - the shared widget family reuses one coherent runtime vocabulary:
    - line/bar/pie data normalization
    - light/dark chart theme tokens
    - legend color generation
    - comparable-value extraction for single/gauge/bar-gauge
  - these helpers are family-scoped and reused across multiple widgets, not one-off page glue
  - by contrast, `buildCompareRequestParams`, `fetchCompareData`, and similar request orchestration remain tied to app data-source APIs and were intentionally left in `app/ops-analysis`
- Decision:
  - add a family-owned runtime module at [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts)
  - migrate the shared widgets to consume that runtime directly
  - keep app compatibility entrypoints, but turn them into re-exports of the family-owned runtime where appropriate
  - preserve app-only compare-query request helpers in place, while reusing the family-owned pure comparison helpers from there
- Migration:
  - added:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts)
  - updated the shared family barrel:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/index.ts)
  - migrated shared widgets to the family-owned runtime:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/bar.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/bar.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/line.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/line.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/pie.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/pie.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/single.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/single.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/gauge.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/gauge.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/bar-gauge.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/bar-gauge.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/top-n.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/top-n.tsx)
  - converted app entrypoints to compatibility re-exports:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/utils/chartTheme.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/utils/chartTheme.ts)
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/utils/randomColorForChart.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/utils/randomColorForChart.ts)

### Remaining shared-boundary cleanup for monitor gaps and role-transfer modal
- Candidate:
  - the last two clear shared-boundary leaks still left under `src/components`:
    - [src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx)
    - [src/components/system-manager-role-transfer/permissionModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/system-manager-role-transfer/permissionModal.tsx)
- Reason:
  - `echarts-line-chart` only depended on one app helper, `normalizeGapIntervals`, which is family-owned line-chart runtime rather than page logic
  - `permissionModal` only needed a narrow subset of the much larger `app/system-manager/types/permission.ts` file, so its component-facing contract was being over-owned by the app layer
- Cross-app comparison:
  - monitor gap normalization is specific to the monitor dashboard widget family and already validated indirectly by the existing monitor widget stories; it is not a cross-page orchestration concern
  - role-transfer permission modal is a shared business family inside System Manager, and the modal-facing types are reused by:
    - the component itself
    - [src/stories/system-manager-user-permission-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/system-manager-user-permission-modal.stories.tsx)

  - the broader permission type file still contains many application-permission editor contracts, table hooks, and permission-rule types that remain app-local
- Decision:
  - move `normalizeGapIntervals` into the existing monitor widget family runtime instead of keeping it in `app/monitor/utils/gapIntervals`
  - move the minimal permission-modal contract into the role-transfer component family instead of keeping it inside the larger app permission type module
  - do not move the rest of `gapIntervals.ts` or the rest of `permission.ts`, because those still belong to app-level workflows
- Migration:

  - updated the shared business component to consume the family contract:
    - [src/components/system-manager-role-transfer/permissionModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/system-manager-role-transfer/permissionModal.tsx)
  - updated the app permission type file to reuse the family-owned modal contract while keeping broader app permission models in place:

### Remaining ops-analysis runtime boundary classification
- Candidate:
  - the last app-runtime helpers still referenced from the ops-analysis shared widget family:
    - `singleValueSparkline`
    - `dashboardActions`
    - `dateTime`
- Reason:
  - after earlier runtime convergence, these were the final places where the shared widget family still leaned on `app/ops-analysis`
  - they are not equal in ownership:
    - sparkline fallback generation and action URL resolution are widget-family behavior
    - request/display time formatting is now used by widgets, app hooks, and settings flows, so it is a broader ops-analysis domain utility
- Cross-app comparison:
  - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/single.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/single.tsx) is the only shared consumer of fallback sparkline generation, and that behavior exists solely to support the shared single-value widget presentation contract
  - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/table.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/table.tsx) is the shared consumer of dashboard action URL assembly and mapping resolution; those semantics are defined by the shared widget config contract itself
  - [src/app/ops-analysis/utils/dateTime.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/utils/dateTime.ts) is reused by:
    - widget runtime display formatting
    - table widget request formatting
    - data-source hooks
    - widget request transforms
    - settings forms
    so it remains broader than one shared widget family
- Decision:
  - move `buildFallbackSparkline`, `buildDashboardActionUrl`, and `resolveDashboardActionParams` into the ops-analysis widget family runtime
  - keep `formatOpsRequestTime` and `formatOpsDisplayTime` in `app/ops-analysis/utils/dateTime.ts` as intentional app-owned domain utilities
  - preserve app compatibility entrypoints for the helpers that moved, so existing non-widget callers can migrate gradually
- Migration:
  - added family-owned runtime helpers to:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/runtime.ts)
  - updated the shared widget family barrel:

    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/table.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/table.tsx)
  - converted app compatibility entrypoints to re-exports:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/utils/singleValueSparkline.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/utils/singleValueSparkline.ts)
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/utils/dashboardActions.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/utils/dashboardActions.ts)
- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - no story removed
  - this slice further aligns runtime ownership underneath the existing ops-analysis widget stories without changing their public contract
  - verification:
    - repo search shows the shared ops-analysis widgets no longer import `singleValueSparkline` or `dashboardActions` from `app/ops-analysis`
    - the only remaining shared-widget imports from `app/ops-analysis` are the intentional `dateTime` utilities

### Remaining shared-boundary audit after family convergence passes
- Candidate:
  - the remaining `src/components -> src/app` imports after the current convergence work
- Reason:
  - after pulling multiple families onto family-owned contracts, the remaining imports are no longer a single undifferentiated backlog
  - some are still likely governance candidates, while others are now better understood as acceptable business/runtime dependencies
  - without classifying them, the next extraction would risk chasing raw import counts instead of real shared-boundary problems
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `ops-analysis-widgets/*` and `ops-analysis-config-sections/*` still depend on `app/ops-analysis` utility modules such as chart transforms, threshold/value-mapping helpers, compare-query helpers, and unit-format helpers
  - `log-analysis-widgets/*` still depend on `app/log` chart/data formatting helpers and a couple of log-specific view-model types
  - `monitor-dashboard-widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx` still depends on `app/monitor/utils/gapIntervals`
  - `system-manager-role-transfer/permissionModal.tsx` still depends on `app/system-manager/types/permission`
- Decision:
  - classify the remaining imports into three buckets:
    - likely next governance candidates:
      - `log-analysis-widgets/*` runtime/data helpers
      - `ops-analysis-config-sections/*` threshold/value-mapping helper ownership
    - acceptable shared-family runtime/util dependencies for now:
      - `ops-analysis-widgets/*` chart/runtime helpers from `app/ops-analysis/utils/*`
      - `monitor-dashboard-widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx` depending on `normalizeGapIntervals`
    - acceptable business-contract dependencies for now:
      - `system-manager-role-transfer/permissionModal.tsx` depending on system-manager permission types
  - do not force another extraction in this audit slice itself; use the classification to choose the next highest-signal family intentionally
- Migration:
  - no code migration in this slice beyond the audit record
- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - no story removed
  - this audit confirms that the Storybook-governed families already cleaned up are now mostly blocked only by family runtime helpers or business contracts, not by large app-local component ownership
  - next-priority candidates:
    - `log-analysis-widgets` runtime/type helpers, because they still look closest to a family-owned shared contract surface
    - `ops-analysis-config-sections` helper/type/runtime cleanup, because these stories already present as a governed config family

- Reason:
  - after the earlier implementation migrations, the monitor family still depended on app-local type and runtime ownership for `ChartData`, `MetricItem`, `GuideItem`, `MetricUnit`, compare-tone formatting, collection-status legend, and refresh-frequency defaults
  - that meant the Storybook-governed monitor family was still borrowing pieces of its contract and small runtime vocabulary from `app/monitor`, even though those semantics are intrinsic to the shared family itself
  - this is a classic last-mile governance leak: implementation moved, but the family contract and supporting runtime stayed behind
- Cross-app comparison:
  - these types and constants are reused across many monitor business widgets: summary cards, trend panels, detail rows, collection status, dashboard shells, and line-chart rendering
  - Storybook already exercises the same family-level semantics independently of page-level monitor routing and query orchestration
  - heavier page/query helpers such as `normalizeGapIntervals` and dashboard data-fetching logic remain app-specific, so the right scope is the widget-facing contract and minimal runtime, not the whole monitor app layer

### OpsPilot card family style-boundary convergence for EntityCard
- Candidate:
  - `EntityCard`, `StudioCard`, and `SkillCard` in the OpsPilot card family:
    - [src/components/opspilot-entity-card/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/opspilot-entity-card/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/opspilot-studio-card/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/opspilot-studio-card/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/opspilot-skill-card/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/opspilot-skill-card/index.tsx)
  - their Storybook contracts:
    - [src/stories/opspilot-entity-card.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/opspilot-entity-card.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/opspilot-studio-card.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/opspilot-studio-card.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/opspilot-skill-card.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/opspilot-skill-card.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - the shared business card implementation `EntityCard` still imported `@/app/opspilot/styles/common.module.scss`
  - that meant the Storybook-governed card contract depended on an app-local style bundle, which is exactly the kind of shared-boundary leak that makes multi-app UI governance drift over time
  - by contrast, the remaining imports from `src/components/opspilot-*` into `@/app/opspilot/types/*` are currently business data contracts, not duplicate framework presentation layers
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `EntityCard` is the visual base used by both `StudioCard` and `SkillCard`, so its shell styling is already a family-level shared contract inside OpsPilot rather than a page-local implementation detail
  - `StudioCard` and `SkillCard` differ in label mapping and redirect targets, but they intentionally reuse the same card surface, layout, pin affordance, menu affordance, and metadata row
  - `ApprovalCard` is Storybook-covered too, but it is a different interaction pattern and not a candidate for forced convergence with the entity cards
- Decision:
  - move the style contract used by `EntityCard` into a component-local stylesheet under the component directory
  - keep `StudioCard` and `SkillCard` as separate business adapters instead of over-abstracting them into a new shared wrapper, because their difference is domain meaning, not accidental duplication
  - keep the existing Storybook stories as the canonical contract; no new overview story is needed for this slice
- Migration:
  - added [src/components/opspilot-entity-card/styles.module.scss](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/opspilot-entity-card/styles.module.scss)

### Monitor dashboard widgets barrel convergence and shared-boundary audit
- Candidate:
  - the shared family barrel [src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/index.ts)
  - the existing Storybook contracts already consuming that barrel, including:
    - [src/stories/monitor-dashboard-widgets.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-dashboard-widgets.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/monitor-stat-card.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-stat-card.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/monitor-detail-panel.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-detail-panel.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - although most monitor widget implementations had already been migrated into `src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/`, the family barrel still used a one-line `export * from '@/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets'`
  - that kept the shared surface coupled to the app layer and undermined the governance goal that Storybook-backed shared families should resolve directly to shared implementations
  - the same audit also surfaced some remaining `src/components -> src/app` imports in `opspilot` and `system-manager`, but those are currently business-boundary type/style dependencies rather than duplicate framework components
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `monitor-dashboard-widgets` is now a mature shared business family with many Storybook contracts and broad runtime use across monitor dashboards
  - `opspilot-entity-card`, `opspilot-studio-card`, `opspilot-skill-card`, `opspilot-approval-card`, and `system-manager-menu-page-item` are also Storybook-covered, but their remaining `app` imports are tied to app-specific types or styling contracts, not duplicate cross-app presentation primitives
  - that means the monitor barrel was the clear governance leak to fix now, while the `opspilot` and `system-manager` items should be reviewed later as business-family boundary cleanup rather than forced framework extraction
- Decision:
  - replace the monitor family barrel with explicit exports from `src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/*`
  - do not create any new shared abstraction for the `opspilot` or `system-manager` cards in this slice
  - keep Storybook family stories as the contract entrypoints; no extra overview-only shared components are introduced
- Migration:
  - updated [src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/index.ts) to export directly from shared implementation files instead of re-exporting the app-layer barrel
  - no runtime page imports needed to change because consumers were already importing from `@/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets`
- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - no story removed
  - the existing monitor stories remain the source of truth and now resolve through a real shared-family barrel instead of an app redirect
  - verification:
    - repo search shows `src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/index.ts` no longer imports from `@/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets`
    - repo search confirms the remaining `src/components -> src/app` imports after this change are concentrated in business-family types, styles, and domain utilities rather than another obvious proxy barrel

### Monitor chart runtime lower-layer extraction for MiniTrendChart and useECharts
- Candidate:
  - the shared lower-layer chart runtime pieces currently used across monitor widget members:
    - `MiniTrendChart`
    - `useECharts`
    - `echarts-setup`
  - the Storybook contract [src/stories/mini-trend-chart.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/mini-trend-chart.stories.tsx)
- Reason:
  - several already-migrated shared business components (`StatCard`, `DetailMetricRow`, `HorizontalBarPanel`) still depended on the app-local `MiniTrendChart`
  - `MiniTrendChart` itself depended on `useECharts`, and `useECharts` depended on app-local `echarts-setup`, which meant the shared family still had a hidden internal runtime dependency chain back into the app tree
  - rather than immediately migrating the heaviest ECharts-backed shells, this lower-layer extraction strengthens the current shared boundary with a smaller, safer slice
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `MiniTrendChart` is already a Storybook-governed framework-level chart primitive used by multiple monitor business components
  - `useECharts` is an internal runtime hook reused by `MiniTrendChart`, `RingChartPanel`, and `EChartsLineChart`
  - this is not a case for expanding public API; it is a case for moving the implementation substrate into the shared family so the public components stop depending on app-local runtime code
- Decision:
  - move `MiniTrendChart`, `useECharts`, and `echarts-setup` into the real shared family directory
  - keep `MiniTrendChart` as the public framework-level contract
  - keep `useECharts` and `echarts-setup` internal implementation-layer modules, not Storybook-facing components

  - added:
    - [src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/mini-trend-chart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/mini-trend-chart.tsx)

    - [src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/echarts-setup.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/echarts-setup.ts)
  - removed the app-local implementations:

    - [src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/useECharts.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/useECharts.ts)
    - [src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/echarts-setup.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/echarts-setup.ts)
  - updated shared-family consumers and remaining app-local chart members to import from the new shared-family runtime layer:

- Candidate:

  - `DetailMetricRow` is used directly by MongoDB dashboards and indirectly by shared detail-card composition in object dashboards
  - `DetailPanelCard` is reused across multiple monitor object dashboards including Apache, Docker, Ping, RabbitMQ, Tomcat, Zookeeper, K8s Node, and K8s Pod via shared dashboard helpers
  - this is stronger than one-module repetition: it is an established monitor detail-display system with stable props and predictable semantics
- Decision:
  - migrate `DetailMetricRow` and `DetailPanelCard` together into the real shared family directory
  - keep the public contract stable by continuing to expose both through the monitor widget family barrel
  - leave `MiniTrendChart` in place for now, because it is already a shared dependency boundary and does not block this pair from becoming true shared-family implementations

  - the registry now maps chart types to the governed barrel exports rather than direct `com*` implementation files

  - migrate runtime monitor dashboard object consumers to that barrel instead of keeping `../../shared/widgets` as a shadow public surface
  - leave family-internal sibling imports inside `src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/` untouched for now; this slice is about app/runtime consumers, not a full file move

### OpsPilot tool-editor runtime consumers converged on the shared family barrel
- Candidates:
  - the shared business family barrel [src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/index.ts)
  - the eight OpsPilot tool editors that reuse the same shared pieces but were still importing implementation files directly:
    - `elasticsearchToolEditor`
    - `jenkinsToolEditor`
    - `kubernetesToolEditor`
    - `mssqlToolEditor`
    - `mysqlToolEditor`
    - `oracleToolEditor`
    - `postgresToolEditor`
    - `redisToolEditor`
- Reason:
  - the shared `opspilot-tool-editor` family already has a governed Storybook contract for:
    - `ToolInstanceSidebar`
    - `ToolConnectionStatusTag`
    - `ToolEditorEmptyState`
  - but runtime consumers were still bypassing the family barrel and importing:
    - `@/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-instance-sidebar`
    - `@/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-connection-status-tag`
    - `@/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-editor-empty-state`
  - that left Storybook and runtime pointing at the same implementation through different public surfaces, which weakens the “Storybook-first shared contract” rule
- Cross-app comparison:
  - these three components are reused systematically across multiple OpsPilot tool editors, not just in one screen
  - unlike scheduler inputs, which already have runtime consumers importing through `@/components/opspilot-scheduler-inputs`, the tool-editor family still had app-level direct-file imports
  - this is a contract-consumption drift problem rather than a missing-component problem
- Decision:
  - keep the existing business-family API as the single shared entry point
  - migrate app-level consumers to the family barrel instead of exposing direct implementation-file imports as de facto public API
  - keep family-internal composition local; only app-level consumers need to be standardized onto the barrel

### Ops-analysis config sections moved from app-local implementations into the shared family
- Candidates:
  - the shared business family barrel [src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/index.ts)
  - its six governed section components, which were still implemented under app-local paths:
    - `metricFieldSelectorFormItem`
    - `singleValueSettingsSection`
    - `thresholdColorConfigSection`
    - `valueFormatConfigSection`
    - `valueMappingsConfigSection`
    - `gaugeSettingsSection`
- Reason:
  - Storybook already treated these as governed business components through the shared family, but the runtime implementation still lived under `src/app/ops-analysis/...`
  - that meant the public shared barrel was only proxying app-local files instead of owning the contract surface directly
  - this is exactly the kind of boundary drift that makes future reuse slippery: Storybook says “shared”, code structure still says “page implementation detail”
- Cross-app comparison:
  - current repo evidence shows these are business-layer `ops-analysis` configuration sections, not framework primitives
  - they are reused across multiple `ops-analysis` flows:
    - dashboard view configuration in [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/viewConfig.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/viewConfig.tsx)

    - Storybook stories under `Business/OpsAnalysis/Config/*`
  - there is no evidence they should be generalized beyond the business family right now, but there is clear evidence they should stop being page-local implementations
- Decision:
  - keep them as `Business/OpsAnalysis/Config/*` shared business components
  - move the actual implementations into `src/components/ops-analysis-config-sections/`
  - update app consumers to import through the governed shared family instead of direct `app/ops-analysis/components/*` paths
  - delete the duplicate app-local implementation files once the shared family becomes the single source

### Log and ops-analysis widget alias cleanup
- Candidates:

    - [src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/index.ts)
- Reason:

    - internal-style names like `ComBar`, `ComLine`, `ComPie`, `EventTable`
    - governed business names like `LogAnalysisBar`, `OpsAnalysisLine`, `OpsAnalysisEventTable`
  - this created duplicate public API surface for the same components, which conflicts with the goal of keeping one stable contract per shared component family
- Cross-app comparison:
  - Storybook stories already use the domain-prefixed exports (`LogAnalysis*`, `OpsAnalysis*`)
  - repo search found no external consumers of the generic alias names through the shared barrels
  - app-local wiring that still uses `Com*` imports references the implementation files directly inside each app, not the shared component barrels
- Decision:
  - keep the domain-prefixed public names as the governed business-component contract
  - remove the duplicate generic aliases from the shared barrels
- Migration:
  - updated [src/components/log-analysis-widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/log-analysis-widgets/index.ts) to remove `Com*` alias exports

- Storybook:

  - Storybook already points at the governed domain-prefixed names, so this slice makes the public API match the contract surface more closely

- Cross-app comparison:

  - it is a low-level implementation detail that supports the internal monitor widget family
- Decision:

### Monitor widget barrel surface cleanup
- Candidates:
  - internal monitor widget subcomponents that were exported through the shared barrel without real shared-consumer evidence:
    - `GuideTooltipContent`
    - `BarList`
- Reason:

  - repo evidence showed no application-level imports through the barrel for either one
  - keeping internal-only helpers on the shared surface increases API noise and conflicts with the “不要有垃圾组件” constraint
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `TitleWithGuide` is the real shared public unit used by monitor business panels
  - `GuideTooltipContent` is only an internal rendering detail of `TitleWithGuide`, plus a Storybook-only direct render case
  - `HorizontalBarPanel` is the real shared public unit used by monitor dashboards
  - `BarList` is only the internal list-only body used by `HorizontalBarPanel`; no independent barrel consumer exists
- Decision:
  - keep the internal implementations in place
  - remove `GuideTooltipContent` and `BarList` from the shared barrel so the public shared surface better matches actual governed components
- Migration:
  - updated [src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/index.ts) to stop exporting:

- Storybook:
  - no new story added
  - this slice reduces accidental shared-surface sprawl rather than introducing a new contract

  - `EChartsLineChart` is the lower-level reusable chart primitive underneath that composition
  - its contract is now analogous to other framework chart primitives already documented directly in Storybook
- Decision:
  - keep `TrendChartPanel` as a business component
  - add a direct framework-level story for `EChartsLineChart`
- Migration:

### Monitor dashboard ECharts tooltip-card convergence
- Candidate:
  - the monitor shared dashboard line widget at [src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx)
- Reason:
  - even after its loading/empty shell had been converged to `ChartSurface`, the widget still carried a private HTML-string tooltip implementation for two cases:
    - descriptive detail rows for timestamp-specific metadata
    - multi-series metric rows with color markers and formatted values
  - both cases now fit the governed `EChartsTooltipCard` contract
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `log` and `ops-analysis` formatter-based bar/line/pie tooltips already rely on `EChartsTooltipCard`
  - the monitor widget uses the same shared card shell, but with two row modes:
    - markerless label/value detail rows
    - circle-marker metric rows
  - the unique monitor logic is only the per-timestamp detail extraction and binary/unit-aware value formatting, which belongs at the mapping layer rather than in a separate tooltip surface
- Decision:
  - replace the private tooltip HTML with `EChartsTooltipCard`
  - keep the monitor-specific detail aggregation and value formatting local
- Migration:
  - updated [src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/widgets/echarts-line-chart.tsx) to render both tooltip modes through `renderEChartsTooltipCard`

  - they also still carried local loading/empty/sidebar wrapper structure even though the repo already standardizes that surface with `ChartWithSidebarLegend`
- Cross-app comparison:

  - `ops-analysis/comBar` repeats the same title + square marker + label/value rows
  - `ops-analysis/comLine` repeats the same title + circle marker + label/value rows, while keeping local zoom, threshold, and dual-axis behavior
  - `ops-analysis/comPie` repeats the same item-tooltip card used by the log pie family
  - all three also align with the same shared sidebar-shell concern already represented by `ChartWithSidebarLegend`
- Decision:
  - migrate tooltip presentation to `EChartsTooltipCard`
  - keep ops-analysis-specific zoom, threshold, legend-selection, and theme logic local

  - all three now render formatter-based tooltips through `renderEChartsTooltipCard`

### HTTP status trend migration to the shared time-series contract

- Reason:
  - after migrating the docker and redis trend wrappers, this widget was the remaining log time-series multi-line chart still carrying its own `ReactEcharts + ChartSurface + grid/legend/axis` shell
  - the only local behavior is status-code bucketing over time, which is data preparation rather than a distinct chart contract
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `apacheTrend`, `httpRequestTrend`, `kafkaTrend`, `mongodbTrend`, `windowsEventTrend`, plus the recently migrated docker/redis wrappers, all already treat `TimeSeriesComposedChart` as the governed framework shell for log trend charts
  - `httpStatusTrend` renders the same framework grammar:
    - time x-axis
    - multi-line area series
    - shared legend
    - shared y-axis formatting
  - its uniqueness is only the grouped status-code data shape, which belongs in local normalization before rendering
- Decision:
  - keep the status-code aggregation local
  - migrate the chart rendering surface to `TimeSeriesComposedChart`
- Migration:
  - updated [src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/http/httpStatusTrend.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/http/httpStatusTrend.tsx) to normalize grouped data and render through `TimeSeriesComposedChart`

  - no new story needed in this slice because the required shared layout variants were already added to `Framework/Charts/TimeSeries/ComposedChart`

### Heatmap and scatter governance decision: keep local for now
- Candidates:
  - [src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comHeatmap.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comHeatmap.tsx)
  - [src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comScatter.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comScatter.tsx)
- Reason:
  - both are still local `ReactEcharts` wrappers, so they needed an explicit audit rather than being left ambiguous
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `comHeatmap` combines:
    - top-N category ranking
    - time/category matrix normalization
    - heatmap color scale semantics
    - bottom visual map control
  - `comScatter` combines:
    - diagonal-threshold semantics
    - above/below partitioned datasets
    - comparison line overlay
    - label-bearing point tooltip behavior
  - current shared contracts in Storybook cover time-series composed charts, sidebar-legend charts, donut/bar summaries, tooltips, chart surfaces, and axis primitives, but there is not yet evidence of a second heatmap or diagonal-scatter implementation across apps
- Decision:
  - do not extract a shared heatmap or scatter framework component yet
  - keep both widgets local until we see real cross-app repetition or a second runtime implementation with the same semantic grammar
- Migration:
  - no code migration in this slice
  - this is a deliberate non-extraction decision to prevent premature shared API design
- Storybook:
  - no new shared story yet
  - if another app grows the same heatmap or diagonal-scatter grammar, introduce the shared framework contract in Storybook first, then migrate both implementations

### ECharts tooltip-card framework extraction
- Candidate:
  - the repeated HTML-string tooltip shell inside log dashboard ECharts widgets:
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comBar.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comBar.tsx)
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comLine.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/widgets/comLine.tsx)

- Reason:
  - these widgets were still repeating the same formatter-level presentation concern:
    - tooltip card chrome
    - title row
    - colored marker dot or square
    - label/value row layout
  - the difference between them lives in marker shape and the label/value mapping, not in the container presentation
  - leaving them as raw HTML strings would preserve an ungoverned parallel UI surface outside Storybook
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `log/comBar` and `log/comLine` repeated nearly the same axis-series tooltip shell, differing mainly in square-vs-circle markers and fallback labels
  - `log/comPie` used the same tooltip card pattern for item-level distribution rows
  - `monitor` and `mlops` already use the governed `ChartSeriesTooltip` contract for React-rendered chart tooltips, which confirms the shell concern is real even though the renderer path differs
  - the stable shared concern here is the tooltip card presentation, while chart data transforms and ECharts formatter payload mapping remain app-local
- Decision:
  - extract a framework-level `EChartsTooltipCard` instead of creating another app-local formatter helper
  - keep formatter-to-row mapping local in each widget so we do not overfit a single ECharts payload shape into the shared API
- Migration:
  - added [src/components/echarts-tooltip-card/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/echarts-tooltip-card/index.tsx)

  - introduced `Framework/Charts/Formatting/EChartsTooltipCard`

  - that made monitor dashboard charts a style and behavior outlier at the shell layer, even while using the same governed empty-state visual language
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `ChartSurface` is already the shared state shell used by chart primitives and business charts in `log`, `mlops`, `ops-analysis`, and shared `src/components/*`
  - monitor dashboard `EChartsLineChart` was still manually rendering the same concern with local absolute overlays
  - the shared concern is strictly chart-state presentation; the monitor widget keeps its own ECharts option building, gap interval overlays, and drag-range behavior
- Decision:
  - migrate the monitor widget to `ChartSurface`
  - keep tooltip formatting, chart rendering, and interaction logic local because they are still domain-specific

- Storybook:

### Stacked bar-chart empty-state convergence
- Candidate:
  - the fallback empty state inside `components/stacked-bar-chart/index.tsx`

  - the charts framework already has `ChartEmptyState` and `ChartSurface` as the approved display contract for chart-empty behavior
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `ChartEmptyState` is already used by `ChartSurface`, `HorizontalCategoryBarChart`, monitor dashboard widgets, and metric-preview surfaces
  - `StackedBarChart` was the outlier still rendering a local raw empty placeholder
  - the shared concern is empty-state presentation, while the stacked-bar chart keeps its own chart behavior and data model
- Decision:
  - replace the raw `Empty` fallback with `ChartEmptyState`
  - keep the stacked-bar chart otherwise unchanged
- Migration:

  - the remaining variation is only the title and payload mapping, which belongs at the call site instead of inside a private tooltip component

  - keep the stacked-bar-specific payload-to-tooltip mapping inside the chart component

### Shared chart axis tick label extraction
- Candidate:
  - the repeated SVG y-axis tick label rendering logic inside chart wrappers in:
    - `app/log/components/charts/barChart.tsx`
    - `app/mlops/components/charts/lineChart.tsx`
    - `app/mlops/components/charts/simpleLineChart.tsx`
    - `app/monitor/components/charts/lineChart.tsx`
- Reason:
  - these wrappers no longer share enough behavior to justify merging the whole chart components, but they do repeat the same display-layer concern:
    - right-aligned SVG axis labels
    - truncation at a stable length
    - native `<title>` fallback for overflowed labels
    - consistent chart text color and spacing
  - this is a framework display primitive, not business logic
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `log` bar charts use the pattern for numeric bar axes
  - `mlops` line charts and simple line charts use the same label shell for numeric and enum-like values
  - `monitor` line charts use the same shell with one offset tweak after local value formatting
  - the shared part is the rendered SVG label contract, while value formatting remains app-local
- Decision:
  - do not merge the chart wrappers themselves yet
  - extract the repeated axis-label renderer into one narrow framework component and keep all data formatting decisions local to each chart wrapper
- Migration:

  - the shared axis label is now part of the chart framework contract instead of staying hidden inside app-local wrappers

### Data-display framework family overview contract
- Candidate:
  - the shared framework data-display family spanning `CustomTable`, `EntityList`, `EllipsisWithTooltip`, severity/status tags, `UserAvatar`, `SemanticBadge`, `RatioProgressCell`, and `MarkdownRenderer`
- Reason:
  - Storybook already covered the member-level display primitives, but the framework still lacked one governed overview showing how dense tables, catalog lists, and inline status semantics combine into a shared data-presentation language
  - this family is one of the strongest style-convergence levers across apps because these components repeatedly appear inside list pages, detail panes, dashboards, event pages, and admin consoles
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `CustomTable` is reused heavily across `job`, `cmdb`, `node-manager`, `alarm`, `system-manager`, `monitor`, `log`, `mlops`, `ops-analysis`, and `opspilot`
  - `EllipsisWithTooltip` is reused across layout headers, tables, selectors, dashboards, and detail drawers
  - `EntityList` is reused by `node-manager`, `system-manager`, `mlops`, and `opspilot`
  - `EventLevelTag`, `EventStatusTag`, `UserAvatar`, `SemanticBadge`, and `RatioProgressCell` provide recurring inline semantics in alarm/event flows and dashboard tables
  - `MarkdownRenderer` is reused in job, log, cmdb, and opspilot detail/content surfaces
  - this is a framework-level display language rather than a single-domain widget family
- Decision:
  - keep the detailed member stories
  - add one framework-level family overview that captures three stable presentation layers:
    - dense operational table
    - entity catalog surface
    - inline display semantics
  - avoid introducing a new display wrapper because the real shared contract already exists in the reusable primitives and their composition
- Migration:
  - added [data-display-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/data-display-family.stories.tsx)
- Storybook:
  - introduced `Framework/DataDisplay/FamilyOverview`
  - Storybook now documents the shared data-presentation contract in addition to the individual display primitives

### Layout framework family overview contract
- Candidate:
  - the shared framework layout family across `components/*layout*`, `components/top-section`, `components/panel-shell`, and the side-menu shell stack
- Reason:
  - Storybook already covered many layout primitives individually:
    - `TopSection`
    - `PageLayout`
    - `PanelShell`
    - `DetailLayoutShell`
    - `RoutedDetailLayoutShell`
    - `SideMenuShell`
    - the integration configuration workspace variants
  - but the framework still lacked one governed overview showing how page scaffolds, detail shells, routed detail shells, and side-navigation shells fit together as one reusable layout vocabulary
  - without that overview, apps could reuse the same pieces while still drifting in composition and page structure
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `TopSection` is reused widely across `system-manager`, `opspilot`, `mlops`, and detail layouts
  - `DetailLayoutShell` and `RoutedDetailLayoutShell` are reused by `node-manager`, `opspilot`, `system-manager`, and integration detail flows
  - `PageLayout` is reused by `system-manager` admin and structure pages
  - `PanelShell` is reused in monitor and system-manager configuration/editing pages
  - the layout family is a framework-level structure layer used across multiple domains, not a single app’s page shell
- Decision:
  - keep the detailed member stories
  - add one framework-level family overview that captures the stable structure layers:
    - page scaffold
    - detail shell
    - routed detail shell
    - side navigation shell
  - avoid adding a new wrapper abstraction because the right shared surface already exists in the current layout primitives and their composition
- Migration:
  - added [layout-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx)
- Storybook:

### Alarm business family overview contract
- Candidate:
  - the shared business family rooted at `components/alarm-*` plus the incident actions composed through `DeclareIncident` and `RelatedAlertsPanel`
- Reason:
  - Storybook already covered the member-level alarm surfaces:
    - `AlarmFilters`
    - `AlarmSearchFilter`
    - `AlarmAction`
    - `AlarmTable`
    - `AlarmDetailDrawer`
    - `AlarmEventTable`
    - `DeclareIncident`
    - `RelatedAlertsPanel`
  - but the family still lacked one governed overview showing how the list workspace, detail drawer, incident declaration, and related-alert triage combine into the real alarm workflow used across alarm and incident pages
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `AlarmFilters`, `AlarmSearchFilter`, `AlarmTable`, and `AlarmAction` are reused by the main alarm list and incident-detail selection flows
  - `AlarmDetailDrawer` is reused by both the table flow and the incident gantt flow
  - `AlarmDetailDrawer` composes `DeclareIncident` and `RelatedAlertsPanel` as stable business slots, which makes the workflow boundary larger than any single table or drawer component
  - `AlarmEventTable` and `AlarmPageBreadcrumb` also support the surrounding alarm pages, but the clearest governed family boundary is the alert-workspace and detail workflow
- Decision:
  - keep the detailed member stories
  - add one family-level overview that captures the stable alarm workflow layers:
    - list workspace
    - detail drawer shell
    - related-alert coordination and incident declaration
  - avoid adding another wrapper component because the reuse signal is already represented by the existing shared surfaces and their composition
- Migration:
  - added [alarm-family.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/alarm-family.stories.tsx)
- Storybook:
  - introduced `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview`
  - Storybook now documents the alarm workflow family above the member-level alarm components
  - `Business/Alarm` no longer depends on readers inferring family boundaries only from separate component stories

### Charts framework family overview contract

  - `log` reuses `ChartSurface`, `ChartLegend`, `ChartWithSidebarLegend`, `ChartRendererShell`, and `TimeSeriesComposedChart`
  - this is a true framework family spanning multiple business domains rather than a domain-local widget set
- Decision:
  - keep the detailed member stories
  - add one framework-level family overview that captures the three stable contract layers:
    - renderer state shell
    - dimension-aware chart workspace
    - sidebar-legend chart layout
  - avoid creating a new wrapper component because the reuse signal is already well-served by the existing primitives and their composition

  - this is a stable business-domain family rather than a framework-level primitive set
- Decision:
  - keep the existing member stories and the dedicated `RoleTransfer` family overview

  - avoid inventing new abstraction layers because the reuse signal is in composition, not in another shared wrapper

### Log-analysis renderer kept app-local, shared imports converged
- Candidate:
  - the `log` analysis dashboard renderer entry inside `app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/components/widgetWrapper.tsx`
- Reason:
  - after auditing the current state, the renderer registry and request orchestration still have only one real runtime consumer
  - promoting that whole renderer into `src/components` now would optimize for symmetry with ops-analysis more than for proven reuse
  - however, the renderer already depends on a stable shared widget family whose public barrel is now governed through Storybook
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `ops-analysis` renderer had two distinct consumers before extraction:
    - dashboard wrapper
    - topology chart node
  - `log` renderer currently remains a single-consumer entry
  - the shared part that is already proven is not the renderer entry itself, but the underlying log-analysis widget family exposed from `components/log-analysis-widgets`
- Decision:
  - keep the `log` renderer app-local for now
  - migrate its stable base widget imports to the governed shared barrel so the real shared surface becomes the only import path for those family members
  - defer renderer extraction until a second real consumer or stronger cross-surface reuse appears
- Migration:
  - updated [src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/components/widgetWrapper.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/components/widgetWrapper.tsx) to consume shared base widgets from [src/components/log-analysis-widgets/index.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/log-analysis-widgets/index.ts)
  - the app-local renderer now relies on the governed public widget surface instead of importing those base members directly from page-local widget files
- Storybook:
  - no new renderer story in this slice
  - the supporting shared widget contracts already live in Storybook and now better match the import path used by the renderer

### Cross-app widget renderer shell extraction
- Candidate:
  - the repeated renderer-level state shell spread across:
    - `app/log/(pages)/analysis/dashBoard/components/widgetWrapper.tsx`
    - `app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/widgetWrapper.tsx`
    - `app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/topology/components/chartNode.tsx`
- Reason:
  - these surfaces keep different data-request and transformation logic, but they repeat the same UI-layer contract:
    - show a full-height loading state while a widget is resolving
    - show a centered fallback or error state when the renderer cannot proceed
    - otherwise render the real widget content
  - the repeated shell is cross-app UI behavior, not business-specific query logic
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `log` dashboard wrapper owns log query orchestration and local widget registry, but its UI shell was just a centered loading spinner plus unknown-type fallback
  - `ops-analysis` dashboard wrapper owns request caching, validation, and renderer wiring, but its outer shell still resolves to loading / error / content
  - `ops-analysis` topology chart node uses the same three-state renderer shell around a different chrome container
  - the shared semantic boundary is the renderer slot shell, while registry contents, fetch policies, and validation remain app-specific
- Decision:
  - introduce one narrow shared shell for renderer-slot states
  - keep existing request logic, renderer registries, and domain-specific error components outside that shell
- Migration:
  - added [src/components/chart-renderer-shell/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/chart-renderer-shell/index.tsx)
  - migrated log dashboard widget wrapper, ops-analysis dashboard widget wrapper, and ops-analysis topology chart node to consume the shared shell
- Storybook:
  - added [chart-renderer-shell.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/chart-renderer-shell.stories.tsx)
  - introduced `Framework/Charts/State/RendererShell` so this cross-app state shell now has a single governed contract

### Ops-analysis widget renderer extraction
- Candidate:
  - the shared widget-rendering entry previously implemented as:

    - `app/ops-analysis/components/widgetRegistry.ts`

  - despite that cross-surface reuse, the family still lived under `app/`, so the governed shared boundary remained implicit
  - Storybook already contained an app-local `WidgetShowcase`, which was a sign that this rendering family had become a stable reusable surface rather than a one-page implementation detail
- Cross-app comparison:
  - within ops-analysis, dashboard owns request orchestration while topology owns node chrome and loading/error composition
  - both surfaces converge on the same semantic contract:
    - resolve chart type
    - choose the registered widget component
    - pass through raw data, baseline data, loading, and optional query callbacks
    - render a fallback when the chart type is unsupported
  - no evidence yet supports promoting the whole renderer to a repo-wide framework component, so the correct boundary is an ops-analysis business-shared component
- Decision:
  - move the renderer and registry into `src/components`
  - keep individual widget implementations where they already live for now
  - add a Storybook contract for the renderer surface itself instead of relying only on app-local showcase coverage
- Migration:
  - moved the shared entry to [src/components/ops-analysis-widget-renderer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-widget-renderer/index.tsx)

  - removed the old app-local renderer and registry entrypoints so this family now has one governed home

    - dashboard widget wrapper

  - despite that real reuse, it still lived under `app/`, so it was not governed as a shared business component and had no Storybook contract

  - within ops-analysis, both dashboard and topology need the same semantic state: a compact chart/widget failure surface with warning icon plus centered message
  - the surrounding loading and data-fetch logic differs between those surfaces, but the rendered failure treatment is the same
  - broader cross-domain scanning did not show a proven generic chart error-state shared by log/monitor/mlops, so a domain-scoped business component is the right boundary for now
- Decision:
  - extract the shared failure UI into `src/components`
  - keep request orchestration and widget rendering helpers app-local

### Intentional grouped taxonomy retained
- Candidate:
  - remaining stories whose file names do not match the final Storybook leaf name, but where the current title is intentionally representing a grouped contract or subdomain taxonomy instead of a single shared component rename:
    - `opspilot-scheduler-inputs.stories.tsx`
    - `ops-analysis-threshold-color-config.stories.tsx`
    - `ops-analysis-value-format-config.stories.tsx`
    - `ops-analysis-value-mappings-config.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-group-edit-modal.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-group-tree.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-menu-group-card.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-menu-page-card.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-menu-page-item.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-role-transfer.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-source-menu-tree.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-transfer-left-tree.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-transfer-right-tree.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-user-password-modal.stories.tsx`
    - `system-manager-user-permission-modal.stories.tsx`
- Reason:
  - a raw filename-to-title audit still reports these stories as mismatches, but that signal is too blunt for governance decisions
  - some of these stories are component-family overviews rather than single-component contracts, and others rely on subdomain groupings such as `UserOrg` and `ApplicationMenu` that meaningfully reduce ambiguity without needing the full component prefix repeated in the final leaf
  - forcing every one of these into a `SystemManager*` or `Opspilot*` leaf would make the taxonomy longer without materially improving contract clarity
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `opspilot-scheduler-inputs.stories.tsx` is intentionally an overview for the shared scheduler input family, covering `TimeListField`, `MonthDayPicker`, and `CronEditor` together
  - the ops-analysis config stories already use the explicit shared section component names as their Storybook leaf contracts:
    - `ThresholdColorConfigSection`
    - `ValueFormatConfigSection`
    - `ValueMappingsConfigSection`
  - the remaining SystemManager stories are already disambiguated by meaningful second-level groupings such as `UserOrg` and `ApplicationMenu`, and they map cleanly onto the current shared component boundaries
- Decision:
  - keep these titles as-is for now
  - treat them as intentional grouped taxonomy, not unresolved drift
  - reserve further renaming for cases where a stronger public contract change or cross-domain ambiguity appears in real usage
- Migration:
  - no code changes in this slice beyond documenting the retained taxonomy decision
- Storybook:
  - Storybook remains the source of truth here as well, but the source of truth is the grouped taxonomy itself rather than a mandatory filename-to-leaf match
  - future audits should exclude these stories from “simple leaf mismatch” cleanup unless the underlying component contract changes

### OpsPilot and audit-filter Storybook title alignment
- Candidate:

  - `SystemManagerAuditFilterBar` is reused across login logs, operation logs, and error-log filtering flows
  - `OpsPilot` card surfaces are part of the shared entity-management vocabulary for studio and skill experiences rather than page-local one-offs
- Decision:
  - keep the existing shared components and taxonomy grouping
  - align only the single-component stories to the explicit governed contract names
  - do not rename grouped overview stories or deeper SystemManager subdomain stories in this slice where the current grouping still carries useful structural meaning
