### Business integrations automatic-configuration batch table operations convergence
- Candidate:
  - repeated batch-table orchestration in app-owned business pages:
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx)
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/automatic.tsx)
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerInstall/installConfig/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerInstall/installConfig/index.tsx)
  - new shared internal hook:
    - [src/hooks/useBulkTableOperations.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/hooks/useBulkTableOperations.ts)
- Reason:
  - these three pages each carried the same selection-state and batch-action orchestration around editable tables:
    - `selectedRowKeys`
    - batch edit selection filtering
    - batch delete confirm and empty-row fallback
    - import append flow
    - batch dropdown items and click dispatch
    - controlled `rowSelection`
  - the duplication was not accidental copy; it was the same interaction pattern implemented separately under monitor, log, and node-manager, which creates drift risk whenever one page tweaks import or deletion behavior
- Cross-app comparison:
  - stable shared behavior across all three surfaces:
    - controlled row selection
    - batch edit working on selected rows only
    - batch delete with confirm dialog and reset to one empty row when the last row is removed
    - excel import appending normalized rows into the current table
    - two-item batch menu with identical enable/disable semantics
  - differences that remain app-local and should not be abstracted away:
    - row key source: monitor/node-manager use `key`, log uses `instance_id`
    - empty row factory: monitor/log depend on integration init config, node-manager depends on controller install defaults
    - modal payload details: `nodeList`, `groupList`, empty-warning copy, and column schemas vary by page
    - submission and validation logic still belongs to each page
- Decision:
  - extract a shared hook, not a new visual component
  - keep ownership in shared internal logic because this is repeated business interaction orchestration, while the visible shells and domain stories remain with the integrations family
  - do not add another Storybook item for the hook itself, because Storybook remains the contract center for shared visual components, not non-visual state helpers

### Shared batch-edit modal convergence for node-manager controller uninstall
- Candidate:
  - existing shared modal:
    - [src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx)
  - remaining app-local duplicate:
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerUninstall/batchEditModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerUninstall/batchEditModal.tsx)
  - consuming page:
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerUninstall/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerUninstall/index.tsx)
- Reason:
  - controller uninstall still kept a separate batch-edit modal even though its real job was the same auth-aware field editing already covered by the shared batch-edit family:
    - edit one credential or connection field in a modal
    - support `auth_type` and `password/private_key` coupling
    - reuse the same `AuthSecretField` behavior
    - return normalized edited fields for fan-out across the table rows
  - the main difference was interaction mode, not a different component family:
    - integration pages use checkbox-driven multi-field batch editing
    - controller uninstall opens the same shell in single-field bulk-edit mode from inline edit icons
- Cross-app comparison:
  - monitor/log/controller-install already share the checkbox-driven batch-edit modal
  - controller-uninstall uses the same field types (`input`, `inputNumber`, `select`, `auth_input`) and the same auth/private-key semantics, but exposes only one field at a time
  - this is a stable variant boundary, not evidence for a second modal component
- Decision:
  - do not keep a node-manager-only `batchEditModal`
  - extend the existing shared modal with a small variant surface:
    - custom title
    - optional hidden field toggles
    - pre-enabled fields
    - initial auth type
  - keep the visual and behavioral contract in one shared modal instead of duplicating another business overlay
- Migration:
  - extended the shared modal to support the single-field bulk-edit variant:
    - [src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx)
  - migrated controller uninstall to the shared modal:

  - monitor/log wrappers that still duplicated the same step + drawer composition:

    - [src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/k8s/collectorInstall.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/k8s/collectorInstall.tsx)
- Reason:
  - after removing the explicit app-local `commonIssuesDrawer` wrappers, monitor/log still each repeated the same composition pattern:
    - render `K8sCollectorInstallStep`
    - hold drawer open state or ref
    - render `K8sCommonIssuesDrawer`
    - wire the step’s “common issues” action to the drawer
  - the remaining differences were business inputs, not composition structure:
    - verify API call
    - copy preset
    - common-issues preset

  - leave monitor/log wrappers in place only for their true business differences: API verification and preset selection

### Alarm rule-scope field convergence for repeated all-vs-filter settings flows
- Candidate:
  - repeated alarm settings sections:
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertAssign/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertAssign/components/operateModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/components/operateModal.tsx)
  - new shared business field section:
    - [src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these two settings drawers repeated the same business interaction block:
    - choose `all` vs `filter`
    - validate the rule scope field as a non-empty OR/AND structure
    - reveal the match-rule editor only in filter mode
  - the only real difference was the injected `MatchRule` flavor:
    - alert assignment uses `levelType="alert"`
    - shield strategy uses `levelType="event"`
- Cross-app comparison:
  - alert-assign and shield-strategy use the same copy keys, form field names, validation semantics, and layout offsets
  - alert-enrichment is adjacent but not identical:
    - it uses local `filterType` state instead of the `match_type` form field
    - it omits the same structural validator and carries enrichment-specific copy/tooltips
  - that makes alert-assign + shield-strategy a stable extraction boundary today, while alert-enrichment should be reviewed later instead of being forced into the same API prematurely
- Decision:
  - extract a shared business section component under `Business/Alarm`
  - keep `MatchRule` injected as a slot so the shared section owns the repeated scope/validation contract without pulling app-local editor implementation details into its API
- Migration:
  - added the shared rule-scope field:
    - [src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx)
  - migrated:

    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/components/operateModal.tsx)
- Storybook:

  - stories document both:
    - default all-scope mode
    - filter mode with the injected rule-editor slot visible
- Verification:
  - scoped `eslint` passed for the new shared field section, both migrated drawers, and the new story
  - follow-up audit showed `alertEnrichment` was adjacent but not identical at first pass, so it was intentionally left for a second slice rather than being forced into the same API too early

- Candidate:

### Framework empty-state convergence across alarm, opspilot, job, and monitor
- Candidate:
  - the existing framework empty-state family:
    - [src/components/compact-empty-state/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/compact-empty-state/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/chart-empty-state/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/chart-empty-state/index.tsx)
  - repeated app-local equivalents still written directly with Ant Design `Empty`:
    - simple list / selector states in Alarm and OpsPilot
    - chart / dashboard empty containers in Job and Monitor
- Reason:
  - multiple apps were still rendering the same `common.noData` empty state inline with `Empty`, even though the repo already had governed framework components for compact feedback and chart-area empty states
  - this was causing unnecessary style drift: spacing, image treatment, and muted text styling depended on whichever page happened to write the empty state
  - the reuse signal is cross-app and presentation-level, so it fits framework ownership instead of a business family
- Cross-app comparison:
  - one stable compact pattern appears across non-chart surfaces:
    - selector/config states with a lightweight simple-image empty state
    - identical message shape: `common.noData`
  - one stable chart pattern appears across visualization surfaces:
    - centered empty placeholder inside a chart shell or dashboard panel
    - same message shape, but different layout contract from list-like empties
  - that split is real and useful:
    - `CompactEmptyState` for compact panel/list/select shells
    - `ChartEmptyState` for chart canvases and dashboard visualization regions
- Decision:
  - do not create any new empty-state component
  - migrate direct `Empty + common.noData` usages onto the existing framework family according to layout semantics
  - keep richer business empty states or custom explanatory copy app-local until they show repeatable structure
- Migration:
  - compact feedback states migrated to the shared framework component:
    - [src/app/alarm/components/k8sGuide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/components/k8sGuide/index.tsx)

  - chart/dashboard empty states migrated to the shared chart contract:
    - [src/app/job/(pages)/home/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/home/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/dashboards/objects/mysql/dashboard.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/objects/mysql/dashboard.tsx)

    - [src/stories/chart-empty-state.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/chart-empty-state.stories.tsx)

### Monitor dashboard widget contract convergence for shared chart types
- Candidate:
  - the shared monitor widget family and its Storybook coverage:
    - [src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/types.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/types.ts)

  - the remaining app-owned compatibility layer that still duplicated family chart contracts:

    - [src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/types.ts](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/dashboards/shared/types.ts)
- Reason:
  - monitor widget contracts were only partially converged: `ChartData` and `MetricItem` were already being re-exported from the shared family, but `GapInterval` and `Dimension` still lived in parallel inside `app/monitor/types`
  - Storybook still had one direct `src/stories -> src/app` dependency through `mini-trend-chart.stories.tsx`, which meant the shared monitor chart family was not yet the only contract center for its own chart data shape
  - `app/monitor/dashboards/shared/types.ts` still carried repeated monitor-widget semantics such as `MetricUnit`, `GuideItem`, and related dashboard chart helper types
- Cross-app comparison:

    - `StatCard`
    - `TrendChartPanel`
    - `EChartsLineChart`
    - shared dashboard metric config and collection-status helpers under `app/monitor/dashboards/shared`
  - this is real family reuse, not a one-off type alias for a single page
- Decision:
  - keep `src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/types.ts` as the owning contract module
  - convert remaining app monitor compatibility layers to reuse that family-owned contract instead of preserving duplicate local interfaces
  - update Storybook to import the chart data type from the shared family barrel, not from `app/monitor/types`

  - expanded the shared family barrel so Storybook can consume the family contract directly:

  - updated the remaining story contract to use the family export:

### Ops Analysis view-toolbar family convergence for dashboard mode
- Candidate:
  - the existing shared business family:
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/editActions.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/editActions.tsx)
  - the remaining page-local parallel implementation:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx)
- Reason:
  - topology and architecture views were already using the shared `ViewToolbar` family, but dashboard still kept its own title, built-in tag, description, and edit-action shell
  - that left one business family documented in Storybook but not yet acting as the true contract center for all ops-analysis view headers
  - dashboard also carried a slightly different surface treatment, which made style convergence across apps harder
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared behavior already existed across three ops-analysis views:
    - title and description block
    - built-in badge
    - right-aligned action cluster
    - edit/save/cancel state transition
  - differences were variant-level, not contract-level:
    - topology adds zoom/select/presentation/time controls
    - architecture keeps a narrow fullscreen + edit cluster
    - dashboard adds refresh/export/add-view/add-group controls and uses a flatter surface style
- Decision:
  - do not create a new dashboard-only toolbar component under `src/components`
  - migrate dashboard onto the existing `ViewToolbar` + `ViewToolbarEditActions` family
  - extend `ViewToolbar` with a `style` override so valid variants like dashboard can reuse the same contract while tuning surface presentation without forking markup
- Migration:
  - updated the shared family contract:

  - added dashboard-mode coverage inside the existing business family overview instead of creating another duplicate toolbar story

  - repo audit now shows topology, architecture, and dashboard all converging on the same shared ops-analysis view-toolbar family instead of keeping a second dashboard-only shell

### Alarm rule-scope field follow-up for enrichment button-mode filtering
- Candidate:
  - the shared alarm rule-scope contract:
    - [src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx)
  - the remaining app-local parallel implementation:
    - [src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertEnrichment/components/operateModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertEnrichment/components/operateModal.tsx)
- Reason:
  - `alertAssign` and `shieldStrategy` had already converged on the shared `AlarmRuleScopeField`, but `alertEnrichment` still kept a local radio-button switch plus conditional `match_rules` block
  - the remaining difference was not business semantics, but presentation and control mode:
    - button-style radio instead of plain radio
    - local state control instead of form-owned `match_type`
    - no strict `match_rules` validator because enrichment cleans incomplete rows on submit
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared semantics across all three alarm settings forms:
    - scope toggle between `all` and `filter`
    - conditional match-rule editor
    - common label / tooltip / required-message contract
  - differences were variant-level only:
    - `alertAssign` and `shieldStrategy` use form-bound plain radio mode with strict validation
    - `alertEnrichment` uses externally controlled button mode and submit-time cleanup
- Decision:
  - extend the existing shared `AlarmRuleScopeField` with a minimal variant surface instead of creating an enrichment-only component
  - keep the abstraction small and explicit:
    - optional controlled `scopeValue` / `onScopeChange`
    - `radioVariant`
    - optional `labelTooltip`
    - optional `validateMatchRules`
    - optional `matchRulesClassName`
- Migration:
  - extended the shared component contract:
    - [src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/alarm-rule-scope-field/index.tsx)

### Chart wrapper audit: shared primitive reuse is already the right boundary
- Candidate:
  - repeated app-local wrappers found during cross-app audit:
    - [src/app/log/components/charts/barChart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/components/charts/barChart.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/components/charts/barChart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/components/charts/barChart.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/components/charts/lineChart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/components/charts/lineChart.tsx)
    - [src/app/mlops/components/charts/lineChart.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/components/charts/lineChart.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these are the most obvious remaining same-name chart components living under multiple apps
  - they look like framework candidates at first glance, so they needed an explicit audit to avoid accidental duplicate shared wrappers later
- Cross-app comparison:
  - common already-shared building blocks:
    - `TimeSeriesBarChart`
    - `TimeSeriesAreaChartCanvas`
    - `ChartSeriesTooltip`
    - `ChartAxisTickLabel`
    - `ChartWithDimensionPanel`
    - `useChartSeriesState`
  - remaining app-local differences are meaningful behavior, not just style:
    - log `barChart` is a single-series count chart with localized time formatting and range brushing
    - monitor `barChart` is a multi-series metric chart with dimension toggling and unit conversion
    - monitor `lineChart` adds threshold lines, gap intervals, event overlays, series-style overrides, and selectable ranges
    - mlops `lineChart` adds annotation clicks, brush timeline syncing, and label-dot rendering semantics
- Decision:
  - do not extract another shared `BarChart` or `LineChart` wrapper
  - the correct abstraction boundary is already the shared chart primitive layer under `src/components`
  - creating another top-level shared wrapper here would produce a weak umbrella API with lots of app-specific escape hatches, which would be a garbage component by this governance standard
- Migration:
  - no file migration in this slice
  - future chart convergence should continue by strengthening shared primitives or hook contracts, not by forcing these wrappers into a fake one-size-fits-all shell
- Storybook:
  - no new story added in this decision slice
  - existing shared chart primitive stories remain the source of truth for reusable behavior

### Event strategy identity fields convergence for log and monitor policies
- Candidate:
  - repeated strategy identity form block inside event policy editors:
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/basicInfoForm.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/basicInfoForm.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/basicInfoForm.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/basicInfoForm.tsx)
- Reason:
  - both forms repeated the same business identity surface:
    - strategy name
    - organization / group ownership selector
  - the surrounding forms diverge heavily after that, but this top block is stable and semantically shared across event strategy setup flows
  - keeping these two fields duplicated in app-local files made simple label / spacing / validation changes harder to govern consistently
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared semantics:
    - required strategy name field bound to `name`
    - required organization selector bound to `organizations`
    - same validation contract and same `GroupTreeSelector` control
  - variant differences:
    - log uses fixed-width input styling and no helper copy
    - monitor uses full-width styling and a helper description under the organization selector
    - monitor adds alert name, target asset selection, and schedule below this shared block, which remain app-owned
- Decision:
  - extract only the stable identity block into a shared business component
  - do not force alert-name, target-selector, or schedule logic into the same abstraction
  - keep the shared API narrow:
    - labels
    - placeholders
    - required message
    - optional helper copy
    - styling hooks for width differences
- Migration:

### Event alert information summary convergence for log and monitor detail drawers
- Candidate:
  - repeated alert-information summary blocks inside detail drawers:
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/event/alert/information.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/event/alert/information.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/alert/information.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/alert/information.tsx)
- Reason:
  - both pages repeated the same summary surface contract:
    - a bordered `Descriptions` overview
    - common alert metadata such as time, level, first alert time, organization / group, strategy name, notification state, operator, notifier
    - a close-alert action row under the summary
  - the content below the summary already diverges strongly and should stay app-owned:
    - log raw log table
    - monitor chart / PMQ message panel
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared summary semantics:
    - title-level information section
    - metadata rows driven by alert state
    - event-level color indicator
    - close-alert action slot
  - valid differences:
    - log includes collect type, alert type, aggregate fields, and a “see more” link action
    - monitor includes asset type, asset detail link, and asset group
- Decision:
  - extract only the summary shell into a shared business component
  - keep metadata values and downstream content generated inside each app
  - use an item-list contract plus action slot so the shared component owns structure, while each app keeps business-specific row composition

- Candidate:

- Reason:
  - all three flows repeated the same interaction skeleton:
    - modal title / optional subtitle
    - single-file upload panel
    - confirm / cancel footer
    - confirm disabled until a valid file or parsed payload exists
  - the business differences live outside the shell:
    - JSON parsing vs direct file upload
    - import API endpoint
    - optional template export action
    - error handling details
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - same modal chrome
    - same one-file upload affordance
    - same confirmation action row
  - variant differences:
    - monitor import enables confirm only after JSON parse success

    - CMDB instance import keeps a subtitle and shows template export below the panel

  - extract a shared business shell for file-import modal flows
  - keep parsing, API submission, template export, and backend error handling inside each page
  - use a narrow shell contract:
    - modal title / subtitle
    - confirm / cancel actions
    - upload panel props
    - optional extra content inside or after the upload panel

### K8s configuration flow shell convergence for log and monitor onboarding
- Candidate:

- Reason:
  - both pages repeated the same flow contract:
    - step 1 access config
    - step 2 collector install
    - step 3 access complete
  - they already shared most step internals through previously extracted components, but the top-level wizard orchestration and reset behavior still lived in two parallel page containers
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - `StepWizardFlow` with `commandData` carried across steps
    - reset-on-secondary-action completion behavior

  - valid differences:

    - different access-config and collector-install implementations

- Decision:

  - use a generic `commandData` state shape so the shell stays reusable without flattening app-specific step data into one rigid type

### Integration modal taxonomy and state-coverage hardening
- Candidate:
  - the two already-shared integration modals with Storybook coverage that no longer matched their governance boundary:
    - [src/components/integration-config-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-config-edit-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-instance-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-instance-edit-modal/index.tsx)
- Reason:
  - runtime convergence was already done, but Storybook still had two gaps:
    - `IntegrationConfigEditModal` story title had drifted from its later governance decision that it is a framework async overlay shell
    - both stories only showed one happy-path state, which was too weak for contract review
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `IntegrationConfigEditModal` is reused by log/monitor config editors and now behaves like a generic async load/render/submit overlay shell
  - `IntegrationInstanceEditModal` still carries direct integration-instance semantics and should remain business-scoped
  - this is a taxonomy and state-coverage problem, not a runtime abstraction problem
- Decision:
  - keep runtime components unchanged
  - move `IntegrationConfigEditModal` story ownership to framework scope
  - keep `IntegrationInstanceEditModal` in business scope
  - expand both stories to cover the states that actually define their contract
- Migration:
  - updated:
    - [src/stories/integration-config-edit-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-config-edit-modal.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/integration-instance-edit-modal.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/integration-instance-edit-modal.stories.tsx)
- Storybook:
  - `IntegrationConfigEditModal` now lives under:
    - `Framework/Overlays/AsyncConfigModal`
  - `IntegrationInstanceEditModal` stays under:
    - `Business/Integrations/IntegrationInstanceEditModal`
  - state coverage added:
    - `LoadedSchema`
    - `EmptyState`
    - `EditSingleInstance`
    - `BatchAssignGroup`

- Candidate:

  - the underlying surfaces now belong to two governance layers:

    - framework support shell for async config editing

  - explicitly label the config edit overlay as a framework-governed support shell inside the overview copy
  - do not duplicate the component contract back under business taxonomy

- Candidate:
  - repeated confirm/cancel footer blocks inside `OperateModal` consumers:
    - [src/components/integration-config-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-config-edit-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-instance-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-instance-edit-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/integration-batch-edit-modal/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/import-file-modal-shell/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/import-file-modal-shell/index.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/metric/groupModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/metric/groupModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/object/objectTypeModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/object/objectTypeModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/groupModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/groupModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/selectIcon.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/selectIcon.tsx)
- Reason:
  - multiple apps were still hand-authoring the same footer structure:
    - primary confirm button
    - secondary cancel button
    - ad hoc margin utility like `mr-[10px]` or `mr-2`
    - repeated loading/disabled wiring
  - this was not business-specific UI, just repeated framework overlay chrome
  - the duplication also kept footer spacing and alignment inconsistent across modules
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared shape across monitor, CMDB, and integration shared components:
    - two-button action cluster
    - right-aligned footer layout
    - optional loading/disabled confirm state
  - valid differences:
    - some footers need left-side extra content such as import helper actions
    - some flows prefer primary button first to preserve established interaction order
- Decision:
  - extract a framework-level footer component instead of a generic modal hook
  - keep modal visibility, form state, and business submit handlers local
  - only standardize the footer action layout and button contract
  - when a modal needs one lightweight helper action such as `test`, extend the footer contract with a constrained secondary-actions slot instead of falling back to ad hoc button arrays
  - do not force body-level test actions into the footer contract when they are semantically part of the form body or step content

  - added:
    - [src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx)

### Imperative form modal state convergence for lightweight form overlays
- Candidate:
  - repeated imperative modal state boilerplate across small form overlays:
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/object/objectTypeModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/object/objectTypeModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/metric/groupModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/metric/groupModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/groupModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/groupModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/editNode.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/editNode.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these overlays were still repeating the same non-visual control flow:
    - keep a `visible` flag
    - keep the last `showModal` config payload
    - reset and repopulate the Ant Design form when opening
    - reimplement a nearly identical imperative `showModal` bridge
  - the repeated logic was shared behavior, but not itself a user-facing visual component
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - imperative `ref.showModal(config)` contract
    - one form instance per overlay
    - derive initial form values from the incoming config
  - valid differences:
    - field sets differ by business surface
    - title/subtitle and submit handlers remain app-owned
    - some configs carry richer metadata than others
- Decision:
  - do not extract another visual modal shell on top of `OperateModal`
  - extract a shared hook for the repeated imperative modal state contract
  - keep fields, labels, submit behavior, and visible Storybook contracts in app or component layers

### Modal action footer contract expansion for single-action modal and drawer footers
- Candidate:
  - the remaining one-button modal and drawer footers that were still implemented with app-local button wrappers:
    - confirm-only modal:
      - [src/app/ops-console/(pages)/home/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-console/(pages)/home/page.tsx)
    - close-only or cancel-only overlays:
      - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/publicEnumLibraryModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/publicEnumLibraryModal.tsx)
      - [src/app/log/(pages)/event/alert/eventDetail.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/event/alert/eventDetail.tsx)
      - [src/app/log/(pages)/search/conditionList.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/search/conditionList.tsx)
      - [src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx)
- Reason:
  - after converging the two-action footers, the remaining duplication was not a new component family; it was the same modal action-bar layout with only one visible action
  - creating a second shared component just for one-button cases would split one framework contract into two overlapping APIs
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - a right-aligned action cluster inside the same modal or drawer footer region
    - one visible action button, either confirm/save or close/cancel
    - identical spacing and alignment concerns as the two-action footer
  - valid differences:
    - confirm-only first-login setup in Ops Console uses loading state and no dismiss action
    - public enum library, raw event detail, saved-condition list, and script view mode are all close-only or cancel-only informational overlays
    - these cases do not need secondary actions or extra footer content
- Decision:
  - extend the existing framework contract instead of creating a new `SingleActionModalFooter`
  - `ModalActionFooter` now accepts optional `confirmText` and `cancelText`, rendering whichever actions are provided
  - this keeps Storybook and migration guidance centered on one footer contract across one-action and two-action overlays
- Migration:
  - expanded the shared component:
    - [src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx)
  - migrated single-action consumers:

    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/publicEnumLibraryModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/list/publicEnumLibraryModal.tsx)
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/event/alert/eventDetail.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/event/alert/eventDetail.tsx)
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/search/conditionList.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/search/conditionList.tsx)
    - [src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx)

    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskDetail.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskDetail.tsx)
    - [src/app/opspilot/(pages)/studio/detail/logInfo/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/(pages)/studio/detail/logInfo/page.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these surfaces all present the same framework-level UI pattern:
    - render structured payloads or multi-line diagnostic text in a scrollable, readable preview surface
  - the differences were only container-level details:
    - drawer body versus card body
    - shorter versus taller max height
    - string payload versus object payload
  - leaving them app-local would keep drifting typography, spacing, and background treatment for the same semantic role
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - read-only preview block
    - scrollable overflow
    - preserved line breaks
    - compact mono-friendly sizing for JSON-like content

    - alarm event raw data lives inside a detail drawer
    - CMDB reporting examples and review payloads live inline inside cards/descriptions
    - OpsPilot workflow details sometimes render strings and sometimes render parsed objects
  - those differences are data-shape or layout-context concerns, not reasons for separate components

    - [src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/output/introRenderer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/output/introRenderer.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these surfaces all present the same framework-level UI pattern:
    - render a command, script, or example payload in a mono-spaced read-only block
    - preserve line breaks
    - keep long lines scrollable
    - optionally expose a copy action
  - the repeated markup had already started drifting across apps in border radius, background tone, copy affordance placement, and spacing
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - alarm integration guides show copyable shell commands and rendered webhook/script templates
    - MLOps serving guides show copyable cURL, request, response, and live result payloads
    - log integration intro pages show non-copyable example code blocks in both dark and light document contexts
  - valid differences:
    - some surfaces need copy, others are display-only
    - log docs need a darker documentation tone
    - a few inline step code blocks want tighter padding than the larger guide cards
  - those differences are visual variants, not reasons to maintain separate implementations
- Decision:
  - add a new framework component:
    - [src/components/code-snippet/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/code-snippet/index.tsx)
  - keep the contract intentionally narrow:
    - `value`
    - `copyable`
    - `copyDisabled`
    - `tone`
    - `maxHeight`
    - `className`
  - do not absorb tabs, section titles, endpoint rows, request editors, or document layout into this component
- Migration:
  - added:
    - [src/components/code-snippet/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/code-snippet/index.tsx)
  - migrated:
    - [src/app/alarm/components/k8sGuide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/components/k8sGuide/index.tsx)
    - [src/app/alarm/components/zabbixGuide/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/alarm/components/zabbixGuide/index.tsx)
    - [src/app/mlops/components/ServingGuideDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/components/ServingGuideDrawer.tsx)
    - [src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/output/introRenderer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/detail/output/introRenderer.tsx)
  - this keeps command-style content on one governed presentation primitive instead of letting each guide surface own its own code shell
- Storybook:

  - organized under:

    - copy action per row
    - disabled styling for empty values
  - the only real differences were field selection and drawer title
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - node detail drawer
    - edge relationship detail drawer
  - valid differences:

    - edge details always show relationship fields

### Content drawer footer convergence across OpsPilot knowledge drawers
- Candidate:
  - knowledge drawers that already matched the shared `ContentDrawer` shell except for one missing container-level capability: standardized footer actions
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/qaDetailDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/qaDetailDrawer.tsx)

- Reason:
  - both drawers were still on raw `Drawer` not because their body content was special, but because the shared drawer shell did not yet expose `footer`
  - footer ownership is a drawer-shell concern, so this is exactly the kind of capability that should live in the shared container instead of keeping raw `Drawer` usage around
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - right-side drawer shell
    - title/open/close/width handling
    - caller-owned inner content
    - footer actions built from local business controls
  - valid differences:
    - QA detail uses a destructive delete action plus close
    - QA edit uses confirm/cancel and an optional “confirm and add next”
    - chunk detail tab in QA detail has its own empty/loading behavior
  - none of those differences require separate overlay shells

  - extend the existing shared component:
    - [src/components/content-drawer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/content-drawer/index.tsx)

- Migration:

  - migrated:
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/qaDetailDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/qaDetailDrawer.tsx)

  - additionally converged QA detail’s no-chunk state onto:

  - both surfaces already match the same framework-level overlay pattern:
    - right-side drawer
    - caller-owned complex body content
    - shared title/open/close/width/mask behavior
    - standard footer actions aligned to the trailing edge
  - keeping them on raw `Drawer` would preserve duplicate shell ownership across app-shell account settings and alarm incident workflows even though the repo already has a Storybook-backed drawer contract
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:

    - incident update composer drawer
  - valid differences:
    - profile editor needs loading state across the whole body
    - incident update drawer uses a narrower width and disables confirm until content is valid

  - these differences belong in caller props and footer state, not in a separate drawer implementation

- Candidate:

  - valid differences:

  - this is the right layering boundary: keep domain API, remove visual primitive duplication

- Migration:

  - public API and all consumers stay unchanged

    - dataset release version in MLOps release management
  - each surface wanted the same thing:

    - some consumers display beside a title, others inside tables

  - those differences are small prop-level variations and do not justify per-page implementations
- Decision:

### Execution status badge convergence across Job, OpsPilot, and MLOps
- Candidate:
  - async execution status pills were still being implemented independently in multiple apps:
    - [src/app/job/(pages)/execution/job-record/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/execution/job-record/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/chatflow/ExecutionPreviewPanel.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/chatflow/ExecutionPreviewPanel.tsx)
    - [src/app/opspilot/(pages)/studio/detail/logInfo/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/(pages)/studio/detail/logInfo/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskHistory.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskHistory.tsx)
    - [src/app/mlops/(pages)/[algorithmType]/(main)/training/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/(pages)/[algorithmType]/(main)/training/page.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these surfaces all express the same business semantic:
    - pending or waiting work
    - running work
    - successful completion
    - failure
    - interruption or cancellation states
  - before convergence, each app maintained its own `Tag color=...` mapping and label logic, which caused style drift and made status semantics harder to govern centrally
  - this is a real cross-app business pattern, not a single-page abstraction
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - compact inline status pill in dense tables, detail headers, or execution timelines
  - shared behavior:
    - status value normalizes into a small semantic palette
    - consumer can still pass domain-specific copy through `label`
  - valid differences:
    - status keys vary slightly by domain (`completed`, `success`, `finished`, `fail`, `interrupt_requested`, `KILLED`)
    - some consumers wrap the badge with a tooltip when error detail exists
  - those differences belong inside one normalization contract instead of separate per-page `Tag` trees
- Decision:
  - add one shared business component:
    - [src/components/execution-status-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/execution-status-badge/index.tsx)
  - build it on top of:
    - [src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx)
  - do not create app-specific `JobStatusTag`, `WorkflowStatusTag`, or `TrainStatusTag` variants while the semantic contract is still shared
- Migration:
  - migrated consumers:
    - [src/app/job/(pages)/execution/job-record/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/execution/job-record/page.tsx)

    - [src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskHistory.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskHistory.tsx)
    - [src/app/mlops/(pages)/[algorithmType]/(main)/training/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/(pages)/[algorithmType]/(main)/training/page.tsx)
  - migration shape:
    - handwritten `Tag` color mappings replaced with `ExecutionStatusBadge`
    - per-domain translated labels stayed in the consumers
    - tooltip wrappers stayed local where the surrounding UX needed them
- Storybook:
  - added shared Storybook contract:
    - [src/stories/execution-status-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/execution-status-badge.stories.tsx)
  - story coverage includes:

### Shared drawer shell completion for remaining MLOps list/detail overlays
- Candidate:
  - after the earlier cross-app drawer convergence, MLOps still had two residual overlays using raw Ant Design `Drawer` even though their structure already matched the governed shared shell:
    - [src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskDrawer.tsx)

- Reason:
  - both overlays followed the same reusable framework pattern already proven in other apps:
    - right-side task/detail drawer
    - shared loading/content body
    - simple footer actions aligned to the lower-right
  - leaving these two on raw `Drawer` kept MLOps visually and structurally out of sync with the rest of the governed drawer system
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared framework pattern had already been established across CMDB, Alarm, Job, OpsPilot, and System Manager through:
    - [src/components/content-drawer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/content-drawer/index.tsx)
    - [src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/modal-action-footer/index.tsx)
  - the two MLOps drawers matched that pattern:
    - standard right placement
    - fixed width
    - no special `getContainer` choreography
    - no custom header layout requiring a forked shell
  - unlike the previously retained specialized drawers, these did not need custom positioning or multi-drawer orchestration
- Decision:
  - reuse existing shared framework components
  - do not create an MLOps-only drawer shell
- Migration:
  - migrated:
    - [src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/mlops/components/TrainTaskDrawer.tsx)

      - footer action row replaced with `ModalActionFooter`

  - migration shape:
    - local business data fetching and table logic remained untouched
    - only the shell and shared status presentation were standardized
- Storybook:
  - no new shell component was introduced, so the authoritative contracts remain:
    - [src/stories/content-drawer.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/content-drawer.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/modal-action-footer.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/modal-action-footer.stories.tsx)
  - because `ExecutionStatusBadge` gained real new normalization states during this migration, its Storybook contract was updated:

    - added governed coverage for:

      - `Archived`
  - verification:

### Semantic badge rollout for cross-app binary status pills
- Candidate:
  - several apps still rendered the same small binary status pills with raw Ant Design `Tag`, even though the visual contract had already been standardized at the primitive level:
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx)
    - [src/app/system-manager/(pages)/application/manage/menu/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/system-manager/(pages)/application/manage/menu/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/settings/namespace/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/settings/namespace/page.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/template/bulkApplyModal.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/template/bulkApplyModal.tsx)
- Reason:
  - these pages all expressed the same non-business UI pattern:
    - a compact inline positive/negative or positive/neutral state pill
  - the labels differ by domain (`normal`, `unavailable`, `enable`, `disable`, `tls enabled`, `tls disabled`, `启用`), but the display contract is the same
  - introducing another high-level generic badge here would risk a vague abstraction, while leaving raw `Tag` everywhere would preserve visual drift
- Cross-app comparison:
  - shared structure:
    - short label
    - inline placement in dense tables or preview rows
    - small semantic color treatment for positive versus negative or neutral
  - difference dimensions:
    - positive state may pair with either neutral fallback or red negative fallback
    - label copy is domain-specific
  - that makes the shared primitive sufficient, while a new business component would be premature
- Decision:
  - reuse the existing framework primitive:
    - [src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx)

  - migration shape:

    - no other tags on those pages were changed unless they belonged to this exact pattern

### Job trigger source badge convergence inside the Job domain
- Candidate:
  - the Job domain still had three separate implementations for the same trigger-source business semantic:

  - this is a stable business semantic, not a generic visual token:
    - manual trigger
    - scheduled trigger
    - API trigger
  - it already repeated across multiple surfaces inside the same product area, with slightly different color implementations and fallback labeling
  - the user explicitly allows business components as the exception to the “no duplicate components across apps” rule, so this belongs in the governed business layer rather than staying duplicated in pages
- Cross-app comparison:
  - this candidate did not show strong evidence of the same business semantic across unrelated apps
  - however, within the Job domain it had enough repeated, real usage to justify a business component:

  - this is exactly the kind of duplication that should become a business component instead of leaking page-level implementations

### Knowledge processing status badge convergence inside OpsPilot knowledge surfaces
- Candidate:
  - OpsPilot knowledge surfaces were still rendering the same processing-state semantic through repeated page-local `Tag` mappings:
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/tableColumns.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/tableColumns.tsx) for document training status
    - [src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/tableColumns.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/tableColumns.tsx) for QA pair generation status
    - [src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/graph/edit/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/graph/edit/page.tsx) for selected-document processing status
- Reason:
  - these surfaces all express the same knowledge-domain business semantic:
    - pending
    - processing or generating
    - completed
    - failed
  - the labels differ by context, and one surface wraps failed states with an error tooltip, but the underlying status-pill contract is the same
  - this is not a generic global status badge, because it belongs specifically to the knowledge ingestion and QA-generation workflow
- Cross-app comparison:
  - there is not yet evidence that unrelated apps share the same exact processing-state vocabulary
  - within the OpsPilot knowledge workflow, though, the repetition is real and stable across tables and selection panels
  - the appropriate boundary is therefore a knowledge-scoped business component

### Custom-reporting runtime status badge convergence inside CMDB task and batch workflows
- Candidate:
  - CMDB custom-reporting runtime statuses were still rendered through page-local `Tag` color mappings in three places:
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskTable.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskTable.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskDetail.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskDetail.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/batchReviewDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/batchReviewDrawer.tsx)
- Reason:
  - the domain actually contains two related but distinct business taxonomies:
    - task runtime statuses: `receiving / pending_review / no_report`
    - batch runtime statuses: `running / success / failed`
  - both were duplicated locally, and the batch side was already visually inconsistent:
    - task detail mapped batch status by value
    - batch review drawer rendered every batch status as `processing`
  - these are not global framework statuses; they belong specifically to CMDB custom-reporting ingestion workflows
- Cross-app comparison:
  - there is not yet evidence that unrelated apps share the task-level `receiving / pending_review / no_report` vocabulary
  - within CMDB custom reporting, though, task and batch statuses are repeated enough to justify a governed business component
  - because the two vocabularies are different, they were intentionally not flattened into a single untyped generic `status` prop
- Decision:
  - add a CMDB business component:
    - [src/components/custom-reporting-runtime-status-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/custom-reporting-runtime-status-badge/index.tsx)
  - build it on top of:
    - [src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx)
  - use a typed boundary:
    - `kind="task"` for task runtime statuses
    - `kind="batch"` for batch runtime statuses
  - keep label ownership with callers so local copy remains explicit while the visual contract converges
- Migration:
  - migrated consumers:
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskTable.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskTable.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskDetail.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/taskDetail.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/batchReviewDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/batchReviewDrawer.tsx)
  - migration shape:
    - task list status now renders the shared task-status branch
    - batch status in task detail now renders the shared batch-status branch
    - batch review drawer now uses the same batch-status mapping instead of always rendering `processing`
- Storybook:
  - added the business-component contract:
    - [src/stories/custom-reporting-runtime-status-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/custom-reporting-runtime-status-badge.stories.tsx)
  - covered:
    - `TaskReceiving`
    - `TaskPendingReview`
    - `TaskNoReport`
    - `BatchRunning`
    - `BatchSuccess`
    - `BatchFailed`
  - verification:
    - scoped `eslint` passed under Node `24.18.0` for the new component, story, and all three migrated CMDB consumers

### Semantic-badge rollout for remaining framework-level binary result pills

    - caller-owned label text

    - [src/app/node-manager/hooks/configuration.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/hooks/configuration.tsx)

  - the unified-filter binding panel still contained page-local `Tag` pills for two framework-level chip treatments:

    - disabled-state pill in neutral gray

- Reason:
  - this slice did not justify a new business component because the semantics are local to the panel
  - but keeping raw `Tag` branches here would leave the page visually outside the shared badge system we already established through `SemanticBadge`
  - because the primitive already supports caller-owned labels and neutral/primary/success palettes, this is a direct reuse case
- Cross-app comparison:
  - blue, success, and neutral chip treatments are already covered through the growing `SemanticBadge` rollout across Job, System Manager, Node Manager, CMDB, and Ops Analysis
  - this panel is a remaining local holdout rather than a new taxonomy
- Decision:
  - reuse the existing framework component:
    - [src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/semantic-badge/index.tsx)
  - do not create a unified-filter-specific wrapper
- Migration:
  - migrated consumer:
    - [src/app/ops-analysis/components/unifiedFilter/filterBindingPanel.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/ops-analysis/components/unifiedFilter/filterBindingPanel.tsx)
  - migration shape:
    - parameter type pill now renders through `SemanticBadge`
    - disabled pill now renders through `SemanticBadge`
    - labels remain caller-owned
- Storybook:
  - existing source of truth remains:
    - [src/stories/semantic-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/semantic-badge.stories.tsx)
  - no new story was required in this slice because primary, success, and neutral badge states were already covered by the current framework stories
  - verification:
    - scoped `eslint` passed under Node `24.18.0` for the migrated Ops Analysis consumer

### Integration-catalog-card metadata badge convergence across Log and Monitor
- Candidate:

- Reason:
  - the card container is already shared through `IntegrationCatalogCard`, but the metadata chips inside it were still drifting through page-local `Tag` usage
  - both apps express the same UI pattern:
    - neutral metadata pill for category or object family
    - optional provenance pill for custom/self-built integrations
  - this is a strong reuse case for existing shared badges rather than another integration-specific wrapper
- Cross-app comparison:
  - Log uses the metadata pill to show `display_category`
  - Monitor uses the metadata pill to show parent object or collect type and conditionally adds a self-built provenance pill
  - the copy differs, but the visual grammar is aligned and already covered by `SemanticBadge` plus `SourceOriginBadge`

  - do not create an `integration-category-tag` or similar duplicate wrapper

    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx)
  - migration shape:
    - neutral category/object pills now render through `SemanticBadge`
    - monitor self-built metadata now renders through `SourceOriginBadge`
    - `IntegrationCatalogCard` stays the shared shell while metadata rendering becomes governed and consistent

### Execution-status-badge rollout for remaining execution-state pills

### Monitor reporting-status convergence into a domain business badge
- Candidate:
  - Monitor still had repeated `normal` / `online` / unavailable reporting-state pills split across page-local `SemanticBadge` branches and local `Tag` rendering:
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/asset/templateConfigDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/asset/templateConfigDrawer.tsx)
- Reason:
  - both consumers express the same Monitor-domain business semantics: whether a reporting source is healthy (`normal` / `online`) or unavailable
  - this is more specific than a framework badge, because the label vocabulary and status normalization belong to Monitor integrations rather than the whole design system
  - keeping one page on `SemanticBadge` branches and another on raw `Tag` would leave a stable business concept without a Storybook-backed contract
- Cross-app comparison:
  - I checked other apps for the same stable semantic boundary and did not find an equally aligned cross-app business concept
  - the repetition is strong inside Monitor across different modules (`view` and `integration asset`) but not broad enough to justify a framework-wide generic availability badge yet
  - so the right boundary here is a Monitor business component, not another generic shared primitive
- Decision:
  - extracted a Monitor-scoped business component:
    - [src/components/monitor-reporting-status-badge/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/monitor-reporting-status-badge/index.tsx)
  - implemented status normalization:
    - `normal` / `online` -> healthy badge
    - everything else -> unavailable badge
- Migration:
  - migrated consumers:
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx)
    - [src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/asset/templateConfigDrawer.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/asset/templateConfigDrawer.tsx)
  - migration shape:
    - replaced inline status palette logic in `viewList`
    - replaced raw reporting-status `Tag` in `templateConfigDrawer`
    - intentionally left status-tinted plugin-name chips in asset list alone because they represent a different interaction pattern
- Storybook:
  - added the business contract:
    - [src/stories/monitor-reporting-status-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/monitor-reporting-status-badge.stories.tsx)
  - explicit coverage includes:
    - `normal`
    - `online`
    - unavailable fallbacks
  - verification:
    - scoped `eslint` passed under Node `24.18.0` for the new business component, story, and both migrated Monitor consumers

### TagCapsuleGroup promotion from CMDB-local helper to shared framework component
- Candidate:
  - a stable framework pattern already existed in CMDB for rendering string arrays as compact colored capsules with overflow handling, but it was trapped under an app-local path while other apps were still hand-rolling raw `Tag` lists:
    - existing local implementation:
      - [src/app/cmdb/components/tag-capsule-group/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/components/tag-capsule-group/index.tsx)
    - repeated non-CMDB consumers:
      - [src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx)
      - [src/app/job/(pages)/template/playbook-library/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/template/playbook-library/page.tsx)
- Reason:
  - this is not a domain-specific badge, it is a reusable framework display pattern:
    - normalize unknown or string-array values
    - render compact colored capsules
    - collapse overflow into `+N`
    - expose the full set through tooltip
  - leaving that implementation under `app/cmdb` while Job hand-renders ad hoc `Tag` arrays creates duplicated UI vocabulary across apps
  - this is exactly the kind of cross-app pattern that should move into the shared component layer and be governed by Storybook
- Cross-app comparison:
  - CMDB already used the component in multiple surfaces:
    - [src/app/cmdb/utils/common.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/utils/common.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetData/detail/baseInfo/list.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetData/detail/baseInfo/list.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetSearch/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetSearch/page.tsx)
  - Job script and playbook libraries were repeating the same “array of organization names rendered as small tags” pattern, but without overflow governance
  - that makes this a strong framework component promotion rather than another app-specific wrapper
- Decision:
  - promoted the CMDB-local helper into a shared framework component:
    - [src/components/tag-capsule-group/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/tag-capsule-group/index.tsx)
  - removed the old app-local implementation after migrating known imports
- Migration:
  - migrated CMDB consumers to the shared import:
    - [src/app/cmdb/utils/common.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/utils/common.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetData/detail/baseInfo/list.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetData/detail/baseInfo/list.tsx)
    - [src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetSearch/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetSearch/page.tsx)
  - migrated confirmed non-CMDB consumers:
    - [src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx)

### NodeManagerRuntimeStatusBadge for repeated Node Manager runtime-state pills
- Candidate:

    - normal
    - error
    - stopped / installation failed
    - installing
    - unknown / not started
  - both pages were already reading from the same domain status mapping but still formatting the visible pill independently
  - this is a good business-component boundary: stable domain semantics, repeated across views, and likely to keep expanding with future Node Manager surfaces

    - replaced collector detail running-status tag with the same shared badge contract
    - kept executor-name tags, OS tags, and install-guidance status tags local for now because they either encode different object identity or use richer icon-first layouts

### OpspilotConfigSeverityBadge for shared OpsPilot config-analysis severity semantics
- Candidate:

    - `high`
    - `medium`
    - `low`
    - `warning`
    - `info`
    - fallback `unknown`
  - before this slice, `DiffReportCard` used Ant Design `Tag` colors while `ConfigAnalysisReportCard` used handwritten Tailwind badge classes, which split the same severity semantics across two rendering systems
  - the repeated semantics and differing implementations make this a strong candidate for a shared business component with Storybook coverage
- Cross-app comparison:
  - `DiffReportCard` repeated the same severity badge twice within one workflow:
    - item list rows
    - modal title
  - `ConfigAnalysisReportCard` repeated the same severity grammar across section headers
  - both consumers share the same `OpsPilot` report domain and severity codes, so a business-level severity badge is a better fit than reusing a generic framework pill directly in each page

  - migration shape:
    - replaced progress-table and installer-step status tags with the shared business badge

    - kept OS and other identity tags local because they do not belong to the runtime-status contract

### SelectableTagFilterGroup for repeated Node Manager list-filter tags
- Candidate:
  - Node Manager list pages repeated the same clickable tag-filter interaction with identical Ant `Tag` styling and toggle behavior:
    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/controller/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/controller/page.tsx)

- Reason:
  - both pages expose compact list filters above `EntityList` cards through the same UI grammar:
    - inactive filter tag
    - selected blue filter tag
    - click-to-toggle behavior
  - the current implementations duplicated the same Tailwind classes and tag color logic, while only differing in the option data source:
    - controller page uses a string array
    - collector page uses option objects
  - this is a framework interaction pattern, not a business-specific badge
- Cross-app comparison:
  - controller filters use OS and architecture-derived strings collected from API results
  - collector filters use OS, kind, and architecture options sourced from node-manager enums
  - despite different data origins, both consumers share the same presentational contract and toggle interaction, which is enough to justify a shared framework component
- Decision:
  - introduced a framework component:
    - [src/components/selectable-tag-filter-group/index.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/components/selectable-tag-filter-group/index.tsx)

    - [src/app/node-manager/(pages)/collector/page.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/app/node-manager/(pages)/collector/page.tsx)
  - migration shape:
    - replaced repeated inline tag rendering loops with a shared selectable filter group
    - kept each page's existing state ownership and fetch behavior

### Storybook taxonomy convergence for framework data-display primitives
- Candidate:
  - several already-governed framework stories in the same display family were still split across two Storybook paths:
    - `Framework/Data Display/*`
    - `Framework/DataDisplay/*`
- Reason:
  - this was not a component-API problem, but it was a real contract-governance problem:
    - the same framework family no longer lived under one stable discovery path
    - recently migrated primitives such as `CodeSnippet`, `CopyableDetailList`, `StructuredDataPreview`, `HttpMethodBadge`, and `TagCapsuleGroup` looked like they belonged to a parallel subtree instead of the main `DataDisplay` family
  - Storybook is the source of truth, so taxonomy drift weakens the usefulness of otherwise-correct shared components
- Cross-app comparison:
  - these stories all represent framework-level display primitives reused across Alarm, Monitor, MLOps, OpsPilot, and other domains
  - they belong beside the existing `Framework/DataDisplay/*` stories such as:
    - `SemanticBadge`
    - `EllipsisWithTooltip`
    - `EntityList`
    - `VersionBadge`
  - this is a classification convergence task, not a new component extraction
- Decision:
  - keep one canonical framework family path:
    - `Framework/DataDisplay/*`
  - do not duplicate stories under both titles
- Migration:
  - retitled stories:
    - [src/stories/code-snippet.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/code-snippet.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/copyable-detail-list.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/copyable-detail-list.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/structured-data-preview.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/structured-data-preview.stories.tsx)
    - [src/stories/http-method-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/http-method-badge.stories.tsx)

### Storybook taxonomy convergence for NodeManager and OpsPilot business families
- Candidate:
  - a few existing business stories were still filed under inconsistent family names even though their sibling stories already established the canonical Storybook subtrees:
    - `Business/Node Manager/*`
    - `Business/OpsPilot/Opspilot*`
- Reason:
  - this is another contract-center issue rather than a component-API issue:
    - the NodeManager family was split between `NodeManager` and `Node Manager`
    - the OpsPilot family mixed `OpsPilot` with `Opspilot*` leaf names
  - that drift makes related business components harder to scan and weakens Storybook as the stable taxonomy for shared business surfaces
- Cross-app comparison:
  - the canonical sibling titles already present in Storybook use:
    - `Business/NodeManager/*`
    - `Business/OpsPilot/OpsPilot*`
  - the outliers belonged to those same business families:
    - runtime-status and operating-system badges for NodeManager
    - availability and config-severity badges for OpsPilot
- Decision:
  - keep one canonical business subtree per family:
    - `Business/NodeManager/*`
    - `Business/OpsPilot/OpsPilot*`
  - do not duplicate stories under both spellings
- Migration:
  - retitled stories:
    - [src/stories/node-manager-runtime-status-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/node-manager-runtime-status-badge.stories.tsx)

    - [src/stories/opspilot-config-severity-badge.stories.tsx](/Users/qiu/projects/bk-lite/.worktrees/web-component-governance/web/src/stories/opspilot-config-severity-badge.stories.tsx)
- Storybook:

### Storybook taxonomy convergence for CMDB CustomReporting business stories
- Candidate:
  - the CustomReporting business stories were split between two Storybook paths:
    - `Business/CMDB/CustomReporting/*`
    - `Business/CMDB/CustomReportingModeBadge`
- Reason:
  - this was a Storybook contract-governance inconsistency, not a component-API gap:
    - `CustomReportingReviewStatusBadge` and `CustomReportingRuntimeStatusBadge` already lived under a canonical `CustomReporting` business subtree
    - `CustomReportingModeBadge` was still filed as a one-off root entry, which made the family look partially standardized
  - since Storybook is the single source of truth for shared components, this drift made the CMDB CustomReporting contract harder to discover as one governed business domain
- Cross-app comparison:
  - the three badges all describe the same CMDB CustomReporting workflow surface:
    - mode selection
    - runtime processing status
    - cleanup review status
  - the component itself is already shared and reused in multiple CMDB surfaces such as:
