## 2026-07-03 Migrated OpsPilot provider page header to the shared PageHeaderShell contract

- candidate component
  - existing framework component:
    - `src/components/page-header-shell/index.tsx`
  - page implementation reviewed:
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/provider/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - the provider page still rendered its title, description, search box, refresh action, and primary add action through page-local flex markup
  - that pattern overlaps with the cross-app page-scaffold vocabulary already used by alarm integration overview, job recent-jobs workspace, monitor detail pages, and log strategy detail pages
- cross-app comparison
  - difference analysis:
    - the provider page needs a title/subtitle block on the left and an action cluster on the right
    - those differences are content-only and already fit the `PageHeaderShell` contract through `title`, `subtitle`, and `actions`
    - unlike job-specific or monitor-specific wrappers, this page did not need a new business header wrapper because its behavior is plain page-scaffold composition
- governance decision
  - reuse `PageHeaderShell` directly
  - keep provider-specific search, refresh, and add interactions as action-slot content
  - do not introduce an OpsPilot-only header wrapper for this page
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/provider/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced manual title/action layout with `PageHeaderShell`
    - preserved vendor search, refresh, add modal trigger, and grid behavior
- Storybook plan
  - no new story required because `PageHeaderShell` is already governed in Storybook under `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
  - continue migrating page-local title/action bars onto existing page-scaffold contracts when their differences are content-only

## 2026-07-03 Extracted ToolbarSplitShell for cross-app split toolbar rows

- candidate component
  - new framework component:
    - `src/components/toolbar-split-shell/index.tsx`
  - page implementations reviewed:
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - both pages still assembled the same `nav-box` pattern locally:
    - left-side filter or mode switcher block
    - right-side search and action cluster
  - that row is no longer an app-specific accident; it is a repeated framework layout contract that sits between single-column `FilterToolbar` and title-driven `PageHeaderShell`
- cross-app comparison
  - difference analysis:
    - OpsPilot knowledge documents uses source-type switching on the left and search, refresh, add, and batch actions on the right
    - CMDB asset manage uses keyword search on the left and add, settings, and layout-management actions on the right
    - the differences are slot content only; the responsive two-zone toolbar structure is the shared pattern
- governance decision
  - extract `ToolbarSplitShell` as a framework component instead of keeping duplicated page-local `nav-box` markup
  - keep all business controls, permissions, counts, and action handlers page-owned through `leading` and `trailing` slots
  - do not stretch `FilterToolbar` to cover this contract because the shared need here is two-zone layout governance, not a denser one-zone action strip
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced page-local split toolbar wrappers with `ToolbarSplitShell`
    - normalized spacing by removing ad hoc margin chaining from child buttons and inputs
    - preserved document-source switching, search, refresh, add, batch actions, model search, config actions, and layout-management behavior
- Storybook plan
  - added a framework story:
    - `src/stories/toolbar-split-shell.stories.tsx`
  - Storybook now covers:
    - segmented-switching plus trailing search/actions composition
    - left-search plus trailing management actions composition

  - that pattern matches the same panel-level heading contract already reused for monitor, job, log, and other OpsPilot title rows with trailing controls

  - difference analysis:
    - this panel header needs a left title and an optional trailing segmented result switcher
    - those differences are content-only and fit `PageHeaderShell` through `title`, `actions`, and lightweight row-class overrides
    - the result cards, graph view, node detail drawer, and tab selection state remain page-specific content and should stay local
- governance decision
  - reuse `PageHeaderShell` directly inside the result panel
  - keep the segmented result switcher as page-owned trailing action content
  - do not introduce a knowledge-testing-specific panel-header wrapper
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/testing/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced the manual title/segmented row with `PageHeaderShell`
    - preserved result-tab switching behavior, result rendering behavior, graph-detail behavior, and drawer interactions

  - no new story required because `PageHeaderShell` is already governed in Storybook under `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
  - continue converging panel-level title/action rows onto the shared scaffold contract when differences are only slot content

  - Storybook for that business shell was also still demonstrating those search slots through raw `Input` / `Input.Search` markup instead of the shared toolbar contract

  - reuse `SearchActionBar` directly for the integration catalog search slot in both apps

  - do not create an integration-catalog-specific search wrapper because the shared framework toolbar already covers the proven pattern

    - replaced the monitor catalog page-local search container with `SearchActionBar` while preserving its hidden import/export controls
    - aligned Storybook business-shell stories to the same shared search toolbar contract

  - no new story file required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchActionBar`
    - `Business/Integrations/Catalog/IntegrationCatalogWorkspaceShell`
  - future changes to integration-catalog search structure should update these shared stories instead of reintroducing page-local search wrappers

    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/toolbar-split-shell/index.tsx`
  - migration targets reviewed:
    - `src/components/cmdb-subscription-drawer/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/job-workspace-panel.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SearchActionBar` is already a cross-app framework primitive, but its internal layout still used handwritten search-plus-actions flex markup and ad hoc spacing utilities
  - this kept one of the highest-frequency toolbar patterns outside the governed split-toolbar contract, which made Storybook examples and downstream pages drift on spacing and wrap behavior
  - the CMDB subscription drawer and workspace-panel story still expressed the same pattern locally, so they were good migration checkpoints after the framework-level refactor
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - left-side search input or search-combination control
    - optional right-side primary and secondary actions
    - responsive wrapping under narrow widths
  - difference analysis:
    - some consumers need plain `Input.Search`, while others use `SearchCombination`
    - search width varies by page
    - action density varies between one CTA and compact button groups
    - these differences are configuration-only and fit `ToolbarSplitShell` plus existing `SearchActionBar` props without creating a new component family
- governance decision
  - keep `SearchActionBar` as the public framework API for search-first toolbars
  - reuse `ToolbarSplitShell` internally so search bars and split toolbars share one layout contract
  - migrate local one-off implementations only when they match the proven search-plus-actions pattern; do not abstract drawer-specific or panel-specific business behaviors
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/cmdb-subscription-drawer/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/search-action-bar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/job-workspace-panel.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced `SearchActionBar`'s local flex row with `ToolbarSplitShell`
    - normalized action spacing in Storybook from margin utilities to gap-based grouping
    - replaced the CMDB subscription drawer's handwritten search/action row with the shared `SearchActionBar`
    - aligned the workspace-panel list example to `ToolbarSplitShell` so Storybook no longer demonstrates a duplicate split-toolbar implementation
- Storybook plan
  - no new story file required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/ToolbarSplitShell`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchActionBar`
    - `Framework/Layout/WorkspacePanel`
  - future search-action toolbar behavior changes should update the shared component stories first, and business examples should consume those contracts instead of reintroducing local flex rows

## 2026-07-03 Migrated OpsAnalysis ViewToolbar internals to the shared PageHeaderShell contract

- candidate component
  - existing framework component:
    - `src/components/page-header-shell/index.tsx`
  - shared business component reviewed:
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/index.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `ViewToolbar` is already the shared business toolbar used by the architecture, dashboard, and topology views, but its title-description-actions layout was still implemented locally
  - that made OpsAnalysis maintain one more header contract outside the governed framework layer even though the structure had already converged with `PageHeaderShell`
  - because the dashboard variant also relies on custom border, spacing, and shadow treatment, this review also validated whether the framework shell needed a small capability extension instead of another business-only fork
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - left-side title and description block
    - optional inline built-in badge beside the title
    - right-side action cluster with compact icon buttons or edit-action groups
  - comparison evidence:
    - `ViewToolbar` serves three OpsAnalysis surfaces:
      - `src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/architecture/components/toolbar.tsx`
      - `src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/dashBoard/components/dashboardToolbar.tsx`
      - `src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/view/topology/components/toolbar.tsx`
    - `PageHeaderShell` already governs the same structural contract across Monitor, CMDB, Integration, and OpsPilot headers
  - difference analysis:
    - OpsAnalysis needs elevated-card styling, compact action alignment, and optional description-only rendering
    - those differences are presentational and fit the shared shell through `className`, `style`, class hooks, and conditional title-row rendering
    - the built-in badge remains business-specific content owned by `ViewToolbar`
- governance decision
  - keep `ViewToolbar` as the business-level public API for OpsAnalysis pages
  - migrate its internal structure onto `PageHeaderShell` instead of inventing a second header primitive
  - extend `PageHeaderShell` with a generic `style` prop and safer conditional title rendering because those capabilities are framework-valid and reusable beyond OpsAnalysis
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/page-header-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/page-header-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced the handwritten ViewToolbar header markup with `PageHeaderShell`
    - preserved the existing ViewToolbar API and the inline built-in badge behavior
    - added `style` support to `PageHeaderShell` so elevated card headers can stay inside the shared contract
    - updated Storybook with a styled header-shell example covering the elevated-toolbar variant
- Storybook plan
  - no new business story file required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/ViewToolbar/Toolbar`
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/ViewToolbar/FamilyOverview`
  - future OpsAnalysis toolbar layout changes should update `PageHeaderShell` or the existing ViewToolbar stories instead of reintroducing local header markup

## 2026-07-03 Migrated EventStrategyPanel header internals to the shared PageHeaderShell contract

- candidate component
  - existing framework component:
    - `src/components/page-header-shell/index.tsx`
  - shared business component reviewed:
    - `src/components/event-strategy-panel/index.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `EventStrategyPanel` is reused across monitor and log strategy-detail workflows, but its panel header still hand-assembled the title/extra row with local flex markup
  - this left one more cross-app business panel header outside the governed framework header contract even though the repeated structure had already stabilized
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - title-only side panels
    - title plus right-side selector/actions
    - extra-only header rows when the panel needs a right-aligned control without a title
  - comparison evidence:
    - monitor strategy pages:
      - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/variablesTable.tsx`
      - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/metricPreview.tsx`

    - log strategy pages:

      - `src/stories/events-family.stories.tsx`
  - difference analysis:
    - some panels have only a title, some add a selector or action cluster, and some expose only a right-aligned control
    - those differences are slot-level and already fit `PageHeaderShell` after its conditional title rendering update
    - shadow, sticky positioning, and body content remain owned by `EventStrategyPanel`
- governance decision
  - keep `EventStrategyPanel` as the business panel API for event-strategy workflows
  - migrate its internal header row onto `PageHeaderShell`
  - add an extra-only story variant so Storybook explicitly captures the titleless header state instead of leaving it implicit in app code
- migration
  - updated:

    - `src/stories/event-strategy-panel.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced the handwritten panel header row with `PageHeaderShell`
    - preserved `title`, `extra`, `stickyTop`, `withShadow`, and body API behavior
    - added Storybook coverage for the extra-only header variant used by threshold configuration flows
- Storybook plan
  - no new story family required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
    - `Business/Events/Strategy/EventStrategyPanel`
    - `Business/Events/FamilyOverview`
  - future event-strategy panel header changes should update the shared shell contract or this existing business story instead of reintroducing local flex-row markup

## 2026-07-03 Migrated SectionHeader internals to the shared PageHeaderShell contract

- candidate component
  - existing framework components:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/page-header-shell/index.tsx`
  - business component reviewed:
    - `src/components/k8s-collector-install/index.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SectionHeader` already acts as a framework-level section-title primitive across system manager, job, monitor integration guides, alarm integration guides, ops-analysis sidebar, and integration configuration shells
  - despite that role, it still maintained local title/icon/description markup and could not express right-side actions, which forced components like `K8sCollectorInstall` to wrap it in another handwritten header row
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - icon plus title
    - optional description
    - increasingly common trailing actions such as guide links, helper buttons, or compact selectors
  - comparison evidence:
    - section-title usage spans:
      - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/authSettings.tsx`
      - `src/app/job/(pages)/home/page.tsx`
      - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/accessGuide/index.tsx`
      - `src/components/integration-k8s-access-config-shell/index.tsx`
      - `src/components/alarm-integration-guides/section-panel.tsx`
      - `src/components/k8s-collector-install/index.tsx`
    - the K8s collector install step already needed a title row plus a trailing “common issues” action
  - difference analysis:
    - consumers vary by typography variant (`default`, `panel`, `compact`), icon source, and whether they need trailing actions
    - those differences are configuration-only and fit `PageHeaderShell` through leading, subtitle, actions, and class hooks
    - section-local cards, form layouts, and panel shells remain owned by the higher-level components
- governance decision
  - keep `SectionHeader` as the framework-level section-title API
  - migrate its internals to `PageHeaderShell` instead of carrying a second standalone header renderer
  - extend `SectionHeader` with an `actions` slot because trailing actions are now a proven cross-surface need, not a one-off K8s exception
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/k8s-collector-install/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/section-header.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced `SectionHeader`'s local markup with `PageHeaderShell`
    - preserved existing icon, description, variant, and className behavior
    - added `actions` and `actionsClassName` support to the framework component
    - replaced the K8s collector install page-local title/action wrapper with the shared `SectionHeader` contract
- Storybook plan
  - no new story family required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
    - `Framework/Content/SectionHeader`
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/CollectorInstall`
  - future section-title rows with trailing actions should update `SectionHeader` stories first instead of wrapping the component in handwritten justify-between containers

## 2026-07-03 Expanded SelectionPreviewLayout and migrated KnowledgeDocumentSelectorDrawer onto the shared selection contract

- candidate component
  - existing framework component:
    - `src/components/selection-preview-layout/index.tsx`
  - shared business component reviewed:
    - `src/components/knowledge-document-selector-drawer/index.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SelectionPreviewLayout` already exists in Storybook as the shared dual-pane selection contract, but `KnowledgeDocumentSelectorDrawer` still maintained its own separate left-table plus right-selected-preview implementation
  - that meant one of the clearest selection workflows in OpsPilot knowledge flows was bypassing the existing framework pattern instead of driving it
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - left-side searchable or pageable selector surface
    - right-side selected-items preview with count, clear action, and per-item removal
    - optional empty state and optional footer guidance
  - comparison evidence:
    - framework examples already live in:
      - `src/stories/selection-preview-layout.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/operate-form-modal.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/operate-form-drawer.stories.tsx`
    - business usage spans OpsPilot document flows through:
      - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/modify/qaPairForm.tsx`
      - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/graph/edit/page.tsx`
      - `src/components/knowledge-document-selector-drawer/index.tsx`
  - difference analysis:
    - the knowledge drawer needs richer preview labels, a domain-specific preview title, optional footer hint text, and hidden clear action when no items are selected
    - those differences are still structural selection-layout concerns and fit the shared component through slot props and class hooks
    - document badges, processing status, and tabbed table content remain business-owned by the knowledge drawer
- governance decision
  - keep `SelectionPreviewLayout` as the framework-level contract for dual-pane selection flows
  - extend it with generic preview-title, footer, clear-label, and class-hook capabilities rather than minting a knowledge-specific split-layout component
  - migrate `KnowledgeDocumentSelectorDrawer` onto that shared contract so the real app flow now validates the same structure Storybook advertises
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/selection-preview-layout/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/knowledge-document-selector-drawer/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/selection-preview-layout.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - added reusable preview customization props to `SelectionPreviewLayout`
    - replaced the knowledge drawer's handwritten two-column selection preview implementation with `SelectionPreviewLayout`
    - preserved business-specific document badges, meta rendering, confirm hint, tabbed source selection, and table pagination behavior
- Storybook plan
  - no new story family required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Selection/SelectionPreviewLayout`
    - `Business/Knowledge/Documents/KnowledgeDocumentSelectorDrawer`
  - future split-selection experiences should update `SelectionPreviewLayout` stories first instead of reintroducing local selected-preview scaffolds inside drawers or modals

  - `KnowledgeQADetailDrawer` is already a shared business drawer with Storybook coverage, but its question, answer, and chunk-metadata headings were still handwritten inside the component

    - `Framework/Selection/SelectionPreviewLayout`
    - `Framework/Feedback/ContentFormDrawer`

  - future knowledge detail and chunk-preview drawer changes should update these governed stories instead of reintroducing local section headers or split-preview scaffolds

## 2026-07-03 Migrated SourceMenuTree and PanelShell stories onto the shared SectionHeader contract

- candidate component
  - existing framework components:
    - `src/components/panel-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - shared business component reviewed:
    - `src/components/system-manager-application-menu/source-menu-tree.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SourceMenuTree` is a real shared business component used by the application-menu configuration page and multiple Storybook overviews, but its panel header still rendered a local `h3`
  - at the same time, `PanelShell` stories were still teaching handwritten title blocks in their own header slot examples, which undermined the shared `SectionHeader` contract we had just strengthened
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - `PanelShell` with a border-separated header band
    - compact section title inside the header slot
    - optional description for guide-style panels
  - comparison evidence:
    - business usage:
      - `src/app/system-manager/(pages)/application/manage/menu/config/page.tsx`
      - `src/components/system-manager-application-menu/source-menu-tree.tsx`

      - `src/stories/system-manager-application-menu-family.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/system-manager-family.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/panel-shell.stories.tsx`
  - difference analysis:
    - some panel headers need only a compact title
    - guide-style panels need title plus description
    - those differences are already covered by `SectionHeader` through title, description, and class overrides
    - tree rendering, selection state, and panel framing remain owned by `SourceMenuTree` and `PanelShell`
- governance decision
  - keep `SourceMenuTree` as the business API and `PanelShell` as the framing primitive
  - standardize the header content itself on `SectionHeader` instead of ad hoc `h3` or custom title markup
  - update framework stories so Storybook demonstrates the governed composition rather than a local exception
- migration
  - updated:

    - `src/stories/panel-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced SourceMenuTree's handwritten panel header with `SectionHeader`
    - replaced PanelShell story header examples with `SectionHeader`
    - preserved existing body, footer, and panel shell APIs
- Storybook plan
  - no new story family required because the source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Content/SectionHeader`
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PanelShell`
    - `Business/SystemManager/ApplicationMenu/SystemManagerSourceMenuTree`
  - future PanelShell header examples should compose `SectionHeader` unless a proven cross-app contract requires a different shared header primitive

## 2026-07-03 Converged high-level OpsPilot, SystemManager, and K8s family overview headings onto SectionHeader

- candidate component
  - existing framework component:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - Storybook overview files reviewed:
    - `src/stories/opspilot-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/system-manager-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-family.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - these three stories sit one level above the narrower business-family overviews and act as portfolio-level examples for how large feature areas are composed
  - each file still repeated local section title, title-plus-description, and nested card heading markup, which meant the broadest Storybook navigation layers were still teaching a parallel header vocabulary
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - section header inside a framed overview panel
    - optional descriptive copy that explains the governed business contract
    - nested compact card titles inside secondary panels
  - comparison evidence:
    - OpsPilot reused the same local title-description shell across workspace, status, approval, routing, and empty-state sections
    - SystemManager reused the same shell across audit, menu governance, organization assignment, and user-profile surfaces, plus repeated title-description cards for modal entry points
    - K8s reused the same shell across multi-step journey sections and nested install/config cards
  - difference analysis:
    - some descriptions are one paragraph and some are two-paragraph explanatory blocks
    - nested cards use medium-weight compact titles while outer sections use semibold titles
    - these are presentation differences only and already fit `SectionHeader` through `description`, `titleClassName`, and spacing overrides
- governance decision
  - reuse `SectionHeader` for both top-level overview sections and nested compact card headings in these stories
  - do not introduce area-specific header wrappers for OpsPilot, SystemManager, or K8s because the pattern is cross-domain and already well served by the framework primitive

## 2026-07-04 Extracted ToolEditorShell for repeated OpsPilot instance-editor layouts

- candidate component
  - new shared business component:
    - `src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-editor-shell.tsx`
  - repeated app-local implementations reviewed:
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mysqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/postgresToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/redisToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/oracleToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mssqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/elasticsearchToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/jenkinsToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/kubernetesToolEditor.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - all eight tool editors repeated the same instance-management shell: left instance sidebar, right detail panel, top-right connection-status tag, and empty-state fallback
  - the only real variation lived in the form fields and the sidebar description text, which means the repeated layout had already become a stable business-family contract rather than one-off page composition
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - `ToolInstanceSidebar` on the left
    - bordered detail panel on the right
    - detail title row with connection status tag
    - empty-state surface when no instance is selected
  - comparison evidence:
    - MySQL, Postgres, Oracle, MSSQL, and Elasticsearch all repeated nearly identical host/credential editor framing
    - Redis and Kubernetes used different fields but the same shell
    - Jenkins used a smaller field set but still the same shell contract
  - difference analysis:
    - Kubernetes requires `selectedVariant="strong"` in the sidebar
    - each editor owns different form controls and test action behavior
    - these differences belong inside the form body and sidebar item mapping, not in the surrounding layout shell
- governance decision
  - extract `ToolEditorShell` as the shared business shell for OpsPilot tool-instance editors
  - keep `ToolInstanceSidebar`, `ToolConnectionStatusTag`, and `ToolEditorEmptyState` as lower-level leaf primitives, but stop reassembling them manually in every tool editor
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/index.ts`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mysqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/postgresToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/redisToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/oracleToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mssqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/elasticsearchToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/jenkinsToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/kubernetesToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tool-editor-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/opspilot-tool-editor-family.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - extracted the repeated shell into `ToolEditorShell`
    - migrated all eight editors to pass sidebar props, detail title/status, and form-body children into the shared shell
    - updated Storybook family overview to demonstrate the new shared shell instead of manually composing the leaf primitives
- Storybook plan
  - new source-of-truth entry:
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/ToolEditorShell`
  - existing family validation remains:
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/InstanceSidebar`
  - future tool editors should compose `ToolEditorShell` first and only add new leaf primitives when the shell contract itself genuinely changes

## 2026-07-04 Extracted ToolEditorField for repeated OpsPilot labeled control blocks

- candidate component
  - new shared business component:
    - `src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-editor-field.tsx`
  - repeated app-local field blocks reviewed:
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mysqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/postgresToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/redisToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/oracleToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mssqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/elasticsearchToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/jenkinsToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/kubernetesToolEditor.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - after `ToolEditorShell` extraction, the next highest-frequency duplication inside the same family was the repeated stacked field block: label row on top, control on the next line, with identical label typography across all editor variants
  - this pattern appeared dozens of times and had become the de facto field contract for OpsPilot tool editors, so leaving it inline would keep the family styling partially governed and partially local
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - field wrapper
    - label text using the same muted `text-sm` token styling
    - control slot below the label
  - comparison evidence:
    - PostgreSQL, MSSQL, Oracle, and MySQL repeated the same host/port/database/user/password field wrapper
    - Redis and Elasticsearch repeated the same wrapper for URL/credential and SSL-related fields
    - Jenkins and Kubernetes repeated the same wrapper despite much smaller forms
  - difference analysis:
    - controls vary between `Input`, `Input.Password`, `InputNumber`, and `Input.TextArea`
    - some grids remain two-column, some fields stay single-column
    - these differences belong to layout composition around the field block, not to the field block itself
- governance decision
  - extract `ToolEditorField` as the shared labeled-control primitive for OpsPilot tool editors
  - do not force it onto unrelated app forms because the evidence here is strongest inside the tool-editor family and we already have different form-row semantics elsewhere such as onboarding setting rows
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/index.ts`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mysqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/postgresToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/redisToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/oracleToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/mssqlToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/elasticsearchToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/jenkinsToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/kubernetesToolEditor.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tool-editor-field.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - replaced repeated inline label wrappers with `ToolEditorField`
    - preserved each editor's control type, grid layout, and testing behavior
    - intentionally left switch-only rows and footer actions untouched because they are a distinct interaction pattern
- Storybook plan
  - new source-of-truth entry:
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/ToolEditorField`
  - keep the shell-level contract layered above it:
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/ToolEditorShell`
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/FamilyOverview`
  - future tool-editor fields should default to `ToolEditorField` unless a field needs genuinely different vertical rhythm or label semantics

  - this was a good governance checkpoint because the right move was stronger reuse of the current shell API, not yet another footer helper

- governance decision
  - do not extract a new footer component

    - removed the log access-config page-local wrapper div
    - simplified the Storybook shell story to rely on the same default contract

    - `Business/Integrations/Onboarding/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/K8s/FamilyOverview`
  - future K8s access flows should override `actionsClassName` only for real layout differences, not for the default primary-next footer alignment

## 2026-07-04 Folded repeated OpsPilot test-action footers into ToolEditorShell

- candidate component
  - reviewed existing shared business shell:

  - do not create a separate footer component
  - extend `ToolEditorShell` with `detailFooter` and `detailFooterClassName` so the footer becomes part of the shared shell contract and Storybook source of truth

    - Storybook now demonstrates both the default footer alignment and an override case through the shell contract

  - source of truth remains:

    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/FamilyOverview`
  - future editor action rows should use `detailFooter` first and only add a new primitive if footer behavior diverges beyond shell-level layout

## 2026-07-04 Removed empty trailing wrappers from the shared search-toolbar shell

- candidate component
  - reviewed shared framework shells:
    - `src/components/toolbar-split-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
  - compared app consumers:
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetSearch/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/result/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/documents/qapair/result/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - several pages were repeatedly patching the same search-toolbar behavior with `justify-start` and `searchClassName="mr-0"` when no trailing actions were present
  - that pattern pointed to a shell-level layout gap: `ToolbarSplitShell` always rendered both slot wrappers even when one side was empty, so consumers had to override spacing for a search-only toolbar
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - `SearchActionBar` used without meaningful trailing actions
    - page-level class overrides added only to neutralize the empty trailing slot
  - comparison evidence:
    - log integration list and both CMDB asset-search states shared the no-actions search-toolbar layout
    - monitor integration and OpsPilot knowledge pages showed the contrasting case where real trailing actions still belong on the shared split-toolbar contract
  - difference analysis:
    - the real difference is whether a trailing slot exists, not a different business component
    - pages with real actions should keep the two-column split layout
    - pages without actions should not need local alignment hacks
- governance decision
  - do not create a new search-only toolbar component
  - fix the root contract by making `ToolbarSplitShell` render slot wrappers only when that slot has content
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/toolbar-split-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetSearch/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/search-action-bar.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - removed the empty trailing wrapper from the framework shell when no trailing node exists
    - simplified log integration and CMDB asset-search pages by deleting no-actions alignment overrides
    - added a Storybook search-only example so the no-actions contract is now explicitly governed
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchActionBar`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/ToolbarSplitShell`
  - future search-only pages should rely on the shell behavior first instead of compensating with page-local `justify-start` / `mr-0` overrides

## 2026-07-04 Removed stale SearchActionBar alignment overrides from shared consumers and stories

- candidate component
  - reviewed existing framework contract:
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/toolbar-split-shell/index.tsx`
  - compared shared consumers and Storybook entries:
    - `src/components/mlops-algorithm-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-catalog-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - after fixing empty trailing wrappers at the shell layer, several shared consumers and stories still carried the same historical alignment overrides: `justify-start`, `justify-between`, and `searchClassName="mr-0"`
  - these overrides no longer expressed business intent; they were leftovers from the pre-governed toolbar contract and risked teaching the wrong default usage in Storybook
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - shared search toolbar with an explicit bottom-margin override only
    - extra alignment classes added even though the default split-toolbar contract now already handles both search-only and search-plus-actions states
  - comparison evidence:
    - `MlopsAlgorithmWorkspaceShell` wrapped the search bar in a shared panel but still forced left alignment and removed a margin that no longer exists
    - integration-catalog stories repeated the same no-actions override across multiple variants
    - monitor-object story repeated a redundant `justify-between` override even though that is already the framework default when actions exist
  - difference analysis:
    - the only meaningful local variation is bottom spacing (`mb-0`)
    - horizontal alignment now belongs to the framework contract and should not be restated unless a page truly deviates from the default pattern
- governance decision
  - do not add new props or another wrapper component
  - remove stale overrides from shared consumers and Storybook so the current framework contract becomes the demonstrated source of truth
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/mlops-algorithm-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-catalog-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - kept only the meaningful `mb-0` spacing override
    - deleted redundant `justify-start`, `justify-between`, and `searchClassName="mr-0"` props
    - left action-bearing stories intact so they still validate the shared split-toolbar layout without local alignment noise
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchActionBar`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/ToolbarSplitShell`
    - `Business/Integrations/IntegrationCatalogWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Monitor/ObjectWorkspaceShell`
  - future stories should demonstrate search toolbar variants with the minimum props needed for the behavior under test

## 2026-07-04 Promoted left-aligned filter rows into the shared FilterToolbar contract

- candidate component
  - reviewed shared framework component:
    - `src/components/filter-toolbar/index.tsx`
  - compared business consumers and Storybook entries:
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/components/FilterBar.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/filter-toolbar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/alarm-settings-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/introduction-table-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - multiple apps and stories were repeating the same left-alignment patch for dense filter rows: `className="... justify-start"` plus `contentClassName="justify-start ..."`
  - that repetition showed a stable framework-level variant rather than page-specific styling, especially because the same pattern appears in both runtime pages and Storybook demonstrations
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - horizontally left-aligned filter controls
    - shared wrapping behavior for multi-line filter rows
    - optional local gap and bottom-margin adjustments
  - comparison evidence:
    - alarm integration filter bar uses left-aligned search and labeled selects
    - alarm and CMDB operation logs both use the same left-aligned dense filter row with wider form controls
    - Storybook already documented the same layout in multiple places, but through class overrides instead of a first-class prop
  - difference analysis:
    - the meaningful variation is alignment (`start` vs `end`)
    - gap size and margin remain local layout tuning and should stay in `contentClassName` / `className`
- governance decision
  - do not create a second filter-toolbar component
  - extend `FilterToolbar` with an explicit `align` prop so left-aligned filter rows become a governed shared variant instead of repeated class hacks
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/filter-toolbar/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/components/FilterBar.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/filter-toolbar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/alarm-settings-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/introduction-table-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - introduced `align="start" | "end"` on the shared component
    - migrated left-aligned consumers to `align="start"`
    - removed duplicated `justify-start` overrides while preserving local margin and gap control
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/FilterToolbar`
    - `Business/Alarm/AlarmSettingsWorkspaceShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/IntroductionTableWorkspaceShell`
  - future left-aligned filter rows should use `align="start"` first and reserve raw justify classes for genuine layout exceptions

## 2026-07-04 Promoted truncation-safe content width into the shared PageHeaderShell contract

    - the repeated override appeared in both shared runtime components and Storybook-facing wrappers
  - difference analysis:

    - the shrinkable content column is foundational layout behavior and belongs in the shared header primitive

  - do not keep `min-w-0` as a per-consumer footnote
  - promote truncation-safe content width into the default `PageHeaderShell` contract and remove redundant overrides from shared consumers

  - migration shape:

    - added a Storybook example that exercises the default long-title-with-actions behavior directly

    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
    - `Framework/Section/SectionHeader` via existing family stories
  - future header wrappers should only override `contentClassName` when they truly need behavior beyond the default truncation-safe contract

    - `src/stories/job-page-header.stories.tsx`

  - once `PageFormHeaderCard` existed, `JobPageHeader` had become a zero-logic business alias over the same shared primitive
  - evidence from current usage showed it was no longer a meaningful runtime abstraction boundary: it only sat between the shared primitive and one shared job shell plus Storybook
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - framed form-style page header with title, description, and optional back affordance
  - comparison evidence:
    - `JobPageHeader` no longer owned unique markup or behavior after the previous extraction
    - `JobListWorkspaceShell` and the job family story could consume `PageFormHeaderCard` directly without losing business semantics
  - difference analysis:
    - the meaningful business contract in job pages now lives in `JobListWorkspaceShell` and the surrounding job-specific toolbars, badges, and flows
    - the page header card itself is already governed at the framework layer
- governance decision
  - remove `JobPageHeader` instead of keeping a redundant alias component
  - let job shells and family stories consume `PageFormHeaderCard` directly so the shared primitive becomes the true source of truth
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/job-list-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/job-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/components/job-page-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/job-page-header.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - rewired the job list workspace shell to use `PageFormHeaderCard`
    - rewired the job family overview to demonstrate the same shared primitive directly
    - removed the redundant business wrapper and its standalone story entry
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth is now cleaner:
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageFormHeaderCard`
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageFormWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Job/FamilyOverview`
  - future job-page header examples should live in the family overview or consume `PageFormHeaderCard` directly rather than reintroducing a thin wrapper component

## 2026-07-04 Removed the redundant ToolEditorField standalone story but kept ToolEditorEmptyState as an independent contract

- candidate components
  - reviewed business components and Storybook entry points:
    - `src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-editor-field.tsx`
    - `src/components/opspilot-tool-editor/tool-editor-empty-state.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tool-editor-field.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tool-editor-empty-state.stories.tsx`
  - compared family overview:
    - `src/stories/opspilot-tool-editor-family.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - after collapsing `ToolInstanceSidebar` and `ToolEditorShell` into the ToolEditor family overview, the remaining standalone ToolEditor stories needed another pass to confirm whether they still added contract value or had become Storybook duplication
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated ToolEditor family patterns:
    - `ToolEditorField` is the shared label-control primitive reused by multiple editors including MySQL, Postgres, Redis, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Elasticsearch, MSSQL, and Oracle editors under `src/app/opspilot/components/skill/`
    - `ToolEditorEmptyState` appears in both governed shells (`ToolInstanceSidebar`, `ToolEditorShell`) and direct page-level empty states such as `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/tool/page.tsx` and `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/skill/detail/settings/page.tsx`
  - difference analysis:
    - the family overview already shows the meaningful `ToolEditorField` contract dimensions:
      - standard text-like control slot
      - status/toggle-like compact control slot
    - the standalone `ToolEditorField` story only re-expressed those same two dimensions with real Ant Design controls, without adding a new API or state boundary
    - the family overview does show one empty editor-shell state, but it does not fully replace the standalone `ToolEditorEmptyState` contract because the standalone entry still documents:
      - bare usage outside the shell/sidebar containers
      - compact versus `fullHeight` rendering
      - copy variations such as "Please select an instance" and "No matching skills"
- governance decision
  - delete the standalone `ToolEditorField` story because the family overview now fully covers its useful contract surface
  - keep the standalone `ToolEditorEmptyState` story because it still carries an independent contract that is consumed directly outside the family shells
- migration
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/tool-editor-field.stories.tsx`
  - kept as standalone source of truth:
    - `src/stories/tool-editor-empty-state.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - use `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/FamilyOverview` to validate labeled field composition
    - preserve `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/EmptyState` until the family overview can clearly express direct bare usage and all empty-state height/copy variants without becoming noisy
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/EmptyState`
  - future standalone ToolEditor stories should survive only when they document a contract that exists outside the governed shell composition

    - `ToolConnectionStatusTag` is not directly consumed across multiple unrelated pages; it is used as the governed connection-status leaf inside `ToolEditorShell`, which then powers multiple editors such as mysql, redis, postgres, oracle, mssql, elasticsearch, jenkins, and kubernetes

    - `Business/OpsPilot/ToolEditor/EmptyState`

      - `SourceMenuTree`

## 2026-07-04 Removed the redundant PasswordFields standalone story while keeping the other Security leaf stories

- candidate components
  - reviewed Security family and member stories:
    - `src/stories/security-family.stories.tsx`

    - `src/stories/password-fields.stories.tsx`

  - the security area already had a governed family overview that assembled the password workflow and secret-entry leaves, so the remaining standalone stories needed a pass to distinguish true state coverage from default-state duplication
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated security workflow pattern:
    - `PasswordPolicyNotice`, `PasswordFields`, and `PasswordFormModal` form one reusable password contract used by shared security flows, user-profile self-service, system-manager administration, and auth reset screens
    - `EditablePasswordField` and `AuthSecretField` form the shared secret-entry leaf layer used by infrastructure and integration credentials
  - difference analysis:
    - `password-fields.stories.tsx` only rendered the default two-password-fields layout plus the optional temporary-password toggle that already appears in `security-family.stories.tsx`
    - its narrower copy overrides (`requiredMessage`, `temporaryTooltip`) do not create a new structural or behavioral contract; they are just alternate text values on the same props surface
    - the other standalone Security stories still carry independent contract value:
      - `PasswordPolicyNotice`: `Loading`
      - `PasswordFormModal`: validation-hint and temporary-toggle interaction assertions
      - `EditablePasswordField`: key-prefix and disabled branches
      - `AuthSecretField`: password/private-key mode switching
- governance decision
  - delete the standalone `PasswordFields` story
  - keep the remaining Security leaf stories because they still document state or behavior branches that the family overview does not fully absorb
- migration
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/password-fields.stories.tsx`
  - kept as Storybook sources of truth:
    - `src/stories/security-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/password-policy-notice.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/password-form-modal.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/editable-password-field.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/auth-secret-field.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - validate the default password-fields composition through the Security family overview
    - preserve the leaf stories that still cover loading, interaction, disabled, or multi-mode branches
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Security/FamilyOverview`
    - `Framework/Security/Password/PolicyNotice`
    - `Framework/Security/Password/FormModal`
    - `Framework/Security/Secrets/EditablePasswordField`
    - `Framework/Security/Secrets/AuthSecretField`
  - future standalone security stories should survive only when they express a state branch or interaction contract that is not already visible inside the family overview

    - `src/stories/monitor-integration-config-state.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:

    - preserve richer dashboard/widget stories until their extra branches are genuinely absorbed elsewhere
- Storybook plan

    - the surviving richer Monitor leaf stories
  - future monitor semantic-badge stories should remain standalone only when they add a branch the family overview does not already render explicitly

  - keep the current security leaf stories reviewed in this pass

  - future security cleanup should remove leaf stories only after the family overview renders each surviving state branch explicitly instead of relying on one representative composition path

  - after collapsing `SystemManagerMenuPageCard`, the remaining application-menu leaves needed a fresh pass to confirm whether they still justified their own contracts
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:

  - difference analysis:
    - `system-manager-menu-group-card.stories.tsx` still owns:
      - editing state
      - empty group state
      - drag-target state
    - `system-manager-menu-page-item.stories.tsx` still owns:

      - inline editing behavior
    - `system-manager-source-menu-tree.stories.tsx` still owns:

    - none of those interaction/state branches are currently rendered explicitly inside the family overview

  - keep these standalone application-menu stories for now:
    - `Business/SystemManager/ApplicationMenu/SystemManagerMenuGroupCard`
    - `Business/SystemManager/ApplicationMenu/SystemManagerMenuPageItem`
    - `Business/SystemManager/ApplicationMenu/SystemManagerSourceMenuTree`

  - re-evaluate only after the family overview absorbs these editing, drag, loading, and disabled branches directly; until then, these are not garbage leaves

## 2026-07-04 Collapsed the standalone page-scaffold leaf stories into the governed Layout family

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/page-header-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/page-form-header-card.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/page-form-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/panel-shell.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/page-intro-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/dashboard-workspace-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-view-toolbar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/event-alert-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/job-list-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/execution/quick-exec/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/execution/file-dist/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/execution/cron-task/create/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/execution/cron-task/edit/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/(pages)/application/manage/menu/config/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/search/queryPanel.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/customReporting/components/batchReviewDrawer.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/detail/page.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - after the badge/tag cleanup line, the next biggest Storybook noise source was the framework page-scaffold layer: the same header, framed panel, and form-shell grammar was split across four standalone leaf stories even though those primitives are already reused by multiple business wrappers and page-level shells
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - `PageHeaderShell` is the base grammar reused by `PageIntroHeader`, `SectionHeader`, `DashboardWorkspaceHeader`, `OpsAnalysisViewToolbar`, `EventAlertWorkspaceShell`, and `PageFormHeaderCard`
    - `PageFormHeaderCard` is reused by `PageFormWorkspaceShell`, `JobListWorkspaceShell`, and the job execution pages
    - `PanelShell` is reused by alarm integration guides, custom-reporting review drawers, monitor query panels, and system-manager menu configuration surfaces
    - all of these belong to the same framework-level page scaffold contract rather than separate business domains
  - difference analysis:
    - `page-header-shell.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - back + actions header
      - richer elevated toolbar styling
      - long-title shrink behavior
    - `page-form-header-card.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - default
      - back
      - back + actions
    - `page-form-workspace-shell.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - default form shell
      - back-enabled form shell
    - `panel-shell.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - default framed panel
      - guide-style panel
      - compact detail card
    - none of those leaves carried independent workflow state; they were all style-and-slot variations of one framework scaffold family
- governance decision
  - delete the standalone framework leaf stories:
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageHeaderShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageFormHeaderCard`
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PageFormWorkspaceShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/PageScaffold/PanelShell`
  - keep the contract governed by `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - keep business wrappers such as `EventAlertWorkspaceShell`, `AlarmSettingsWorkspaceShell`, and `JobListWorkspaceShell` as separate stories because they still describe domain-specific data layout and interaction framing, not just the shared scaffold primitive
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/page-header-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/page-form-header-card.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/page-form-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/panel-shell.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/EventAlert/EventAlertWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Alarm/Settings/AlarmSettingsWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Job/Workspace/ListShell`
- Storybook plan
  - the Layout family now owns the full page-scaffold grammar:
    - base header shell
    - framed form header card
    - framed form workspace shell
    - shared content panel shell
  - future framework scaffold variants should land in the family overview first instead of creating a new leaf story unless they introduce genuinely independent interaction state

      - hidden upload list
    - `multi-file-upload-panel.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - default
      - hidden upload list
      - extra action
    - `import-file-modal-shell.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - JSON import
      - Excel import with template
    - none of those leaves introduced independent workflow state beyond the shared upload subsystem; they are all slot, copy, icon, and list-visibility variants of one framework contract

  - delete the standalone framework upload leaf stories:
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/DropPanel`
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/SingleFilePanel`
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/MultiFilePanel`
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/ImportFileModalShell`
  - keep the contract governed by `Framework/Forms/Upload/FamilyOverview`

  - removed:
    - `src/stories/upload-drop-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/single-file-upload-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/multi-file-upload-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/import-file-modal-shell.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Forms/Upload/FamilyOverview`
- Storybook plan
  - the upload family now owns:
    - base single-file drop panel
    - directory upload
    - single-file hidden-list and rich-trigger variants
    - multi-file hidden-list/template variant
    - JSON import modal and workbook import modal
  - future upload-shell adjustments should land in the family overview first unless they add an independently governed workflow beyond the shared upload subsystem

    - `AuthSecretField` is the infrastructure-oriented wrapper reused by integrations and NodeManager installation/uninstall flows

  - keep the leaf input contract governed by `Framework/Security/FamilyOverview`

## 2026-07-04 Collapsed the standalone CompactEmptyState story into the governed Feedback family

- candidate components
  - reviewed:

    - `src/stories/compact-empty-state.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/stories/job-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/cmdb-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-catalog-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-instance-management-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/components/content-drawer/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/entity-list/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/tree-selector-panel/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-data-source-params-config/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/related-alerts-panel/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/top-menu/notifications.tsx`
    - `src/components/monitor-integration-config-state/index.tsx`
  - reviewed app-level reuse:
    - Alarm / CMDB / Monitor / Log / OpsAnalysis / OpsPilot / NodeManager pages all consume the same compact empty-state primitive directly
- why it was reviewed
  - `CompactEmptyState` is one of the highest-frequency framework feedback primitives in the repo, and its standalone story had narrowed to the same contract already represented by the feedback family: description-only empty feedback with spacing variants
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - the feedback family already governs generic empty/state feedback alongside completion and overlay shells
    - business pages across multiple apps reuse `CompactEmptyState` directly for no-data, no-selection, precondition-empty, and table-empty states
  - difference analysis:
    - `compact-empty-state.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - default no-data copy
      - guidance copy
      - selection prompt
      - table-empty spacing
    - these are still the same feedback contract:
      - description-only compact empty state
      - spacing tuned to list/table/detail surfaces
    - no branch introduced independent interaction state or a separate higher-level workflow
- governance decision
  - delete the standalone `Framework/Feedback/CompactEmptyState` story
  - keep the empty-state contract governed by `Framework/Feedback/FamilyOverview`
  - keep the overlay shells (`OperateModal`, `OperateDrawer`, `OperateFormModal`, `OperateFormDrawer`, `ContentFormDrawer`) standalone for now because they still own larger action/footer/alias/state matrices
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/feedback-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/compact-empty-state.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Feedback/FamilyOverview`
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateModal`
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateDrawer`
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormModal`
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormDrawer`
    - `Framework/Feedback/ContentFormDrawer`
- Storybook plan
  - the feedback family now owns compact empty-state copy/spacing branches directly
  - future compact no-data feedback changes should update the family overview instead of reintroducing a separate leaf story

## 2026-07-04 Collapsed the standalone metric-display leaf stories into the governed DataDisplay family

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/data-display-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/auto-fit-metric-value.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/summary-metric-card.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/log-analysis-widgets/single.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/single.tsx`
    - `src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/stat-card.tsx`
    - `src/components/log-kpi-card/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/home/page.tsx`
    - `src/components/related-alerts-panel/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/detail/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/components/SummaryStats.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/collection/profess/components/collectionStats.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/collection/profess/components/nodeMgmtSyncDetail.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/collection/profess/components/taskDetail.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - `AutoFitMetricValue` is a pure framework display primitive, and `SummaryMetricCard` is its higher-level metric-card wrapper reused across multiple product surfaces. Both had standalone stories, but the shared metric-display grammar was still missing from the DataDisplay family overview
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - `AutoFitMetricValue` powers log-analysis single metrics, ops-analysis singles, monitor stat cards, and log KPI cards
    - `SummaryMetricCard` powers alarm integration summaries, CMDB collection summaries, job home dashboard summaries, and related-alert summaries
    - these are all framework display semantics for metric emphasis, units, trend context, and compact summary framing
  - difference analysis:
    - `auto-fit-metric-value.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - baseline number + unit
      - centered number-only display
      - long string fallback
    - `summary-metric-card.stories.tsx` only owned:
      - alarm-style horizontal card
      - compact collection card
      - long-unit metric
      - iconless summary
      - vertical KPI card
      - borderless detail metric
      - collection result card
    - these branches are still one shared display grammar around auto-sizing values and summary cards; they do not introduce a separate business workflow or interaction contract
- governance decision
  - delete the standalone framework stories:
    - `Framework/DataDisplay/AutoFitMetricValue`
    - `Framework/DataDisplay/SummaryMetricCard`
  - keep the contract governed by `Framework/DataDisplay/FamilyOverview`
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/data-display-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/auto-fit-metric-value.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/summary-metric-card.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/DataDisplay/FamilyOverview`
- Storybook plan
  - the data-display family now owns:
    - auto-fit metric baseline / centered / long-label branches
    - horizontal, vertical, iconless, borderless, and result-style summary metric cards
  - future framework metric-display adjustments should land in the family overview first instead of reintroducing separate metric leaf stories

    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/collection/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/management/page.tsx`
    - `src/components/summary-detail-layout-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/alarm-k8s-guide/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/alarm-zabbix-guide/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/controllerInstall/installing/operationGuidanceSections.tsx`
    - `src/components/k8s-common-issues-drawer/index.tsx`
  - kept standalone after review:
    - `src/stories/section-header.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/detail-list-drawer-shell.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - the remaining content leaf stories were no longer isolated one-off widgets. They formed one repeated content-contract lane for page introductions, summary intros, guided steps, notes, and troubleshooting blocks used across CMDB, Alarm, and Node Manager surfaces
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - `Introduction` is reused by CMDB list and workspace pages as the default page-level explanatory shell
    - `DetailIntro` is reused through summary/detail layouts as the compact summary header shell
    - `GuideStepPanel`, `NoteListPanel`, and `TroubleshootingCard` are reused across Alarm integration guides and Node Manager installation guidance
  - difference analysis:
    - the deleted stories only expressed local visual branches such as narrow intro width, rich media intro, card/timeline guide steps, plain/filled notes, and accent/default troubleshooting states
    - these are still one shared content-contract family rather than separate reusable Storybook roots
    - `SectionHeader` still owns a broader action and variant matrix used widely across app shells, so it remains standalone
- governance decision
  - create `Framework/Content/FamilyOverview` as the contract center for content guidance patterns
  - delete the standalone framework stories:
    - `Framework/Content/Introduction`
    - `Framework/Content/DetailIntro`
    - `Framework/Content/GuideStepPanel`
    - `Framework/Content/NoteListPanel`
    - `Framework/Content/TroubleshootingCard`
- migration
  - added:
    - `src/stories/content-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/introduction.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/detail-intro.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/guide-step-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/note-list-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/troubleshooting-card.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Content/FamilyOverview`
    - `Framework/Content/SectionHeader`
    - `Framework/Content/DetailListDrawerShell`
- Storybook plan
  - the content family now owns intro, summary intro, guided steps, note lists, and troubleshooting branches directly
  - future content guidance changes should land in the family overview first instead of reintroducing separate leaf pages

## 2026-07-04 Collapsed the standalone toolbar leaves into the governed Toolbar family

  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/filter-toolbar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/search-action-bar.stories.tsx`

- why they were reviewed
  - the remaining toolbar stories were mostly layout and composition shells for search, filters, and actions. Two of them were already thin wrappers over `ToolbarSplitShell`, which signaled one shared framework contract rather than four separate roots
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - `ToolbarSplitShell` is the base left-right toolbar layout contract
    - `SearchActionBar` composes `ToolbarSplitShell` with AntD search input and action buttons
    - `SearchCombinationToolbar` composes `ToolbarSplitShell` with `SearchCombination` and trailing actions
    - `FilterToolbar` covers the same toolbar lane with right/start/between alignment variants for dense filters
  - difference analysis:
    - the deleted stories only owned local combinations of search width, action count, segmented controls, dropdown actions, and filter alignment
    - those differences are variant branches inside one toolbar contract family, not separate reusable Storybook roots
- governance decision
  - create `Framework/Toolbar/FamilyOverview` as the contract center for toolbar layout and action patterns
  - delete the standalone framework stories:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/FilterToolbar`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchActionBar`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchCombinationToolbar`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/ToolbarSplitShell`
- migration
  - added:
    - `src/stories/toolbar-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/filter-toolbar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/search-action-bar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/search-combination-toolbar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/toolbar-split-shell.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/FamilyOverview`
- Storybook plan
  - the toolbar family now owns split-shell, search-led, search-combination, and filter-alignment branches directly
  - future toolbar shell changes should land in the family overview first instead of reintroducing separate leaf pages

## 2026-07-04 Collapsed the SideNavigation implementation-detail leaves into the governed Layout family

    - `src/stories/sub-layout.stories.tsx`

    - `src/components/layout/sub-layout/sideMenuNavList.tsx`
    - `src/components/layout/sub-layout/side-menu.tsx`

  - repeated patterns already governed:

- Storybook plan

## 2026-07-04 Removed the non-visual redirect helper from Storybook contracts

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/redirect-menu.stories.tsx`
    - `src/components/redirect-menu/index.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/app/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/node-manager/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/ops-analysis/(pages)/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - the remaining Navigation leaf looked suspiciously non-visual. The implementation confirmed it only performs a router replace on mount and returns `null`, which means it has no visual or interactive contract for Storybook to validate
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated runtime pattern:
    - `RedirectToFirstMenu` is reused by multiple top-level app entry pages to jump to the first permitted menu
  - difference analysis:
    - despite broad reuse, it is a routing helper rather than a UI component
    - Storybook is the trusted source for component behavior, variants, and visual contracts, so a renderless runtime redirect does not belong in the governed shared-component surface
- governance decision
  - remove the standalone Storybook story:
    - `Framework/Navigation/RedirectToFirstMenu`
  - keep the implementation in app code, but treat it as a runtime helper instead of a standard shared component contract
- migration
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/redirect-menu.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Navigation/StepWizardFlow`
    - `Framework/Navigation/TreeSelectorPanel`
- Storybook plan
  - future renderless helpers should not enter Storybook unless they grow a real visual or interactive contract

## 2026-07-04 Removed the raw icon wrapper from Storybook contracts

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/icon.stories.tsx`
    - `src/components/icon/index.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/detail-intro/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/top-section/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/layout/sub-layout/sideMenuNavList.tsx`
    - `src/components/integration-access-complete/index.tsx`
    - plus broad app-level consumption across CMDB, Alarm, Monitor, Ops Analysis, Node Manager, and OpsPilot surfaces
- why it was reviewed
  - the remaining Display leaf looked too thin to justify Storybook space. The implementation confirmed it is a raw SVG sprite wrapper, while the story only documented a single default icon name
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated runtime pattern:
    - `Icon` is widely reused as a low-level rendering primitive inside many shared and business components
  - difference analysis:
    - despite broad reuse, it does not expose a meaningful visual system contract beyond `type`, `className`, and `style`
    - the single default story does not validate icon families, token usage, accessibility semantics, or any higher-level behavior
    - semantic icon usage is already better represented through the components that consume `Icon`, such as section headers, top sections, detail intros, completion results, and side navigation
- governance decision
  - remove the standalone Storybook story:
    - `Framework/Display/Icon`
  - keep `Icon` as a low-level implementation primitive used by governed components instead of treating it as a first-class Storybook contract
- migration
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/icon.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - icon behavior is now represented indirectly through governed framework and business components
- Storybook plan
  - raw rendering primitives should only live in Storybook when they own a real contract surface, not when they are thin wrappers around sprite usage

  - `Framework/Overlays/ContentDrawer` still existed as a standalone framework page even though the repo only consumes `ContentDrawer` through shared wrappers and the feedback family already serves as the overlay contract center
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - `ContentFormDrawer` already governs the action-bearing branch of the same drawer shell
    - `DetailListDrawerShell` already governs content-detail composition in the Content family
    - the feedback family now covers the raw content-preview branch, header extra branch, footer branch, loading branch, empty fallback branch, and locked-mask branch for `ContentDrawer`
  - difference analysis:
    - the deleted standalone story mostly documented shell-level states: plain text content, caller-owned children, loading, empty, custom footer, and mask behavior
    - those branches describe the raw overlay shell, not an independent product-facing pattern with its own broader taxonomy
    - real product code does not import `ContentDrawer` directly outside shared wrappers, which weakens the case for a separate top-level Storybook leaf
- governance decision
  - remove the duplicate standalone framework story:
    - `Framework/Overlays/ContentDrawer`
  - keep `ContentDrawer` governed inside:
    - `Framework/Feedback/FamilyOverview`
  - keep wrapper-specific contracts where they add higher-level semantics:
    - `Framework/Feedback/ContentFormDrawer`
    - `Framework/Content/FamilyOverview` for detail-list drawer compositions
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/feedback-family.stories.tsx`
  - removed:
    - `src/stories/content-drawer.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/Feedback/FamilyOverview`
    - `Framework/Feedback/ContentFormDrawer`
    - `Framework/Content/FamilyOverview`
- Storybook plan
  - future `ContentDrawer` shell changes should update the feedback family first instead of reintroducing a standalone raw overlay leaf
