## 2026-07-05 Reviewed CodeEditor and kept the standalone framework contract

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/code-editor.stories.tsx`
    - business-family references in:
      - `src/stories/monitor-family.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/node-manager-family.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/code-editor/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/collect/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/node-manager/(pages)/cloudregion/node/collectorDetail/configModal.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - `Framework/Editors/CodeEditor` remained one of the last standalone framework leaves, so this slice needed to verify whether it had become redundant after business-family Storybook coverage expanded
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - monitor and node-manager business stories already show real editable authoring surfaces that use `CodeEditor`
    - read-only installation and access-guide examples have already converged onto `CodeSnippet`, which reduces accidental overlap between editor and snippet contracts
  - difference analysis:
    - the standalone `CodeEditor` page still owns framework-specific behavior that business stories do not fully replace:
      - fullscreen header behavior
      - loading while remaining editable in principle
      - legacy `showCopy` alias compatibility
      - mode-agnostic editor wiring independent of monitor or node-manager business semantics
    - there is no existing framework family root that can absorb those editor-specific branches without diluting Storybook ownership
- governance decision
  - keep the standalone framework story:
    - `Framework/Editors/CodeEditor`
  - do not force-merge it into business families or an unrelated framework family just to reduce leaf count
- migration
  - no file migration in this slice
  - this is an explicit governance hold based on current evidence, not a missing cleanup
- Storybook plan
  - if additional shared editor primitives appear, create a true `Framework/Editors/FamilyOverview` and move `CodeEditor` there
  - until then, `Framework/Editors/CodeEditor` remains the source of truth for editor-only contract branches

## 2026-07-05 Reviewed Permission and kept the standalone framework contract

- candidate components
  - reviewed:

    - business-story references in:
      - `src/stories/events-family.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/event-alert-information-summary.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:

    - broad page/component usage across alarm, cmdb, opspilot, system-manager, and shared widgets
- why they were reviewed
  - `Framework/Guards/Permission` stayed standalone after several family consolidations, so this slice needed to confirm whether it should be folded into another Storybook subtree or explicitly retained
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - the permission wrapper is reused broadly across apps as the same guard primitive around actions, table operations, toolbar commands, and instance-level edit/delete flows
    - some business stories already show the guard in context, which helps verify composed workflows
  - difference analysis:
    - the standalone page still owns the framework-level guard contract itself:
      - required-permission gating
      - custom fallback rendering
      - instance-permission denial behavior with tooltip semantics
    - no current framework family root cleanly owns guard semantics; folding it into layout, feedback, or security would blur component taxonomy rather than improve it
- governance decision
  - keep the standalone framework story:
    - `Framework/Guards/Permission`
  - do not merge it into unrelated families without a real guard/policy subtree to receive it
- migration
  - no file migration in this slice
  - this is an explicit governance hold based on current evidence, not a missing cleanup
- Storybook plan
  - if more shared guard or policy primitives emerge, create a dedicated framework guard family before revisiting this decision
  - until then, `Framework/Guards/Permission` remains the source of truth for permission-gating behavior

## 2026-07-05 Folded DynamicForm and GroupTreeSelect into the DataEntry family contract

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/dynamic-form.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/group-tree-select.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/dynamic-form/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/group-tree-select/index.tsx`
    - shared and app consumers across opspilot, system-manager, alarm, cmdb, job, monitor, mlops, node-manager, ops-analysis, and integration forms
- why they were reviewed
  - `Framework/DataEntry/DynamicForm` and `Framework/DataEntry/GroupTreeSelect` were still isolated leaves even though they now form one stable data-entry stack and `DynamicForm` directly composes `GroupTreeSelect` through its shared field map
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - `DynamicForm` is the common form-shell primitive for vertical field composition across opspilot and system-manager modal/edit flows
    - `GroupTreeSelect` is the common ownership/group-assignment primitive reused broadly across alarm, cmdb, job, monitor, mlops, node-manager, and opspilot
    - the new family now covers both standalone roots' meaningful branches:
      - baseline input/textarea/select field composition
      - embedded `groupTreeSelect` field rendering inside `DynamicForm`
      - multiple vs single selection
      - search + clear behavior
      - locked values
      - subtree filtering and form binding
  - difference analysis:
    - the two old standalone pages described adjacent parts of the same governed data-entry contract rather than separate product taxonomies
    - keeping them split hid the fact that shared forms already rely on `GroupTreeSelect` as a first-class field type
- governance decision
  - remove the duplicate standalone framework stories:
    - `Framework/DataEntry/DynamicForm`
    - `Framework/DataEntry/GroupTreeSelect`
  - keep both primitives governed inside:
    - `Framework/DataEntry/FamilyOverview`

  - removed:
    - `src/stories/dynamic-form.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/group-tree-select.stories.tsx`
  - surviving Storybook contracts:
    - `Framework/DataEntry/FamilyOverview`
- Storybook plan
  - future `DynamicForm` and `GroupTreeSelect` changes should update the data-entry family first instead of reintroducing separate data-entry leaf pages

## 2026-07-05 Reviewed OperateFormModal and kept the standalone framework contract

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/operate-form-modal.stories.tsx`
    - supporting family references in:
      - `src/stories/feedback-family.stories.tsx`
      - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/operate-form-modal/index.tsx`
    - extensive usage across cmdb, job, mlops, system-manager, ops-analysis, log, alarm, and shared modals
- why they were reviewed
  - after consolidating the two drawer-form leaves, this slice needed to verify whether `OperateFormModal` had also become redundant or still owned a wider modal-specific framework contract
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - the feedback family already shows the baseline governed modal shell
    - many apps reuse the same modal wrapper for create/edit, selection, preview, and destructive flows
  - difference analysis:
    - the standalone modal story still owns a larger independent branch surface than the family page currently and coherently can:
      - confirm-only vs cancel-first
      - destructive multi-action placement
      - confirm popconfirm safety gate
      - wide viewer without footer
      - compact field entry
      - acknowledgement gate and post-confirm secondary actions
    - because this wrapper is the dominant cross-app form-overlay primitive, collapsing those branches into the family right now would make the feedback family noisy and reduce the modal contract's clarity
- governance decision
  - keep the standalone framework story:
    - `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormModal`
  - do not force-merge it into the feedback family until there is a clearer modal subtree or a materially smaller independent branch surface
- migration
  - no file migration in this slice
  - this is an explicit governance hold based on current evidence, not a missing cleanup
- Storybook plan
  - if modal-shell stories converge further, introduce a true feedback modal subtree before revisiting this decision
  - until then, `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormModal` remains the source of truth for the broader governed modal contract

## 2026-07-05 Reviewed layout shell roots and kept the standalone framework contracts

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/summary-detail-layout-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tree-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - supporting family reference:
      - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/components/management-table-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/summary-detail-layout-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/tree-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - business-shell consumers such as:
      - `src/components/event-alert-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
      - `src/components/integration-instance-management-shell/index.tsx`
      - `src/components/integration-catalog-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
      - `src/components/monitor-object-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
      - multiple app layouts in alarm, cmdb, log, monitor, node-manager, opspilot, and system-manager
- why they were reviewed
  - after folding several smaller layout and header leaves into the layout family, the remaining standalone layout roots needed an explicit audit to confirm whether they were still independent framework contracts or just family branches that had not been consolidated yet
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - all three components are reused broadly across apps as higher-order workspace/detail shells
    - the layout family already documents the surrounding page-shell vocabulary and explicitly points to these three as larger independent contracts
  - difference analysis:
    - `ManagementTableShell` still owns a broad table-workspace surface that includes page top sections, default search/actions composition, custom toolbars, modal slots, selection flows, pagination, and advanced table props
    - `SummaryDetailLayoutShell` still owns a routed detail-layout surface with summary card layout variants, menu composition, back-navigation semantics, and optional task progress composition
    - `TreeWorkspaceShell` still owns tree-sidebar workspace behavior including fixed vs resizable sidebars, collapse storage, sidebar header composition, and tree-panel layout contracts
    - each of these shells acts as a foundation for other business shells rather than as a thin prop alias over a lower family primitive
- governance decision
  - keep the standalone framework stories:
    - `Framework/Layout/ManagementTableShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/SummaryDetailLayoutShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/TreeWorkspaceShell`
  - do not merge them into `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview` while they still own broader page-level semantics than the family can carry cleanly
- migration
  - no file migration in this slice
  - this is an explicit governance hold based on current evidence, not a missing cleanup
- Storybook plan
  - if any of these shells shrink into thinner wrappers after additional business-shell extraction, revisit whether they should move under a dedicated layout subfamily
  - until then, each standalone layout root remains a source of truth for a materially larger framework contract than the family overview

## 2026-07-05 Current standalone framework root allowlist

- purpose
  - after this round of Storybook consolidation, the remaining standalone framework roots are no longer "forgotten leaves"; they are an explicit allowlist that should only change when new evidence shows their independent contract surface has genuinely shrunk or a new framework subfamily exists to receive them
- current allowlist
  - `Framework/Editors/CodeEditor`
    - keep while editor-only branches such as fullscreen, editable loading, and legacy copy compatibility still need a framework source of truth
  - `Framework/Feedback/OperateFormModal`
    - keep while the governed modal wrapper still owns a broader branch surface than the feedback family can carry cleanly
  - `Framework/Guards/Permission`
    - keep while there is no dedicated framework guard/policy subtree and the permission contract still needs a standalone root
  - `Framework/Layout/ManagementTableShell`
    - keep while it remains the higher-order table-workspace shell that many business shells compose
  - `Framework/Layout/SummaryDetailLayoutShell`
    - keep while it remains the routed detail-layout shell with summary, menu, and progress semantics
  - `Framework/Layout/TreeWorkspaceShell`
    - keep while it remains the higher-order tree-sidebar workspace shell that business workspaces compose
- governance rule
  - do not remove one of these standalone roots merely because the total leaf count looks high enough to trim
  - remove or relocate a root only when one of the following becomes true:
    - its remaining branches are already fully represented inside an existing framework family
    - it has shrunk into a thin wrapper over a lower-level governed primitive
    - a new framework subfamily exists that can own its contract without mixing unrelated taxonomies
- audit expectation
  - future governance passes should treat this allowlist as the baseline and only append deltas when a root is added, removed, or moved
  - Storybook scans should continue to confirm that no new accidental standalone framework roots appear outside this allowlist and the family overviews

## 2026-07-05 Reviewed cross-domain list workspace shells and did not extract a new shared business shell

- candidate components
  - reviewed:
    - `src/components/job-list-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-settings-list-shell/index.tsx`
  - reviewed story contracts:
    - `src/stories/job-list-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-settings-list-shell.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed real composition points:
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/execution/job-record/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/target/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/template/playbook-library/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/template/script-library/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/settings/manage/page.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - these two business shells are among the closest remaining cross-domain list-workspace candidates because both sit above shared table primitives and pair a page intro/header with search actions plus a page-local table or detail region
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated patterns already governed:
    - both shells rely on lower-level shared primitives rather than app-local raw layout:
      - `JobListWorkspaceShell` composes `PageFormHeaderCard`, `SearchCombinationToolbar`, `WorkspacePanel`, and `CustomTable`
      - `OpsAnalysisSettingsListShell` composes `TopSection`, `SearchActionBar`, and `CustomTable`
    - both expose page-local content, actions, and table configuration on top of the governed shared table ecosystem
  - difference analysis:
    - job list workspaces are filter-schema driven and center on multi-field search-combination behavior with optional inline detail content or internal table fallback
    - ops-analysis settings lists are simple keyword-search workspaces with top-intro framing, modal slots, and a denser settings-style table rhythm
    - those differences are not just prop-level variants; they reflect two still-distinct business navigation and filtering models
- governance decision
  - do not extract a new cross-domain shared business shell from these two candidates at this time
  - keep:
    - `JobListWorkspaceShell` as the job-domain list workspace contract
    - `OpsAnalysisSettingsListShell` as the ops-analysis settings list contract
- migration
  - no file migration in this slice
  - this is an explicit governance hold based on current evidence, not a missed extraction opportunity
- Storybook plan
  - revisit if future business shells converge on one of the following:
    - shared filter-schema driven list workspaces across multiple domains
    - shared intro-plus-keyword-search settings workspaces across multiple domains
  - until then, both shells stay business-local and continue to validate their shared dependencies through Storybook

## 2026-07-05 Reclassified the bare K8s install shell into framework scope and kept the real K8s contracts focused

- candidate component
  - `src/stories/k8s-collector-install.stories.tsx`
  - reviewed alongside:
    - `src/stories/k8s-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-access-asset-fields.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-collector-install-step.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-common-issues-drawer.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - the current K8s Storybook cluster contains one family overview plus several focused stories, so it was a natural place to check whether any leaf had become redundant after the earlier preset and fixture consolidation
  - the governance goal for this pass is not just fewer files, but a cleaner split between framework contracts and business contracts with Storybook as the single source of truth
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `K8sAccessAssetFields`, `K8sCollectorInstallStep`, and `K8sCommonIssuesDrawer` still each expose an independent contract surface that the family overview does not replace:
      - access-asset field layout and existing/new asset states
      - install-step state machine, preset-driven copy variants, and success/failure transitions
      - drawer open-state behavior and troubleshooting preset variants
    - `K8sCollectorInstall`, by contrast, is a lower-level shell consumed by `K8sCollectorInstallStep` across monitor, log, and CMDB flows
  - difference analysis:
    - the three focused K8s business stories still own domain semantics and explicit variant coverage, so deleting them would hide real governed contracts inside a higher-level overview page
    - the bare `K8sCollectorInstall` story does not own those business semantics; it only documents the shared command-display plus verification-feedback shell under the step component
- governance decision
  - keep these business contract stories in place:
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/AccessAssetFields`
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/CollectorInstallStep`
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/CommonIssuesDrawer`
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/FamilyOverview`
  - reclassify the lower-level `K8sCollectorInstall` Storybook entry from business scope into framework scope so the catalog better reflects its actual ownership boundary
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/k8s-collector-install.stories.tsx`
  - Storybook title moved:
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/CollectorInstall` -> `Framework/Feedback/K8sCollectorInstallShell`
- Storybook plan
  - focused K8s business stories remain the source of truth for onboarding semantics and preset variants
  - `Framework/Feedback/K8sCollectorInstallShell` now documents the reusable shell beneath that business layer, keeping framework/business taxonomy cleaner without deleting meaningful contracts
## 2026-07-05 Removed stale toolbar overrides from integration instance management flows

- candidate components
  - reviewed shared business shell:
    - `src/components/integration-instance-management-shell/index.tsx`
  - reviewed runtime and Storybook consumers:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/asset/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/receive/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-instance-management-shell.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - the shell already defines the default search-row alignment contract through `searchClassName = '!justify-between w-full items-center gap-3'`
  - monitor, log, and Storybook examples were still restating that exact class string locally and using older `mr-[8px]` spacing on action children
  - that drift makes the shell default less trustworthy and leaves action-row spacing inconsistent between runtime pages and Storybook
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - integration instance list page with one leading search input and trailing actions
    - action rows built from primary buttons, dropdowns, and refresh controls
  - difference analysis:
    - monitor adds an access button before the action dropdown and refresh control
    - log keeps only the dropdown and refresh control
    - those differences are content-only; neither app needs to override the shell's default search-row alignment or rely on child-level right margins
- governance decision
  - keep `IntegrationInstanceManagementShell` as the owning business shell contract
  - remove consumer-level copies of the default `searchClassName`
  - standardize action-row spacing on `gap-2` wrappers instead of scattering `mr-[8px]` onto individual buttons and dropdowns
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/asset/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/receive/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-instance-management-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - deleted redundant `searchClassName` props that exactly matched the shell default
    - rewired action groups to use wrapper-level `gap-2`
    - removed child-level `mr-[8px]` spacing overrides
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Business/Integrations/InstanceManagement/IntegrationInstanceManagementShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/ManagementTableShell`
    - `Framework/Layout/TreeWorkspaceShell`
  - future integration instance pages should override `searchClassName` only for real layout deviations, and should prefer wrapper-level gaps for toolbar actions
## 2026-07-05 Removed stale SearchActionBar alignment overrides from runtime consumers

- candidate components
  - reviewed shared framework contract:
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/toolbar-split-shell/index.tsx`
  - reviewed runtime consumers:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/monitorAlarm.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/collection/profess/page.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - the shared `SearchActionBar` already owns horizontal alignment through `ToolbarSplitShell`, and a prior governance pass had removed the same `justify-start` / `justify-between` / `searchClassName="mr-0"` overrides from Storybook and shared shell consumers
  - these three runtime pages were still carrying the older compensating classes, so runtime behavior was lagging behind the governed component contract
- cross-app comparison
  - repeated pattern:
    - one leading search field rendered through `SearchActionBar`
    - optional trailing controls such as primary actions or refresh selectors
    - local `mb-0` spacing override to fit the enclosing page rhythm
  - difference analysis:
    - monitor integration catalog is search-first and keeps hidden import/export actions for feature gating
    - monitor alarm view pairs the search field with `TimeSelector`
    - CMDB auto-discovery pairs the search field with a create-task button
    - none of those differences require consumer-level horizontal alignment overrides because the framework contract already handles both search-only and search-plus-actions layouts
- governance decision
  - keep `SearchActionBar` as the single source of truth for alignment and slot distribution
  - retain only the meaningful local bottom-spacing override `mb-0`
  - normalize leftover hidden action spacing in monitor integration catalog to wrapper-level `gap-2`
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/monitorAlarm.tsx`
    - `src/app/cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/collection/profess/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - removed `justify-start` / `justify-between` class overrides from `SearchActionBar`
    - removed `searchClassName="mr-0"` where it only duplicated the governed default behavior
    - replaced hidden child margin spacing with wrapper-level `gap-2` in the monitor integration catalog action slot
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/ToolbarSplitShell`
    - `Framework/Toolbar/SearchActionBar`
    - affected business surfaces continue to validate higher-level flows in their own stories and families
  - future runtime pages should override `SearchActionBar` alignment only for real deviations from the shared toolbar contract

  - reviewed runtime consumers:

    - `src/app/log/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/page.tsx`

  - after earlier PageHeaderShell cleanup passes, these two runtime pages were still explicitly passing default prop values such as `contentClassName="min-w-0"` and `titleRowClassName="flex items-center gap-2"`
  - leaving those defaults restated in runtime code weakens the shared primitive as the single source of truth and makes future contract changes harder to propagate

  - repeated pattern:
    - back button leading slot
    - create/edit title with optional detail subtitle
    - compact title row and zero-top-margin description row
  - difference analysis:
    - both pages intentionally customize vertical alignment with `headerRowClassName="flex items-center gap-3"`
    - both pages intentionally keep tighter title typography and collapsed description spacing
    - neither page needs to restate the default shrink-safe content column or default title-row layout
- governance decision
  - keep the business-specific editor framing and typography overrides
  - remove only the props that exactly match `PageHeaderShell` defaults so the primitive remains the real contract owner

    - `Business/Events/FamilyOverview`
  - future event-strategy editor headers should only override PageHeaderShell props when they diverge from the shared default contract
## 2026-07-05 Removed a duplicated PageHeaderShell default from SectionHeader

- candidate components
  - reviewed shared semantic wrapper:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - checked against the lower-level primitive:
    - `src/components/page-header-shell/index.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SectionHeader` is the governed semantic contract for subsection headings and already composes `PageHeaderShell`
  - despite that, it was still forwarding `headerRowClassName="flex items-start justify-between gap-4"`, which is exactly the current `PageHeaderShell` default
  - leaving that duplicate in the wrapper splits the layout default across two component layers and makes future primitive changes harder to propagate
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `SectionHeader` is reused across layout families, dashboard widgets, workspace shells, and business surfaces as the standard subsection heading
    - all of those consumers rely on the wrapper's title, description, leading icon, action slot, and variant semantics rather than on a bespoke header-row layout
  - difference analysis:
    - `SectionHeader` still owns meaningful semantics through `variant`, icon resolution, and its title/description/action class layering
    - it does not need to re-own the primitive's default row distribution when there is no divergence from `PageHeaderShell`
- governance decision
  - keep `SectionHeader` as the semantic wrapper
  - remove only the prop that exactly mirrors the primitive default so `PageHeaderShell` remains the single source of truth for its base row layout
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - deleted the redundant `headerRowClassName` pass-through

## 2026-07-05 Moved fixed-sidebar shrink behavior into TreeWorkspaceShell

- candidate components
  - reviewed framework primitive:
    - `src/components/tree-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
  - checked repeated business and runtime consumers:
    - `src/components/integration-catalog-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/integration-instance-management-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tree-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/receive/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - fixed-mode tree workspaces repeatedly needed `sidebarClassName="shrink-0"` just to keep the left column width stable
  - that requirement showed up in framework stories, business wrappers, and at least one runtime page, which means it was no longer a one-off consumer preference
  - leaving the width-stability rule outside the primitive makes fixed tree layouts fragile and pushes the same defensive class into multiple layers
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - fixed tree workspaces across integration catalog, integration instance management, and the framework story all rely on a non-shrinking sidebar column
    - resizable mode does not use `sidebarClassName`, so this behavior belongs specifically to the fixed branch of the primitive
  - difference analysis:
    - consumers still need the option to append extra fixed-sidebar classes
    - they do not need to keep restating `shrink-0` when no extra behavior is being introduced
- governance decision
  - promote fixed-sidebar width stability into `TreeWorkspaceShell` itself
  - remove repeated `shrink-0` defaults and call-site props from wrappers, stories, and runtime consumers that only mirrored the primitive requirement
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/tree-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/integration-catalog-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/integration-instance-management-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/tree-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/receive/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - fixed mode now prepends `shrink-0` inside `TreeWorkspaceShell`
    - wrapper defaults that only repeated that behavior were removed
    - framework story and runtime consumer props that only restated the primitive rule were deleted
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/TreeWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Integrations/Catalog/IntegrationCatalogWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Integrations/InstanceManagement/IntegrationInstanceManagementShell`
  - future tree-workspace cleanup should keep fixed/resizable branch behavior in the framework primitive and let business stories focus on domain states, not sidebar flex safeguards
## 2026-07-05 Promoted embedded SearchActionBar spacing into a shared API

- candidate components
  - reviewed shared toolbar primitive:
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
  - checked repeated business, runtime, and Storybook consumers:
    - `src/components/mlops-algorithm-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-catalog-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/toolbar-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/metric/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SearchActionBar` defaults to `mb-4`, but many embedded-shell consumers had to override that with `className="mb-0"`
  - the same override pattern appeared across Storybook examples, business wrappers, and runtime pages, which makes it a stable layout mode rather than an incidental one-off
  - leaving the pattern as a raw class override keeps the contract implicit and invites more duplicated utility strings across apps
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - standalone search-led action bars want the default outer rhythm
    - search bars embedded inside workspace shells, toolbars, and catalog headers want the exact same flush variant with no bottom margin
  - difference analysis:
    - consumers still need `className` for true layout customizations like `justify-between`
    - they should not need `className="mb-0"` just to activate the embedded-shell spacing mode
- governance decision
  - keep one shared `SearchActionBar`
  - promote the repeated embedded-shell spacing pattern into an explicit `spacing="flush"` API instead of creating a duplicate wrapper or continuing ad hoc utility overrides
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/mlops-algorithm-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-catalog-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/toolbar-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/metric/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - added `spacing="default" | "flush"` to `SearchActionBar`
    - migrated high-confidence embedded consumers from `className="mb-0"` to `spacing="flush"`
    - preserved `className` for actual layout variation instead of spacing toggles
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/Integrations/Catalog/IntegrationCatalogWorkspaceShell`
    - `Business/Monitor/Workspace/ObjectTreeShell`
  - future toolbar cleanup should convert repeated spacing-only overrides into explicit shared API when the same mode appears across runtime and Storybook surfaces

- candidate components
  - reviewed shared toolbar primitive:
    - `src/components/filter-toolbar/index.tsx`
  - checked repeated Storybook and runtime consumers:
    - `src/stories/event-alert-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/toolbar-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/(pages)/settings/error-logs/page.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `FilterToolbar` defaults to `mb-3`, but multiple embedded-toolbar call sites were overriding that with `className="mb-0"`
  - the same spacing override appeared in both framework/business stories and runtime pages, which indicates a stable embedded-shell mode rather than isolated custom styling
  - continuing to encode that mode through raw utility strings would keep the contract implicit and fragmented across apps
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - standalone filter rows want the default outer rhythm
    - filter rows embedded inside shell toolbars, alert filter panels, and table headers want a flush variant with no bottom margin
  - difference analysis:
    - `className` is still needed for real custom spacing such as `mb-[18px]`
    - spacing-only mode switches should be explicit shared API, not ad hoc margin overrides
- governance decision
  - keep one shared `FilterToolbar`
  - promote the repeated embedded-shell spacing pattern into an explicit `spacing="default" | "flush"` API
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/filter-toolbar/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/event-alert-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/toolbar-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/(pages)/settings/error-logs/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - added `spacing="default" | "flush"` to `FilterToolbar`
    - migrated high-confidence embedded consumers from `className="mb-0"` to `spacing="flush"`
    - preserved `className` for non-default layout customization such as explicit larger margins
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Toolbar/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/EventAlert/EventAlertWorkspaceShell`
  - future filter-toolbar cleanup should continue converting spacing-only overrides into explicit shared API and leave arbitrary margins in `className` only when they are true variants

    - embedded section headers inside cards, panel headers, or analytics title rows want a flush variant with no outer bottom margin
  - difference analysis:

    - `mb-0` by itself is a shared mode switch and should be explicit API

  - keep one shared `SectionHeader`
  - promote the repeated flush spacing pattern into an explicit `spacing="default" | "flush"` API

  - migration shape:

    - preserved `className` for real custom spacing values such as `mb-3`
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:

  - keep one shared `Introduction` component

    - border removal, transparent backgrounds, and custom title-row layout remain page-level concerns
- governance decision
  - keep one shared `PageFormHeaderCard` plus one shared `PageFormWorkspaceShell`
  - promote the repeated header spacing intent into `spacing="default" | "compact" | "flush"` on `PageFormHeaderCard`
  - expose the same semantic mode as `headerSpacing` on `PageFormWorkspaceShell`
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/page-form-header-card/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/page-form-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/job-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/execution/job-record/page.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - added explicit header spacing variants for the shared job header card
    - migrated repeated `mb-4` uses to `spacing="default"`
    - migrated the stacked layout story from `mb-0` to `spacing="flush"`
    - migrated `PageFormWorkspaceShell` to semantic `headerSpacing="compact"` instead of raw `mb-3`
- Storybook plan
  - sources of truth remain:
    - `Business/Job/FamilyOverview`
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - future job-shell governance should keep absorbing only stable scaffold rhythm, while leaving task-specific surface chrome in class hooks
## 2026-07-06 Promoted SectionHeader compact inner-panel spacing into an explicit shared variant

- candidate components
  - reviewed shared heading primitive:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - checked Storybook governance surfaces:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-family.stories.tsx`
  - checked runtime adopter:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/accessGuide/index.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SectionHeader` already encoded the outer shell modes `default` and `flush`, but inner panel headings still repeatedly fell back to raw `className="mb-3"`
  - the same `mb-3` rhythm appears across many Storybook family contracts and also in production monitor integration surfaces, which means it is no longer a one-off utility override
  - the repeated difference changes only the bottom spacing of the heading block, not its typography or action row behavior
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - card-in-card demo surfaces and monitor integration sub-panels both need a denser heading gap than the default section band spacing
    - outer shell headings still use `spacing="flush"` or the default section rhythm depending on the container that already owns spacing
  - difference analysis:
    - the stable divergence is the inner-panel spacing mode `mb-3`
    - title sizing, descriptions, icons, and actions remain governed by existing `variant` and class hooks
- governance decision
  - keep one shared `SectionHeader`
  - promote the repeated inner-panel rhythm into `spacing="default" | "compact" | "flush"`
  - reserve `className` for true layout exceptions instead of repeating the compact spacing utility
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/list/detail/configure/accessGuide/index.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - added `spacing="compact"` to `SectionHeader`
    - migrated representative Storybook inner-panel headings from `className="mb-3"` to `spacing="compact"`
    - migrated monitor runtime access-guide headings from `className="mb-3"` to `spacing="compact"`
- Storybook plan
  - sources of truth remain:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/Monitor/FamilyOverview`
  - future heading governance can continue replacing repeated `mb-3` uses in other families incrementally now that the shared compact contract exists

## 2026-07-06 ManagementTableShell divided toolbar migration across system-manager audit pages

- candidate components
  - reused shared framework components:
    - `src/components/management-table-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/filter-toolbar/index.tsx`
  - checked runtime pages:
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/operationLogs.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/loginLogs.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/(pages)/settings/error-logs/page.tsx`
  - updated Storybook contract:
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - system-manager 的三类日志页都已经切到 `ManagementTableShell`，但工具条带仍然混用旧的默认间距或页面私有 `toolbarContainerClassName`
  - 同类日志/审计列表在 `alarm`、`cmdb`、`opspilot` 已经开始收敛到统一的分隔工具条带模式，继续保留私有间距会让跨应用样式节奏再次分叉
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - operation log、login log、error log 都属于“筛选器 + 时间选择 + 搜索/重置 + 数据表格”的同一框架模式
    - `alarm/(pages)/settings/operationLog/page.tsx` 与 `cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/operationLog/page.tsx` 已经使用 `toolbarContainerVariant="divided"` 搭配 `FilterToolbar spacing="flush"`
  - difference analysis:
    - 差异点主要是筛选字段数量、是否带导出按钮、以及各页面自身的表格列
    - 工具条带的布局节奏并没有业务差异，适合完全复用同一 shared contract
- governance decision
  - 不新增业务组件
  - 直接复用 `ManagementTableShell` 的 `toolbarContainerVariant="divided"` 和 `FilterToolbar spacing="flush"` 作为日志/审计列表的标准框架契约
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/operationLogs.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/loginLogs.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/(pages)/settings/error-logs/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - operation log 与 login log 去掉旧默认工具条节奏，统一使用 `spacing="flush"`
    - error log 去掉页面私有 `toolbarContainerClassName="mb-4 rounded p-4"`，改为 shared variant
    - Storybook 追加审计日志工具条示例，作为跨应用框架契约的验证入口
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/ManagementTableShell`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续审计 `ManagementTableShell + FilterToolbar` 组合在 `monitor`、`system-manager`、`alarm`、`cmdb`、`opspilot` 中仍未对齐 shared spacing contract 的列表页
## 2026-07-08 ManagementTableShell spaced toolbar migration across monitor list pages

- candidate components
  - reused shared framework components:
    - `src/components/management-table-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/filter-toolbar/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
  - checked runtime pages:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/group/page.tsx`
  - updated Storybook contract:
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - 两个 monitor 列表页都已经由 `ManagementTableShell` 承载，但仍把工具条垂直节奏写在页面层：一个通过 `FilterToolbar className="mb-[10px]"`，一个通过 `searchClassName="mb-[10px] ..."`
  - 这类写法会让 shared shell 失去布局治理能力，也会让不同页面继续漂移出不同的 toolbar band 节奏
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `viewList` 与 `integration/group` 都是“上方筛选/搜索条 + 下方列表表格”的同一 framework 模式
    - 同类模式在 `opspilot/(pages)/studio/detail/logInfo/page.tsx` 与 `cmdb/(pages)/assetManage/autoDiscovery/featureLibrary/soid/page.tsx` 已经使用 `toolbarContainerVariant="spaced"` 配合 `spacing="flush"`
  - difference analysis:
    - `viewList` 使用自定义 `FilterToolbar`
    - `integration/group` 使用 `ManagementTableShell` 的 `searchProps + actions`
    - 差异只在工具条内容，不在外层 spacing contract
- governance decision
  - 不新增组件
  - 继续把“工具条外边距”收回到 `ManagementTableShell.toolbarContainerVariant="spaced"`，并让内部 `FilterToolbar` / `SearchActionBar` 维持 `flush` 节奏
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/viewList.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/integration/group/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - `viewList` 去掉页面私有 `mb-[10px]`，改为 shell 提供的 `spaced` band + `FilterToolbar spacing="flush"`
    - `integration/group` 去掉 `searchClassName` 中的私有 `mb-[10px]`，改为 `toolbarContainerVariant="spaced"`
    - Storybook 追加 `searchProps + actions` 的 `spaced` 示例，补齐这类 monitor 列表页的框架契约
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/ManagementTableShell`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续扫描 `ManagementTableShell` 上仍通过 `searchClassName`、`toolbarContainerClassName`、或子级 `mb-*` 强行控制工具条节奏的页面
## 2026-07-08 ManagementTableShell stacked toolbar migration for monitor alarm views

- candidate components
  - reused shared framework components:
    - `src/components/management-table-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/search-action-bar/index.tsx`
  - checked runtime page:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/monitorAlarm.tsx`
  - updated Storybook contract:
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `monitorAlarm` 已经把顶部结构放进 `ManagementTableShell.toolbar`，但仍通过外层 `div.mb-[10px]` 手动控制整块 band 的底部间距
  - 页面内部真正需要保留的只是“分段切换在上、搜索工具条在下”的垂直堆叠关系，这应该和 shell 的 toolbar band 节奏解耦
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - 这是 `ManagementTableShell` 的另一类稳定模式：toolbar band 内部由两层控件构成，上层是切换器，下层是 `SearchActionBar`
    - 先前 Storybook 只覆盖了单层 toolbar 与 `searchProps + actions`，还缺这一类 stacked toolbar 合约
  - difference analysis:
    - 业务差异只体现在 segmented 选项和时间刷新行为
    - 外层 band 的底部间距不应继续由页面私有 `mb-*` 负责
- governance decision
  - 不新增 shared component
  - 用 `ManagementTableShell.toolbarContainerVariant="spaced"` 承担外层 toolbar band 节奏
  - 用内部 `space-y-4` 仅表达 toolbar 内部的堆叠关系
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/view/monitorAlarm.tsx`
    - `src/stories/management-table-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 去掉 `monitorAlarm` 外层 `mb-[10px]` 与 segmented 私有 `mb-[16px]`
    - 改为 `toolbarContainerVariant="spaced"` + 内部 `space-y-4`
    - Storybook 追加 stacked toolbar 示例，确保这类模式也由同一个 framework story 覆盖
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/ManagementTableShell`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续识别仍在 toolbar 内容外层包 `mb-*` 容器的列表页，优先处理已经落在 `ManagementTableShell.toolbar` 上的页面

## 2026-07-08 SectionHeader regular spacing contract for 16px content rhythm

- candidate components
  - extended shared framework component:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - checked runtime pages:
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/home/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/testing/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/settings/quota/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/authSettings.tsx`
    - `src/app/ops-analysis/components/sidebar.tsx`
  - updated Storybook contracts:
    - `src/stories/job-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-family.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `SectionHeader` 之前只有 `flush`(0), `compact`(12px), `default`(24px) 三档，但运行时跨应用持续出现 `className="mb-4"` 的 16px 标题下内容节奏
  - 这些用法已经不是偶发例外，而是 shared heading 缺少中间 spacing contract 的信号
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - job 首页最近任务、OpsPilot 测试结果、配额使用区块、system-manager 安全设置、ops-analysis 侧栏标题都属于“标题后立刻进入内容/操作区”的 16px 节奏
    - Storybook 里 `job-family` 与 `k8s-family` 也已经存在同类 `SectionHeader className="mb-4"` 示例
  - difference analysis:
    - 这些场景既不是 `compact` 的紧凑内嵌标题，也不是 `default` 的大段落间距
    - 差异主要在标题字号、是否带 actions、以及个别额外 `mt-*`，不影响共享 spacing 语义
- governance decision
  - 为 `SectionHeader` 补充 `spacing="regular"`，专门承载这类跨应用稳定存在的 16px 标题下节奏
  - 继续保留 `className` 处理额外装饰或独立 `mt-*`，但不再让 `mb-4` 作为页面私有 spacing 来源
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/job/(pages)/home/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/knowledge/detail/testing/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/(pages)/settings/quota/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/security/authSettings.tsx`
    - `src/app/ops-analysis/components/sidebar.tsx`
    - `src/stories/job-family.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-family.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 新增 `spacing="regular"` -> `mb-4`
    - 将高置信度的 `SectionHeader className="mb-4"` 迁移到 `spacing="regular"`
    - 保留 `mt-6` 等非 spacing 语义的局部类名
- Storybook plan
  - sources of truth remain:
    - `Business/Job/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/K8s/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续扫描运行时与 Storybook 中 `SectionHeader className="mb-4"` 的剩余点位，只迁移明确属于 16px 标题下内容节奏的稳定模式
## 2026-07-08 SectionHeader regular spacing promoted into framework Storybook contract

- candidate components
  - reused shared framework component:
    - `src/components/section-header/index.tsx`
  - updated Storybook surface:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `spacing="regular"` 已经在多处运行时和业务 family 中落地，但如果框架层 Storybook 不显式展示，这个新档位仍然不够可见，后续页面容易继续回退到手写 `mb-4`
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `regular` 不是业务专属样式，而是 shared heading 的通用节奏档位
    - 它与 `flush`、`compact` 同属于框架层 spacing vocabulary，应该在 layout family 里被直接说明
  - difference analysis:
    - 业务 family 负责展示真实业务场景
    - framework family 应负责把 spacing 语义讲清楚，降低误用成本
- governance decision
  - 不新增组件
  - 将 `SectionHeader regular` 提升到 framework Storybook 作为显式 contract，而不是只通过业务 stories 间接暴露
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 在 `Workspace headings` 分组中补充 `flush / regular / compact` 的 heading rhythm 对照示例
    - 明确 `regular` 对应 16px title-to-content handoff
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续让新增 spacing/toolbar contract 优先在 framework family 明示，再向业务 family 和运行时迁移
## 2026-07-08 PageIntroHeader compact spacing migration across strategy-detail flows

- candidate components
  - extended shared framework component:
    - `src/components/page-intro-header/index.tsx`
  - checked runtime pages:
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/page.tsx`
  - updated Storybook surface:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - log/monitor 两条策略详情流都通过页面级 `index.module.scss` 的 `.title` 类来给 `PageIntroHeader` 补 `margin-bottom: 10px`，并顺手试图覆写标题字号
  - 这说明“策略详情页头”的紧凑节奏已经是跨应用复现的模式，不该继续藏在局部 CSS 里
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - 两页都是“返回按钮 + 16px 标题 + 紧凑页头与表单区 handoff”的策略编辑页
    - 差异只在文案和后续表单内容，不在页头 spacing 语义
  - difference analysis:
    - 组件原本只有默认 `mb-4`，不足以表达这类更紧凑的策略页头节奏
    - 页面局部 CSS 同时承担 spacing 和标题字号，职责边界不清晰
- governance decision
  - 为 `PageIntroHeader` 补充 `spacing="compact"`，将 10px handoff 收回 shared contract
  - 同时用现有 `titleClassName` 负责 16px 标题样式，不再依赖局部 `.title` 类
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/components/page-intro-header/index.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/detail/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/log/(pages)/event/strategy/index.module.scss`
    - `src/app/monitor/(pages)/event/strategy/index.module.scss`
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 新增 `spacing="compact"` -> `mb-[10px]`
    - 两个策略详情页改用 `spacing="compact"` + `titleClassName`
    - 删除只为 PageIntroHeader 服务的局部 `.title` 样式
    - Storybook 将紧凑策略页头例子切到 `spacing="compact"`
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续扫描 `PageIntroHeader` 是否还存在局部 className / CSS module 负责 spacing 的页面，再判断是否形成第二个稳定变体
## 2026-07-08 PageIntroHeader spacing vocabulary clarified in framework Storybook

- candidate components
  - reused shared framework component:
    - `src/components/page-intro-header/index.tsx`
  - updated Storybook surface:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `PageIntroHeader compact` 已经在策略详情流中落地，但运行时剩余覆写点位暂时还不足以支撑新的 spacing 档位
  - 这时更重要的是让 framework story 把 `default` 与 `compact` 的边界讲清，避免后续页面继续回退到 `className` 微调
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - provider 这类总览页使用默认 intro spacing
    - strategy detail 这类编辑页使用 compact intro spacing
  - difference analysis:
    - 两类场景都属于同一个 shared primitive，只是 handoff 节奏不同
    - 当前证据只支持 `default` 和 `compact` 两档，不支持继续扩新变体
- governance decision
  - 不新增 API
  - 在 framework Storybook 中显式对照 `PageIntroHeader default/compact`，把当前 spacing vocabulary 固化为唯一来源
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 在 `Intro headers` 分组中补充 default/compact 对照示例
    - 明确默认 intro 与紧凑策略 intro 的不同 handoff 语义
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth remains:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 当后续再发现第二个以上运行时页面重复使用同类 `mb-5` 或其他 intro 覆写时，再决定是否扩充新的 spacing 变体

  - checked runtime page:

- why it was reviewed
  - 在补完 `PageIntroHeader default/compact` 契约之后，运行时只剩 alarm 集成总览页还通过 `className="mb-5"` 覆写页头底部节奏
  - 这个点位目前没有第二个运行时同类场景与之形成稳定模式，因此不应扩出新的 shared spacing 变体

    - alarm 集成总览页与 OpsPilot provider 总览页都属于总览页 intro 场景
    - framework story 已明确 default intro 的 handoff 语义
  - difference analysis:

    - 当前证据不足以证明这是一种应被系统化的独立 intro 节奏

  - migration shape:

## 2026-07-08 Removed orphan alarm integration FilterBar

- candidate components
  - checked page-local component:
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/components/FilterBar.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - 在继续做 toolbar spacing 治理时，这个组件看起来像 alarm 集成总览页遗留的旧筛选条实现
  - 但当前运行时页面已经完全不引用它，保留只会让仓库里继续挂着一份未接入 Storybook、也未接入运行时的垃圾组件
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - 如果 alarm 集成总览页真的需要筛选条，现有 shared `FilterToolbar` / `SearchActionBar` 契约已经足够承接
  - difference analysis:
    - 当前这份 `FilterBar` 既不是共享组件，也不是活跃业务组件
    - 它没有被任何页面引用，也没有进入 Storybook，因此不应继续存在
- governance decision
  - 不将其提升为 shared component
  - 直接删除未使用的 page-local 孤儿组件，保持 web 下组件面干净
- migration
  - updated:
    - removed `src/app/alarm/(pages)/integration/components/FilterBar.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 通过全局引用检索确认无运行时引用后删除
- Storybook plan
  - no story change:
    - 该文件既未接入 Storybook 也未接入运行时，删除即可；不为孤儿组件补 story
## 2026-07-08 Removed orphan components with duplicate runtime responsibility

- candidate components
  - removed page-local / feature-local orphan components:
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/custom-chat-sse/ToolCallGroup.tsx`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/custom-chat-sse/submitUserChoice.ts`
    - `src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/forms/BaseEntityFields.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/settings/PortalConsolePreview.tsx`
    - `src/app/system-manager/components/application/permission/tableCells.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - 这批文件都不在运行时 import 链上，也没有对应 Storybook story，已经失去组件契约身份
  - 更重要的是，它们都不是“缺一个 story 的可复用组件”，而是已经被相邻活跃实现覆盖的旧残留
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `ToolCallGroup` 的工具调用展示责任已经被活跃的 `toolCallRenderer.tsx` 行内渲染路径覆盖
    - `submitUserChoice.ts` 的提交逻辑已经直接内聚在活跃的 `UserChoiceCard.tsx`
    - `BaseEntityFields.tsx` 试图抽一层知识库/技能/工作台公共表单字段，但实际三个活跃表单 `KnowledgeForm.tsx`、`SkillForm.tsx`、`StudioForm.tsx` 已各自承接了不同字段语义
    - `tableCells.tsx` 的权限勾选单元格已被 `moduleContent.tsx` 内联列定义接管
  - difference analysis:
    - `PortalConsolePreview.tsx` 没有任何运行时接入，也没有 Storybook 验证面，不构成受治理组件
    - `BaseEntityFields.tsx` 虽然看起来像“抽象候选”，但当前跨表单真实差异已经大于共性，强行保留只会制造未来的伪复用入口
    - `tableCells.tsx` 与 `moduleContent.tsx` 行为重复且 API 分裂，继续并存只会让权限表格组件边界更混乱
- governance decision
  - 不将上述文件升级为 shared component
  - 直接删除未接入运行时且未进入 Storybook 的孤儿组件，避免“看起来可复用、实际上无人使用”的垃圾入口继续扩散
- migration
  - updated:
    - removed `src/app/opspilot/components/custom-chat-sse/ToolCallGroup.tsx`
    - removed `src/app/opspilot/components/custom-chat-sse/submitUserChoice.ts`
    - removed `src/app/opspilot/components/knowledge/forms/BaseEntityFields.tsx`
    - removed `src/app/system-manager/components/settings/PortalConsolePreview.tsx`
    - removed `src/app/system-manager/components/application/permission/tableCells.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 通过全局引用检索确认无运行时 / Storybook 引用后删除
    - 保留活跃实现作为唯一入口，不为重复残留补抽象层
- Storybook plan
  - no story change:
    - 这些文件没有任何有效 story，也不属于应被补 story 的共享契约；删除后由现有 framework / business story 继续承接真实组件面

  - business shared component:

  - 两个组件都已经从页面代码里抽到 `src/components`，并在多个运行时入口复用

    - `TransferTreePanel` 属于 system-manager 业务域组件，差异在树节点内容，而不在搜索+容器骨架

    - `TransferTreePanel` 补入 `Business/SystemManager/RoleTransfer/FamilyOverview`
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/SystemManager/RoleTransfer/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续扫描 `src/components` 中仍无 family story 覆盖的 shared 组件，优先补 framework primitive 与已经跨页面复用的业务壳组件

  - removed leaf story:

  - next migration target:

    - removed `src/stories/event-timeline-panel.stories.tsx`

    - `Events` family 增加 timeline panel 展示后，删除独立 leaf story

    - `Business/Events/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:

## 2026-07-08 Alarm rule-setting leaf stories absorbed into family workflow coverage

- candidate components
  - already-covered business components:
    - `src/components/alarm-match-rule/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/alarm-effective-time/index.tsx`
  - removed leaf stories:
    - `src/stories/alarm-match-rule.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/alarm-effective-time.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - `alarm-family` 已经承接了规则匹配与生效时间两个设置原语
  - 但 leaf story 还单独保留多组条件、单次时间窗、周时间窗这些局部状态，入口仍然是分裂的
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:

    - `AlarmEffectiveTime` 服务同一批设置流的时间窗约束
  - difference analysis:
    - leaf story 的差异只是在 family 尚未直接展示的状态分支，不是新的契约边界
    - 把这些状态补进 `alarm-family` 后，单独 leaf story 就不再提供额外价值
- governance decision
  - 保留两个组件为 alarm 设置域 shared primitives
  - 删除对应 leaf story，改由 `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview` 统一承接基础态和变体态
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/alarm-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/alarm-match-rule.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/alarm-effective-time.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - `alarm-family` 增加多组匹配规则、单次时间窗、日常时间窗示例
    - 删除只重复这些状态的 leaf story
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续区分“仍有独立交互验证价值的 leaf story”和“仅重复 family 状态分支的 leaf story”，优先删除后者
## 2026-07-08 Removed redundant Integrations K8s and SNMP leaf stories after family convergence

- candidate components
  - already-covered business components:
    - `src/components/k8s-access-asset-fields/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/k8s-collector-install-step/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/k8s-common-issues-drawer/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/snmp-trap-guide-panel/index.tsx`
  - removed leaf stories:
    - `src/stories/k8s-access-asset-fields.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-collector-install-step.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/k8s-common-issues-drawer.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/snmp-trap-guide-panel.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - `k8s-family` 已经承接 K8s 访问资产、安装步骤、常见问题三段核心业务流
  - `integrations-family` 也已经直接承接 SNMP Trap 接入引导面板
  - 保留对应 leaf story 只会重复呈现相同业务语义，而不会带来新的 Storybook 契约信息
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `K8sAccessAssetFields` 已在 `integration-onboarding-family` 与 `k8s-family` 中覆盖 monitor/log/K8s 访问资产绑定语义
    - `K8sCollectorInstallStep` 已在 `k8s-family` 中覆盖 monitor/log/CMDB 的安装与验证状态分支
    - `K8sCommonIssuesDrawer` 已在 `k8s-family` 中作为安装排障段落展示
    - `SnmpTrapGuidePanel` 已在 `integrations-family` 中作为协议型接入指导面板展示
  - difference analysis:
    - 这些 leaf story 的差异大多是 copy/preset 或局部状态分支，而这些都已经进入对应 family
    - 它们不再构成新的业务入口层级，只是重复页面
- governance decision
  - 删除四个重复 leaf story
  - 保留 `Business/Integrations/FamilyOverview`、`Business/Integrations/K8s/FamilyOverview` 与 `Business/Integrations/Onboarding/FamilyOverview` 作为唯一契约入口
- migration
  - updated:
    - removed `src/stories/k8s-access-asset-fields.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/k8s-collector-install-step.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/k8s-common-issues-drawer.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/snmp-trap-guide-panel.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 不再让协议面板和 K8s 叶子状态各占一页
    - 统一回收到业务 family story 中管理
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Integrations/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/Integrations/Onboarding/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续区分仍需独立验证的多步壳组件（如 `IntegrationK8s*Shell`）和已经被 family 完整吸收的协议/步骤型叶子组件
## 2026-07-08 Consolidated Monitor family and widget leaf stories into governed Storybook contracts

- candidate components
  - framework widget candidates:
    - `src/components/monitor-dashboard-widgets/index.tsx` exports `HorizontalBarPanel` and `StackedBarPanel`
  - business candidates:
    - `src/components/monitor-lazy-metric-item/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/monitor-line-chart/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/monitor-object-icon/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/monitor-object-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
  - removed leaf stories:
    - `src/stories/monitor-horizontal-bar-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-stacked-bar-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-lazy-metric-item.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-line-chart.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-object-icon.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-object-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - `monitor-dashboard-widgets` 已经是监控图表/仪表类框架组件族的契约中心，但此前缺少排行面板与堆叠容量面板，导致框架组件仍散落成 leaf story
  - `monitor-family` 已经承接监控业务语义组件，但指标卡片、对象图标、内联曲线与对象树工作区仍各占单页，Storybook 导航过碎
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `HorizontalBarPanel` 与 `StackedBarPanel` 都服务于监控仪表盘/对象详情这类框架布局，属于 monitor widget family 的同类面板
    - `MonitorLazyMetricItem`、`LineChart`、`MonitorObjectIcon`、`MonitorObjectWorkspaceShell` 都围绕 monitor view/object workspace 的业务语义协同工作，不是独立跨域框架 primitive
  - difference analysis:
    - `HorizontalBarPanel` 的差异维度是排行、分层排行、趋势排行，属于同一框架组件变体
    - `StackedBarPanel` 的差异维度是正常容量与超卖容量，属于同一框架组件状态分支
    - `MonitorLazyMetricItem` 的差异维度是 loaded/loading，`MonitorObjectIcon` 的差异维度是 icon/fallback，`LineChart` 和 `MonitorObjectWorkspaceShell` 都只是 monitor-family 里的业务状态片段
- governance decision
  - 将 `HorizontalBarPanel` 与 `StackedBarPanel` 并入 `Business/Monitor/Widgets/FamilyOverview`
  - 将 `MonitorLazyMetricItem`、`LineChart`、`MonitorObjectIcon`、`MonitorObjectWorkspaceShell` 并入 `Business/Monitor/FamilyOverview`
  - 删除六个只重复 family 契约的 Monitor leaf stories
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/monitor-dashboard-widgets.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/monitor-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed six monitor leaf stories listed above
  - migration shape:
    - widget family 新增排行/趋势/容量配比示例，统一承接框架型监控图表组件
    - monitor family 新增对象图标、指标卡加载态、内联曲线、对象树工作区示例，统一承接业务语义组件
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Monitor/Widgets/FamilyOverview`
    - `Business/Monitor/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续审计 Monitor 域剩余 leaf story 是否还承担独立交互验证职责，优先保留真正有契约增量的页面
## 2026-07-08 Consolidated OpsAnalysis unified-filter stories into one business family

- candidate components
  - business workflow candidates:
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-unified-filter/unifiedFilterBar.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-unified-filter/filterBindingPanel.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-unified-filter/unifiedFilterConfigModal.tsx`
  - removed leaf stories:
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-unified-filter-bar.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-filter-binding-panel.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-unified-filter-config-modal.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - 三个 story 分别展示查询栏、参数绑定、配置弹窗，但它们都围绕同一个 unified filter 业务能力服务
  - 继续分散成三页会让 Storybook 导航表现成“零件目录”，而不是“统一过滤能力契约”
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `UnifiedFilterBar` 负责查询执行与外观模式
    - `FilterBindingPanel` 负责数据源参数与过滤器的绑定关系
    - `UnifiedFilterConfigModal` 负责扫描数据源、配置定义、排序与默认值
    - 三者天然构成同一条 OpsAnalysis 过滤配置工作流
  - difference analysis:
    - `UnifiedFilterBar` 的差异维度只是 default / embedded 外观
    - `FilterBindingPanel` 的差异维度是可绑定 / 不可绑定 / 空参数状态
    - `UnifiedFilterConfigModal` 是同一工作流里的配置阶段，而不是独立跨域 primitive
- governance decision
  - 新建 `Business/OpsAnalysis/UnifiedFilter/FamilyOverview`
  - 删除三个原 leaf story，改由 unified-filter family 统一承接查询栏、绑定面板、配置弹窗契约
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-unified-filter-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed the three unified-filter leaf stories listed above
  - migration shape:
    - family story 统一展示 query bar、binding panel、config modal 三段能力
    - 用一个业务入口承接 unified filter 的完整交互语义
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/UnifiedFilter/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续审计 OpsAnalysis 剩余 leaf story，区分“组件级独立契约”与“只是 family 某个状态切片”的页面
## 2026-07-08 Folded OpsAnalysis widget renderer into widgets family

- candidate components
  - business widget orchestration candidate:
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-widget-renderer/index.tsx`
  - related family host:
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-widgets/*`
  - removed leaf story:
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-widget-renderer.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `WidgetRenderer` 的 story 只验证两件事：`text` 类型会落到 `OpsAnalysisTextPanel`，未知类型会回退到错误态
  - 这两条路径本质上都属于 `ops-analysis-widgets family` 已治理的 widget 组件族契约，而不是一个新的业务入口
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `WidgetRenderer` 只是 `chartType -> widget component` 的分发表
    - 它消费的真实展示契约全部来自 `OpsAnalysisBar/Line/Table/TextPanel/...` 与 `OpsAnalysisWidgetErrorState`
  - difference analysis:
    - `text` 分支只是 family 里已有 `OpsAnalysisTextPanel` 的入口壳路径
    - `unknown` 分支只是 family 里已有 `OpsAnalysisWidgetErrorState` 的 fallback 路径
    - 它没有独立于 widgets family 之外的新视觉语义或交互模型
- governance decision
  - 将 `WidgetRenderer` 并入 `Business/OpsAnalysis/Widgets/FamilyOverview`
  - 删除独立 leaf story，避免一个入口壳重复占据 Storybook 导航层级
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-widgets.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/ops-analysis-widget-renderer.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - widgets family 新增 renderer 的 text 路径与 unknown fallback 路径
    - 统一由 widgets family 承接组件族与入口壳的完整契约
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/Widgets/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续检查 OpsAnalysis 剩余叶子页里，哪些只是 family 组件的壳层状态，而不是独立能力中心
## 2026-07-08 Consolidated OpsAnalysis data-source controls into one business family

- candidate components
  - business query-setup candidates:
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-data-source-select/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/ops-analysis-data-source-params-config/index.tsx`
  - removed leaf stories:
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-data-source-select.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-data-source-params-config.stories.tsx`
- why they were reviewed
  - 在真实页面里，数据源选择与参数配置总是成对出现，先选数据源，再根据 `DatasourceItem.params` 渲染查询参数
  - 分成两个 leaf story 会割裂同一条查询配置语义，让 Storybook 看起来像零件清单而不是业务能力中心
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `OpsAnalysisDataSourceSelect` 负责权限态、搜索态和 datasource 切换
    - `OpsAnalysisDataSourceParamsConfig` 负责从 datasource 元数据生成 string / number / boolean / date / timeRange 输入
    - topology single-value node config 与 dashboard view config 都按同一顺序组合这两个组件
  - difference analysis:
    - 差异主要在周边布局、后续图表配置和 `includeFilterTypes` 取值
    - 选择与参数渲染本身依然是稳定的 ops-analysis 查询配置契约
- governance decision
  - 新建 `Business/OpsAnalysis/DataSource/FamilyOverview`
  - 删除 `Select` 与 `ParamsConfig` 两个独立 leaf story，由 family 统一承接独立态与组合态
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/ops-analysis-data-source-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/ops-analysis-data-source-select.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/ops-analysis-data-source-params-config.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - family 同时展示 datasource select、params config 和组合式 query configuration flow
    - 用一个业务入口表达“选源 + 配参”的完整语义
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/OpsAnalysis/DataSource/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续检查 OpsAnalysis 剩余叶子页，优先收敛同一业务链路下仍然分散的 Storybook 入口

- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - `AlarmLevelIcon` 统一承接 icon font、data URI、空值 fallback 三类展示来源
    - `AlarmPageBreadcrumb` 统一承接父级返回、路径解析、本地化菜单与追加 crumb slot
  - difference analysis:
    - 这些差异都属于 alarm-domain primitive 的稳定变体，而不是值得升级成 shared framework component 的跨域模式
    - 它们适合保留实现独立，但 Storybook 契约归并到同一个 alarm family
- governance decision
  - 将 `AlarmLevelIcon` 和 `AlarmPageBreadcrumb` 并入 `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview`
  - 在 family 内增加 navigation/display primitives 段落统一覆盖这些变体
  - 删除两个独立 leaf story，避免 Storybook 垃圾节点继续增长
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/alarm-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/alarm-level-icon.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/alarm-page-breadcrumb.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - family 新增 breadcrumb 的 incident/integration detail 变体
    - family 新增 alarm level icon 的 icon font、inline image、empty fallback 变体
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续区分真正独立的交互壳组件与只属于 alarm family 的小型语义基元
## 2026-07-09 Removed AlarmSettingsWorkspaceShell in favor of the shared framework shell

- candidate component
  - duplicate wrapper candidate:
    - `src/components/alarm-settings-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
  - removed leaf story:
    - `src/stories/alarm-settings-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `AlarmSettingsWorkspaceShell` 只是在 `IntroductionTableWorkspaceShell` 之上透传 props，并重复声明了同一份默认 `panelClassName`
  - 它没有额外的 alarm-domain 行为、状态模型或独立布局契约，属于应被清理的 app-local wrapper
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - alarm settings 页面与 cmdb operation log 页面都在使用同一种“介绍区 + 表格工作区”框架模式
    - 真实可复用契约已经落在 `IntroductionTableWorkspaceShell`
  - difference analysis:
    - alarm settings 页面的差异只体现在 title、message、toolbar/search、columns、modal slot
    - 这些都属于 framework shell 的正常参数化，而不是新的业务壳组件
- governance decision
  - 删除 `AlarmSettingsWorkspaceShell`
  - alarm settings 页面直接消费 `IntroductionTableWorkspaceShell`
  - 删除对应 leaf story，避免同一框架壳在 Storybook 中出现两个名字不同的入口
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertAssign/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/alertEnrichment/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/shieldStrategy/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/operationLog/page.tsx`
    - `src/app/alarm/(pages)/settings/correlationRules/page.tsx`
    - `src/stories/layout-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/components/alarm-settings-workspace-shell/index.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/alarm-settings-workspace-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - 页面运行时入口统一改为 shared framework shell
    - Storybook 契约统一回收到 `layout-family`
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续清理只包一层 shared shell 且没有额外业务语义的 app-local wrapper
## 2026-07-09 Folded AlarmAssignModal into the alarm action workflow family

- candidate component
  - business workflow state candidate:
    - `src/components/alarm-action/assign-modal.tsx`
  - removed leaf story:
    - `src/stories/alarm-assign-modal.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `AlarmAssignModal` 没有独立页面入口，只会在 `AlarmAction` 的 assign / reassign 分支里被拉起
  - 单独保留 `Business/Alarm/AlarmAssignModal` 会把同一 alarm action workflow 拆成动作按钮一页、内部 modal 一页
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - alarm action 统一持有批量/单行的 assign 与 reassign 触发逻辑
    - assign modal 统一承接 assignee 选择、确认提交、取消回收这条内部交互链路
  - difference analysis:
    - 差异主要只有 `actionType` 为 `assign` 或 `reassign`
    - 这属于同一 workflow 内部状态切换，而不是新的跨域共享组件或独立业务入口
- governance decision
  - 将 `AlarmAssignModal` 的 Storybook 契约并入 `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview`
  - 在 alarm action variants 内保留 assign / reassign modal 预览
  - 删除独立 leaf story，避免 workflow 被拆散
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/alarm-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/alarm-assign-modal.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - alarm family 新增 assign modal states 段落
    - 通过同一 workflow 家族承接动作按钮与 modal 状态
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Alarm/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续审查 Alarm 剩余 story，只保留真正独立的业务壳或跨域共享契约

  - shared catalog candidate:

  - next migration target:

  - 当前治理 worktree 里的 family story 已经覆盖 `UserInformation` 主抽屉，但还没有把“修改邮箱/密码需要先过身份验证”的嵌套 workflow 讲清楚

    - `UserInformation` 仍然是统一的账户信息抽屉入口
    - 差异不在新的外部组件，而在内部的 identity verification -> email code/password flow
  - difference analysis:
    - 这属于同一个 user-profile family 的内部交互分支
    - 不应该为内部 modal 再派生 shadow story 或重复组件
- governance decision
  - 不新增独立 leaf story

- migration

    - `src/stories/system-manager-user-profile-family.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - family 在 information surface 上增加 nested verification workflow callout
    - 保持 Storybook 契约与当前运行时语义对齐
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/SystemManager/UserProfile/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续区分旧 worktree 中剩余分叉哪些需要补 Storybook 契约，哪些只是运行时实现演化

    - 品牌、应用切换、通知与用户入口仍然是同一个 app-shell header contract

    - removed `src/stories/top-menu.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:

    - 保留 full navigation / header-only 两个稳定变体
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Framework/Layout/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:

  - difference analysis:

    - 这些差异属于 integration editing workflow 的内部变体，不构成新的 Storybook root

  - 删除独立 leaf story，保持 integrations family 作为 integration 域唯一业务契约入口
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/integrations-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/integration-instance-edit-modal.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - family 在 `Editing workflows and supporting shells` 段补充 `Edit single instance` 与 `Batch assign group`
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Integrations/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续清理 integration 域里仍然只是 family 子工作流却单挂 root 的 edit / config / import modal

    - 这些差异属于同一个导入 workflow 的上下文参数，不构成新的 Storybook root

  - migration shape:

## 2026-07-10 Folded K8s shell stories into the k8s family

- candidate component
  - business workflow candidates:
    - `src/components/integration-k8s-access-config-shell/index.tsx`
    - `src/components/integration-k8s-configuration-shell/index.tsx`
  - removed leaf stories:
    - `src/stories/integration-k8s-access-config-shell.stories.tsx`
    - `src/stories/integration-k8s-configuration-shell.stories.tsx`
- why it was reviewed
  - `k8s-family` 已经承接同一条 K8s onboarding workflow，但这两个 shell 仍然各自占着 `Business/Integrations/K8s/*` 独立 root
  - 真实代码里 `IntegrationK8sAccessConfigShell` 已服务于 log K8s 接入页，`IntegrationK8sConfigurationShell` 已服务于 log 与 cmdb 的多步引导流
- cross-app comparison
  - shared structure:
    - monitor / log / cmdb 都围绕同一条三段式 onboarding：接入配置、采集器安装、接入完成
    - 差异都通过 preset、child content 或 command data 结构注入，而不是换一套壳层 API
  - difference analysis:
    - access shell 的差异主要是 monitor 与 log 的 stepCallout、asset copy、附加表单字段
    - configuration shell 的差异主要是 monitor / log / cmdb 三个分支在 access form 内容、command data 字段和完成态 preset 上不同
    - 这些差异都属于 K8s onboarding workflow 的内部变体，不构成新的 Storybook root
- governance decision
  - 将 `IntegrationK8sAccessConfigShell` 与 `IntegrationK8sConfigurationShell` 一并并入 `src/stories/k8s-family.stories.tsx`
  - 删除两个独立 leaf story，保持 k8s family 作为 K8s onboarding 域唯一业务契约入口
- migration
  - updated:
    - `src/stories/k8s-family.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/integration-k8s-access-config-shell.stories.tsx`
    - removed `src/stories/integration-k8s-configuration-shell.stories.tsx`
  - migration shape:
    - family 的 `Shared K8s flow shells` 段补充 access shell 的 monitor / log 变体
    - family 同段补充 configuration shell 的 monitor / log / cmdb 三种工作流分支
- Storybook plan
  - source of truth:
    - `Business/Integrations/K8s/FamilyOverview`
  - next migration target:
    - 继续检查 K8s / integration 域里是否还有只属于 onboarding workflow 子分支却仍单挂 root 的 install / guidance story
